# Know24 Production Build Plan
# PART 1: SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE & TECH STACK

---

## 1.1 Executive Summary

### What Know24 Is

Know24 is a multi-tenant SaaS platform — "Shopify for Knowledge Businesses" — that enables subject matter experts (nurse educators, fitness coaches, marketing consultants, etc.) to go from raw expertise to a fully operational knowledge business in under one hour. A single onboarding flow called "Setup My Business" ingests a creator's knowledge, uses AI to generate sellable digital products, builds a branded storefront, and activates marketing automation. Creators pay $99/month per business; their customers transact directly on the creator's storefront with Stripe Connect.

### Tech Stack Chosen

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Frontend | Next.js 15 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui | RSC-first, edge-optimized, type-safe, production-proven at scale |
| AI Orchestration | Vercel AI SDK 4 | Unified multi-provider interface (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) with streaming, tool calling, and structured output |
| Auth | Clerk | Purpose-built multi-tenant auth with org primitives that map directly to Know24's per-business model |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector) | Full-stack BaaS: relational DB, vector search, RLS-enforced multi-tenancy, Edge Functions, Realtime |
| Payments | Stripe Billing + Stripe Connect | Industry standard; Connect enables creator-to-customer direct payments while Know24 collects platform fees |
| Job Queue | BullMQ + Upstash Redis | Durable job processing for long-running AI tasks (product generation, Scout scans, blog publishing) |
| File Storage | Cloudflare R2 | Zero egress fees — critical for PDF-heavy knowledge products; S3-compatible API |
| Hosting | Vercel (frontend/API) + Railway (background workers) | Vercel for edge-optimized Next.js; Railway for persistent Node workers BullMQ requires |
| CDN | Cloudflare | DDoS protection + Workers for storefront edge routing |
| Monitoring | Sentry + Vercel Analytics | Error tracking + real user metrics |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions | Industry standard; integrates with all services above |

### Build Approach

The founder will use **Claude Code** (for agentic, context-aware implementation) and **Cursor** (for file navigation and inline edits) as the primary build tools. This plan is written to be directly actionable by those tools: every section contains precise configuration values, real file paths, and explicit decision rationale so the AI coding assistants can execute without ambiguity.

### Target

A production-ready, market-ready platform deployable to real customers. Not an MVP with technical debt — a scalable architecture that handles the full Know24 feature surface without rewrites at 1,000 or 10,000 customers.

```
╔══════════════════════════════╗
║  DATA ISOLATION MODEL        ║
╠══════════════════════════════╣
║  Method: PostgreSQL RLS      ║
║  Every table that contains   ║
║  business data has:          ║
║                              ║
║  CREATE POLICY "business_    ║
║   isolation" ON table_name   ║
║   FOR ALL USING (            ║
║    business_id IN (          ║
║     SELECT id FROM           ║
║     businesses WHERE         ║
║     owner_id = auth.uid()    ║
║    )                         ║
║   );                         ║
║                              ║
║  Vector embeddings in        ║
║  knowledge_chunks carry      ║
║  business_id — RAG queries   ║
║  filter by business before   ║
║  similarity search.          ║
╚══════════════════════════════╝
```

## 1.4 Request Flow Diagrams

### Flow 1: "Setup My Business" — Creator Onboarding

```
ACTOR: Creator (authenticated Know24 user)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 1: KNOWLEDGE INTAKE                                                            │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator → /setup UI                                                                 │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─ Paste URL ──────────→ POST /api/upload/url                                   │
│     │                             │                                                  │
│     │                             └─→ Enqueue job: knowledge-ingest (BullMQ)        │
│     │                                      │                                         │
│     ├─ Upload PDF ─────────→ POST /api/upload/presign → R2 presigned URL            │
│     │   (direct to R2)           Frontend uploads directly to R2                    │
│     │                             POST /api/upload/complete → Enqueue ingest job    │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─ AI Interview ───────→ POST /api/ai/chat (streamText, Anthropic Claude)       │
│                                    Streaming conversation, captures answers          │
│                                    POST /api/upload/interview → save transcript      │
│                                                                                      │
│  [RAILWAY WORKER: knowledge-ingest job]                                             │
│     - Fetch URL content / read PDF / parse transcript                               │
│     - Chunk text into ~500 token segments                                           │
│     - POST to Supabase Edge Function /functions/embed                               │
│         → OpenAI text-embedding-3-small → vector(1536)                             │
│         → INSERT INTO knowledge_chunks (content, embedding, business_id)           │
│     - UPDATE knowledge_sources SET status='processed'                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 2: AI ANALYSIS                                                                 │
│                                                                                      │
│  Frontend polls GET /api/setup/analysis?business_id=xxx                             │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ Next.js API Route:                                                          │
│              SELECT * FROM knowledge_chunks WHERE business_id = $1                  │
│              LIMIT 50 (top chunks by recency/coverage)                              │
│                                                                                      │
│              generateObject({                                                        │
│                model: openai('gpt-4o'),                                             │
│                schema: KnowledgeAnalysisSchema (Zod),                               │
│                prompt: `Analyze this expert's knowledge and recommend products...`   │
│              })                                                                      │
│                                                                                      │
│              Returns: { summary, topics[], recommendedProducts[] }                  │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator sees analysis results → clicks "These look great" or selects own products  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 3 & 4: PRODUCT SELECTION + AI BUILDING                                         │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator selects products → POST /api/products/generate                             │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ For each selected product type:                                             │
│              Enqueue job: product-generation (BullMQ)                               │
│                                                                                      │
│  [RAILWAY WORKER: product-generation job]                                           │
│     For each product:                                                                │
│     1. Retrieve relevant knowledge_chunks via pgvector similarity search            │
│     2. generateObject() → full product content (structured by product type)        │
│     3. Generate PDF (via pdf-lib or Puppeteer for Guide/eBook types)               │
│     4. Upload PDF to R2: know24-product-assets/{business_id}/{product_id}.pdf      │
│     5. Generate cover image: DALL-E 3 via generateImage()                          │
│     6. Upload cover to R2: know24-generated-images/{business_id}/{product_id}.png  │
│     7. INSERT INTO products (title, description, type, price, asset_url, cover_url) │
│     8. Broadcast realtime event → frontend progress bar advances                   │
│                                                                                      │
│  Simultaneously:                                                                     │
│     - Generate storefront config (name, tagline, bio, FAQ, color palette)          │
│     - Seed 3 blog posts (blog-publish queue)                                        │
│     - Configure email sequences (INSERT INTO email_sequences)                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STEP 5: GO LIVE                                                                     │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator clicks "Go Live" → POST /api/setup/golive                                  │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─→ UPDATE businesses SET status='active', published_at=NOW()                   │
│     ├─→ Stripe Connect: Create Express Account for creator (if not exists)          │
│     │      stripe.accounts.create({ type: 'express', country: 'US' })              │
│     ├─→ DNS: Cloudflare Worker maps {slug}.know24.io → business_id                 │
│     └─→ Return: { storefront_url, qr_code_data_url }                               │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator redirected to /dashboard — business row appears                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Flow 2: Customer Purchasing a Product

```
ACTOR: End Customer (buyer visiting creator's storefront)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. STOREFRONT VISIT                                                                 │
│                                                                                      │
│  Customer → nurse-leader.know24.io                                                  │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ Cloudflare Worker intercepts:                                               │
│              KV lookup: "nurse-leader" → business_id = "bus_001"                   │
│              Forward to Vercel: GET /app/[slug]/page.tsx                            │
│                                                                                      │
│  Next.js RSC (server component):                                                     │
│     SELECT storefront_config, products WHERE business_id = 'bus_001'               │
│     Render storefront with creator's branding (NOT Know24 UI)                       │
│     Static generation via ISR (revalidateTag on product updates)                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. PRODUCT PAGE + CHECKOUT                                                          │
│                                                                                      │
│  Customer clicks "Buy Now — $47"                                                    │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ POST /api/checkout/create                                                   │
│              Payload: { product_id, business_id }                                   │
│                                                                                      │
│  API Route:                                                                          │
│     1. Fetch product: price, name, creator's Stripe Connect account_id             │
│     2. stripe.paymentIntents.create({                                               │
│           amount: 4700,  // $47.00 in cents                                         │
│           currency: 'usd',                                                          │
│           transfer_data: { destination: 'acct_AA' },  // creator's account         │
│           application_fee_amount: 235,  // 5% platform fee = $2.35                │
│           metadata: { product_id, business_id, customer_email }                    │
│        })                                                                            │
│     3. Return: { client_secret, publishable_key }                                   │
│                                                                                      │
│  Frontend renders Stripe Elements with client_secret                                │
│  Customer enters card details → stripe.confirmPayment()                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. PAYMENT CONFIRMATION + ACCESS DELIVERY                                           │
│                                                                                      │
│  Stripe → POST /api/stripe/webhook (payment_intent.succeeded)                       │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ API Route verifies Stripe signature                                         │
│              1. INSERT INTO customers (email, stripe_customer_id, business_id)     │
│              2. INSERT INTO orders (customer_id, product_id, status='completed')   │
│              3. Determine product type:                                              │
│                                                                                      │
│                  PDF Product:                                                        │
│                    Generate signed R2 URL (24hr expiry)                             │
│                    Send transactional email with download link                      │
│                                                                                      │
│                  Chatbot Subscription:                                               │
│                    Stripe subscription created in webhook                           │
│                    INSERT INTO chatbot_access (customer_id, business_id, active)   │
│                    Emit access token in email                                        │
│                                                                                      │
│              4. Trigger: email-send job → BullMQ                                   │
│                    Welcome email + product delivery                                  │
│                    Enroll in nurture email sequence                                  │
│                                                                                      │
│  Customer receives email with download link or chatbot access URL                   │
│  Redirected to /access/[order_id] page with immediate product access               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Flow 3: Scout Scan Execution

```
ACTOR: Automated system + creator (Scout add-on active)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. CRON TRIGGER                                                                     │
│                                                                                      │
│  Supabase Edge Function: /functions/scout-trigger                                   │
│  Schedule: Every 6 hours (pg_cron or Supabase scheduled functions)                  │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ SELECT business_id FROM businesses                                          │
│              WHERE scout_active = true                                               │
│              AND scout_scans_used_this_month < 20                                   │
│              AND (last_scan_at IS NULL OR                                           │
│                   last_scan_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '6 hours')                       │
│                                                                                      │
│         For each eligible business:                                                  │
│              POST /api/jobs/scout (enqueue to BullMQ scout-scan queue)              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. WORKER EXECUTION                                                                 │
│                                                                                      │
│  [RAILWAY WORKER: scout-scan job]                                                   │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─→ Fetch business context:                                                     │
│     │      knowledge_chunks summary, product titles, niche keywords                 │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─→ WEB SCANNING (parallel, per platform):                                     │
│     │      Reddit: Reddit API → search r/nursing, r/leadership, etc.               │
│     │      X/Twitter: Twitter API v2 → search trending topics + mentions           │
│     │      Quora: Web scraping (Playwright headless) → question search             │
│     │      LinkedIn: LinkedIn API or scraping → post search                        │
│     │      Podcasts: Listen Notes API → guest opportunity search                   │
│     │      News: Google News RSS → trending topic detection                        │
│     │                                                                                │
│     │      Collect raw results: { platform, url, title, content, engagement }      │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─→ AI ANALYSIS (per result batch):                                             │
│     │      generateObject({                                                          │
│     │        model: openai('gpt-4o'),                                               │
│     │        schema: OpportunitySchema (Zod),                                       │
│     │        prompt: `You are a marketing strategist for {creator_niche}.           │
│     │                 Analyze this post/thread. Score its relevance (0-100)         │
│     │                 to {product_titles}. Generate: opportunity summary,           │
│     │                 why it matters, 2-3 suggested moves, draft response.`        │
│     │      })                                                                        │
│     │                                                                                │
│     │      Filter: only keep opportunities with relevance_score >= 60              │
│     │                                                                                │
│     ├─→ DATABASE WRITE:                                                             │
│     │      INSERT INTO scout_scans (business_id, scan_at, opportunities_found)     │
│     │      INSERT INTO scout_opportunities (scan_id, platform, type,               │
│     │        title, url, relevance_score, context, suggested_moves, draft)         │
│     │      UPDATE businesses SET scout_scans_used_this_month = +1                  │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ NOTIFICATION:                                                               │
│              Supabase Realtime broadcast → dashboard updates badge count            │
│              Optional: POST to email-send queue → "Scout found N opportunities"    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. CREATOR REVIEW                                                                   │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator opens Dashboard → Marketing → Scout tab                                    │
│     │                                                                                │
│     └─→ GET /api/scout/opportunities?business_id=xxx                               │
│              Returns paginated opportunity cards, sorted by relevance_score         │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator clicks [Approve] → POST /api/scout/approve                                 │
│     - Mark opportunity as approved                                                   │
│     - If platform supports it: enqueue social-post-generate with draft content     │
│                                                                                      │
│  Creator clicks [Edit] → Slide-over with draft editable text                       │
│  Creator clicks [Dismiss] → UPDATE scout_opportunities SET dismissed=true          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 1.5 Tech Stack Specification

### Next.js 15 (App Router)

- **Version**: `next@15.2.x` (latest stable as of March 2026)
- **Why over alternatives**: React Server Components are native — no separate server needed for server-rendered product pages. App Router enables per-route streaming, layouts, and nested loading states ideal for the multi-panel dashboard UI. Vercel's deployment model is built around Next.js (zero-config edge deployments, ISR for storefronts). Alternatives considered: Remix (worse Vercel integration, less RSC maturity), SvelteKit (smaller ecosystem for Stripe/Clerk/AI SDK integrations).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```js
  // next.config.ts
  const nextConfig = {
    experimental: {
      reactCompiler: true,    // enable React Compiler for automatic memoization
    },
    images: {
      remotePatterns: [
        { hostname: '*.r2.cloudflarestorage.com' },
        { hostname: 'img.clerk.com' },
      ],
    },
    // Suppress Supabase server component warnings
    serverExternalPackages: ['@supabase/supabase-js'],
  }
  ```
- **Cost at scale**: Hosting cost is Vercel (see Vercel entry below). Next.js itself is free/open source.

**Docs**: https://nextjs.org/docs

---

### Tailwind CSS 4.1

- **Version**: `tailwindcss@4.1.x` + `@tailwindcss/vite` or `@tailwindcss/postcss`
- **Why over alternatives**: v4 is CSS-first configuration (no `tailwind.config.js`) — all tokens defined in `@theme` block in CSS. 5x faster full builds, 100x faster incremental. Native CSS cascade layers, container queries, and 3D transforms. Alternatives: plain CSS modules (no utility-first speed), styled-components (runtime overhead), Vanilla Extract (too much boilerplate for this team).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```css
  /* src/app/globals.css */
  @import "tailwindcss";
  
  @theme {
    --color-navy-900: #0f172a;    /* sidebar background */
    --color-teal-600: #0891b2;    /* primary accent */
    --color-surface: #ffffff;
    --font-sans: "Inter", system-ui, sans-serif;
  }
  ```
- **Cost**: Free/open source.

**Docs**: https://tailwindcss.com/docs

---

### shadcn/ui

- **Version**: Latest CLI (`npx shadcn@latest`) — components are copied into `/src/components/ui/`, not imported from npm. No semver pinning needed; components are owned by the project.
- **Why over alternatives**: Not a component library — it's a collection of accessible, unstyled-by-default Radix UI primitives with Tailwind styling that you own and modify. Critical for Know24's slide-over panels (Sheet component), product cards (Card), tabs (Tabs), progress bars (Progress), and command palette (Command). Alternatives: Radix UI raw (more work), Mantine (too opinionated), Headless UI (fewer components).
- **Components to install at setup**:
  ```bash
  npx shadcn@latest add button card input label select sheet tabs 
    dialog progress badge avatar dropdown-menu command toast 
    popover calendar checkbox radio-group switch slider textarea 
    table form separator scroll-area skeleton alert
  ```
- **Cost**: Free/open source.

**Docs**: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs

---

### Vercel AI SDK 4

- **Version**: `ai@4.x` (stable, v6 in beta — do not use beta). Current stable: `ai@4.3.x`
- **Why over alternatives**: Unified API for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google — swap providers with one line. `streamText()` returns a ReadableStream compatible with Next.js RSC streaming. `generateObject()` with Zod schemas guarantees structured output for product generation (no JSON parsing hacks). `useChat()` hook handles AI Workspace chat state. Alternatives: LangChain (too heavy, too opinionated), raw fetch to OpenAI API (no multi-provider, no streaming helpers), AI SDK v3 (deprecated).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // src/lib/ai/providers.ts
  import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai'
  import { anthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic'
  import { google } from '@ai-sdk/google'
  
  export const primaryModel = openai('gpt-4o')
  export const reasoningModel = anthropic('claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022')
  export const longContextModel = google('gemini-1.5-pro-latest')
  export const embeddingModel = openai.embedding('text-embedding-3-small')
  ```
- **Provider packages**: `@ai-sdk/openai@^1.x`, `@ai-sdk/anthropic@^1.x`, `@ai-sdk/google@^1.x`
- **Cost**: The SDK is free. LLM costs are pass-through:
  - OpenAI GPT-4o: ~$2.50/1M input tokens, $10/1M output tokens
  - Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet: ~$3/1M input, $15/1M output
  - text-embedding-3-small: $0.02/1M tokens
  - Estimated AI cost at 100 businesses: ~$200–400/month; at 1,000 businesses: ~$2,000–4,000/month (absorbed in $99/mo subscription)

**Docs**: https://ai-sdk.dev/docs

---

### Supabase (PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector)

- **Version**: Supabase Pro plan. PostgreSQL 16 (managed). pgvector extension `0.7.x`.
- **Why over alternatives**: Full BaaS — one service provides: PostgreSQL + connection pooling (PgBouncer), Row Level Security (multi-tenancy enforcement), vector similarity search (knowledge RAG), Edge Functions (Deno, low cold-start), Realtime (WebSockets for live job progress), Storage (supplementary — we use R2 for main assets), Auto-generated REST + GraphQL APIs. Supabase Auth is bundled but we use Clerk for auth UX; we use Supabase's RLS with Clerk JWTs. Alternatives: PlanetScale (no pgvector), Neon (no Edge Functions, no Realtime), raw AWS RDS + Pinecone (double the services to manage, no BaaS conveniences).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```sql
  -- Enable pgvector
  CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
  
  -- Knowledge chunks table (core of RAG)
  CREATE TABLE knowledge_chunks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    source_id UUID REFERENCES knowledge_sources(id),
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    embedding vector(1536),  -- text-embedding-3-small dimensions
    token_count INT,
    chunk_index INT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
  );
  
  -- HNSW index for fast approximate nearest neighbor search
  CREATE INDEX ON knowledge_chunks 
    USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
    WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);
  
  -- RLS policy
  ALTER TABLE knowledge_chunks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
  CREATE POLICY "business_isolation" ON knowledge_chunks
    FOR ALL USING (
      business_id IN (
        SELECT id FROM businesses WHERE owner_id = auth.uid()
      )
    );
  ```
- **Clerk + Supabase JWT integration**: Add Clerk JWT template for Supabase in Clerk dashboard → Supabase recognizes Clerk-issued JWTs for RLS.
- **Cost**:
  - 0 customers: Free tier ($0, 2 projects, 500MB DB, 50K MAUs)
  - 100 businesses: Pro plan ~$25–50/month
  - 1,000 businesses: Pro plan ~$75–150/month (DB + MAU overages)
  - 10,000 businesses: Team plan $599/month + usage overages (~$1,000–2,000/month total)

**Docs**: https://supabase.com/docs

---

### PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector 0.7

- **Version**: PostgreSQL 16 (managed by Supabase). pgvector `0.7.x`.
- **Why pgvector over dedicated vector DB**: Keeps vector search co-located with relational data — one transaction can JOIN products, filter by business_id, AND do similarity search. No separate Pinecone/Weaviate service, no cross-service latency, no second authentication system. For Know24's scale (thousands of businesses, millions of chunks), pgvector with HNSW indexes is performant enough.
- **pgvector HNSW config for production**:
  ```sql
  -- Set for large datasets during HNSW build
  SET max_parallel_maintenance_workers = 7;
  SET maintenance_work_mem = '8GB';
  
  -- Query-time tuning (in connection or session)
  SET hnsw.ef_search = 100;  -- higher = more accurate, slower
  ```

**Docs**: https://github.com/pgvector/pgvector

---

### Clerk

- **Version**: `@clerk/nextjs@^6.x`
- **Why over alternatives**: Clerk has first-class Next.js App Router support with `<ClerkProvider>`, `auth()` server-side helper, and `currentUser()`. Organizations feature maps perfectly to Know24 businesses (1 user → N organizations). `useOrganizationList()` hook drives the multi-business dashboard. Pre-built `<UserButton>`, `<SignIn>`, `<SignUp>` components eliminate auth UI work. Supabase Auth: doesn't have the same team/org primitives and requires building own auth UI. Auth0: 21x more expensive per MAU at scale.
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // middleware.ts (root)
  import { clerkMiddleware, createRouteMatcher } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'
  
  const isPublicRoute = createRouteMatcher([
    '/',
    '/pricing',
    '/blog(.*)',
    '/help(.*)',
    '/api/storefront/(.*)',    // public storefront data
    '/api/stripe/webhook',     // Stripe webhooks (bypass Clerk)
    '/s/(.*)',                 // creator storefronts
  ])
  
  export default clerkMiddleware(async (auth, req) => {
    if (!isPublicRoute(req)) await auth.protect()
  })
  
  export const config = {
    matcher: ['/((?!_next|[^?]*\\.(?:html?|css|js(?!on)|jpe?g|webp|png|gif|svg|ttf|woff2?|ico|csv|docx?|xlsx?|zip|webmanifest)).*)']
  }
  ```
- **Cost**:
  - 0–10,000 Know24 users: Free (10,000 MRU included)
  - 100 businesses (~100 users): Free
  - 1,000 businesses (~1,000 users): Free
  - 10,000 businesses (~10,000 users): ~$0–25/month (at free tier boundary)
  - 50,000+ users: Pro plan $25/month base + $0.02/MAU overage

**Docs**: https://clerk.com/docs/nextjs/get-started

---

### Stripe Billing + Stripe Connect

- **Version**: `stripe@^16.x` (Node.js SDK), `@stripe/stripe-js@^4.x` (browser)
- **Why**: Industry standard payment infrastructure. Stripe Billing handles recurring subscriptions with usage metering (AI overage charges). Stripe Connect Express enables creators to receive payments directly — Know24 never holds customer funds, which dramatically reduces financial compliance burden. 1099 tax form generation is automated via Connect.
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // src/lib/stripe/server.ts
  import Stripe from 'stripe'
  export const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!, {
    apiVersion: '2025-01-27.acacia',
    typescript: true,
  })
  
  // Webhook handler signature verification
  // /api/stripe/webhook/route.ts
  const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(
    body,
    req.headers.get('stripe-signature')!,
    process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!
  )
  ```
- **Products to create in Stripe**:
  - Price ID: `price_know24_base_monthly` → $99/month (recurring)
  - Price ID: `price_scout_addon_monthly` → $199/month (recurring)
  - Metered price for AI overage (per 1K tokens above ceiling)
- **Connect fees**: 0.25% + 25¢ per payout to creator's bank account (Stripe charges this). Platform fee charged to buyer: configurable (e.g., 5% `application_fee_amount`).
- **Cost**: 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction (absorbed by buyer or creator, not Know24). Know24's subscription revenue goes through regular Stripe Billing (no Connect surcharge).

**Docs**: https://stripe.com/docs, https://stripe.com/docs/connect

---

### Upstash Redis (BullMQ backend)

- **Version**: `@upstash/redis@^1.x` (for direct access), `ioredis@^5.x` (BullMQ's required Redis client — connect to Upstash via ioredis)
- **Why over alternatives**: Serverless Redis — no provisioned instance to manage. Upstash's Fixed plan at $10/month is explicitly recommended for BullMQ use cases (pay-as-you-go incurs excessive costs from BullMQ's polling pattern). Alternatives: Upstash pay-as-you-go (too expensive for BullMQ), Redis Cloud (managed but more expensive), self-hosted Redis on Railway (operationally simpler to keep Redis managed).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // src/lib/queue/connection.ts
  import IORedis from 'ioredis'
  
  export const redisConnection = new IORedis(process.env.UPSTASH_REDIS_URL!, {
    maxRetriesPerRequest: null,  // Required by BullMQ
    enableReadyCheck: false,     // Required for TLS connections
    tls: {},                     // Upstash requires TLS
  })
  ```
- **Cost**:
  - Fixed plan: $10/month (250MB data, 50GB bandwidth) — sufficient for job queue metadata
  - At 10,000 businesses: ~$10–30/month (multiple Fixed databases or larger plan)

**Docs**: https://upstash.com/docs/redis/integrations/bullmq

---

### BullMQ

- **Version**: `bullmq@^5.x` (latest stable: 5.58.x)
- **Why over alternatives**: Most battle-tested Redis-backed job queue for Node.js. Supports: delayed jobs, retries with exponential backoff, concurrency limiting, job prioritization, and rate limiting — all needed for Scout scans and AI generation. Alternatives: Inngest (managed, good DX but vendor lock-in, higher cost at scale), Trigger.dev (similar concern), plain Supabase pg_cron (not suitable for long-running jobs).
- **Queue definitions**:
  ```ts
  // src/workers/queues.ts
  import { Queue } from 'bullmq'
  import { redisConnection } from '@/lib/queue/connection'
  
  export const productGenerationQueue = new Queue('product-generation', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const knowledgeIngestQueue   = new Queue('knowledge-ingest', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const blogPublishQueue       = new Queue('blog-publish', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const emailSendQueue         = new Queue('email-send', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const scoutScanQueue         = new Queue('scout-scan', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const socialPostQueue        = new Queue('social-post-generate', { connection: redisConnection })
  export const embeddingIngestQueue   = new Queue('embedding-ingest', { connection: redisConnection })
  ```
- **Cost**: Free/open source (cost is Upstash Redis + Railway compute).

**Docs**: https://docs.bullmq.io

---

### Cloudflare R2

- **Version**: `@aws-sdk/client-s3@^3.x` (R2 is S3-compatible) or `@cloudflare/workers-types` for Workers
- **Why over alternatives**: **Zero egress fees** — this is the decisive factor. Know24 delivers PDF products to buyers repeatedly; S3 charges $0.09/GB egress, which would compound at scale. R2 charges $0.015/GB storage with $0 egress. For an average 5MB PDF purchased 100 times, S3 would charge ~$0.045/product download; R2 charges $0. Alternatives: Supabase Storage (uses S3 under the hood, egress fees apply), AWS S3 (egress fees), Vercel Blob (higher price per GB).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // src/lib/storage/r2.ts
  import { S3Client, PutObjectCommand, GetObjectCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-s3'
  import { getSignedUrl } from '@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner'
  
  export const r2 = new S3Client({
    region: 'auto',
    endpoint: `https://${process.env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`,
    credentials: {
      accessKeyId: process.env.R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID!,
      secretAccessKey: process.env.R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY!,
    },
  })
  
  // Generate time-limited signed URL for PDF delivery (24hr)
  export async function getProductDownloadUrl(key: string): Promise<string> {
    return getSignedUrl(r2, new GetObjectCommand({
      Bucket: process.env.R2_PRODUCT_ASSETS_BUCKET!,
      Key: key,
    }), { expiresIn: 86400 })
  }
  ```
- **Buckets**:
  - `know24-knowledge-docs` — uploaded PDFs, URLs cached (private)
  - `know24-product-assets` — generated product PDFs (private, served via signed URL)
  - `know24-generated-images` — AI-generated covers, social images (public via R2.dev URL)
  - `know24-public-pdfs` — lead magnets (public, CDN-cached)
- **Cost**:
  - 0 customers: Free tier (10GB storage, 1M Class A ops/month, 10M Class B ops/month)
  - 100 businesses: ~$5–15/month (estimated 100GB storage, moderate reads)
  - 1,000 businesses: ~$50–150/month
  - 10,000 businesses: ~$500–1,500/month

**Docs**: https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2

---

### Vercel

- **Version/Plan**: Pro plan ($20/month + usage)
- **Why**: Native Next.js hosting — zero-config deployments, automatic preview environments per PR, edge network (300+ PoPs), ISR for storefront pages, built-in analytics, and spend management controls. Railway handles workers (Vercel doesn't support long-running processes). Alternatives: AWS Amplify (worse DX), Netlify (less Next.js optimization), self-hosting on Railway (lose ISR + edge network).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```json
  // vercel.json
  {
    "functions": {
      "src/app/api/ai/**": {
        "maxDuration": 60
      },
      "src/app/api/stripe/webhook": {
        "maxDuration": 30
      }
    },
    "rewrites": [
      {
        "source": "/s/:slug/:path*",
        "destination": "/storefront/:slug/:path*"
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Cost**:
  - 0 customers: Free (Hobby plan sufficient for dev)
  - 100 customers: Pro $20/month + ~$10–30 usage = ~$30–50/month
  - 1,000 customers: Pro + usage ~$100–200/month
  - 10,000 customers: Pro + heavy usage ~$500–1,000/month (or Enterprise negotiation)

**Docs**: https://vercel.com/docs

---

### Railway

- **Version/Plan**: Pro plan ($20/month base + usage)
- **Why**: Persistent Node.js processes — BullMQ workers must run continuously (they poll Redis). Vercel and Cloudflare Workers don't support long-running persistent processes. Railway deploys Docker containers with always-on compute, autoscaling, and GitHub Actions integration. Alternatives: Render (similar, slightly less DX), Heroku (more expensive), AWS ECS (far more operational overhead for this team).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```dockerfile
  # Dockerfile (in /workers directory)
  FROM node:20-alpine
  WORKDIR /app
  COPY package*.json ./
  RUN npm ci --production
  COPY . .
  CMD ["node", "dist/workers/index.js"]
  ```
  ```yaml
  # railway.toml
  [build]
  builder = "dockerfile"
  dockerfilePath = "Dockerfile.worker"
  
  [deploy]
  restartPolicyType = "ON_FAILURE"
  restartPolicyMaxRetries = 5
  ```
- **Cost**:
  - 0 customers: Free trial ($5 credits)
  - 100 customers: Pro ~$20–40/month (1-2 worker instances)
  - 1,000 customers: Pro ~$80–150/month (auto-scaled workers)
  - 10,000 customers: Pro ~$300–600/month (multiple worker processes)

**Docs**: https://docs.railway.com

---

### Sentry

- **Version**: `@sentry/nextjs@^8.x`
- **Plan**: Team ($26/month)
- **Why**: Source map upload in CI, Next.js App Router support (captures both RSC and client errors), worker process error tracking (via `@sentry/node`), performance tracing for AI calls. Team plan includes unlimited users and third-party integrations (GitHub, Slack). Alternatives: Datadog (overkill and expensive), Highlight.io (good alternative, lower cost, less mature).
- **Configuration requirements**:
  ```ts
  // sentry.client.config.ts
  import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'
  Sentry.init({
    dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
    tracesSampleRate: 0.1,  // 10% of transactions in production
    debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development',
    integrations: [Sentry.replayIntegration()],
    replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.01,
    replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0,
  })
  ```
- **Cost**: Team plan ~$26/month flat.

**Docs**: https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/guides/nextjs

---

### GitHub Actions

- **Version**: N/A (cloud service, always current)
- **Why**: Free CI/CD for public repos; $0.008/minute for private repos (GitHub Free includes 2,000 minutes/month; Pro includes 3,000 minutes/month). Integrates natively with Vercel (via Vercel GitHub App) and Railway (via railway CLI action). Runs: type checking, linting, tests, Sentry source map upload, Railway worker deployment.
- **Workflows to create**:
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — on PR: TypeScript typecheck, ESLint, Vitest unit tests
  - `.github/workflows/deploy-production.yml` — on push to main: deploy to Vercel + Railway, upload Sentry source maps
  - `.github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml` — on push to staging branch: deploy preview
  - `.github/workflows/db-migrate.yml` — manual: run Supabase migrations
- **Cost**: Free for public repo; ~$0–20/month for private at typical CI usage.

**Docs**: https://docs.github.com/en/actions

---

### Cost Summary Table

| Service | 0 customers | 100 customers | 1,000 customers | 10,000 customers |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Vercel | $0 | ~$50/mo | ~$150/mo | ~$750/mo |
| Supabase | $0 | ~$50/mo | ~$150/mo | ~$2,000/mo |
| Clerk | $0 | $0 | $0–25/mo | ~$25/mo |
| Railway | $0 | ~$40/mo | ~$120/mo | ~$500/mo |
| Upstash Redis | $0 | $10/mo | $20/mo | $50/mo |
| Cloudflare R2 | $0 | ~$15/mo | ~$100/mo | ~$1,000/mo |
| Sentry | $0 | $26/mo | $26/mo | $26/mo |
| OpenAI/Anthropic | $0 | ~$200/mo | ~$2,000/mo | ~$20,000/mo |
| Stripe | $0 | % of GMV | % of GMV | % of GMV |
| **TOTAL (infra)** | **$0** | **~$391/mo** | **~$2,566/mo** | **~$24,351/mo** |
| **MRR at those customers** | — | $9,900/mo | $99,000/mo | $990,000/mo |
| **Infra as % of MRR** | — | **~4%** | **~2.6%** | **~2.5%** |

*Note: AI costs are the dominant scaling cost driver. Usage ceilings in the product tier keep these predictable. Stripe fees (2.9% + 30¢) are pass-through; platform fee on creator transactions is additional revenue.*

## 1.6 Environment Configuration

### File Structure

```
know24/
├── .env.local          ← development secrets (NEVER commit)
├── .env.staging        ← staging values (commit non-secrets, use Vercel env for secrets)
├── .env.example        ← template with all keys, no values (COMMIT THIS)
└── .env.test           ← test environment (mock values)
```

### Complete `.env.example`

```bash
# ============================================================
# KNOW24 — ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in values
# ============================================================

# ── APP ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000
NEXT_PUBLIC_STOREFRONT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000/s
NODE_ENV=development

# ── CLERK (Authentication) ──────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://dashboard.clerk.com → API Keys
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
CLERK_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...

# Clerk webhook secret (from Clerk Dashboard → Webhooks)
CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...

# Clerk redirect URLs
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/sign-up
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/dashboard
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_UP_URL=/setup

# ── SUPABASE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://supabase.com/dashboard → Project Settings → API
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxx.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...  # safe for browser (RLS enforced)
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJ...      # NEVER expose to browser — server-only

# Supabase database connection (for migrations and direct queries)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:[PASSWORD]@db.xxxx.supabase.co:5432/postgres
DIRECT_URL=postgresql://postgres:[PASSWORD]@db.xxxx.supabase.co:5432/postgres

# ── STRIPE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://dashboard.stripe.com → Developers → API Keys
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...

# Webhook secret (from Stripe Dashboard → Webhooks)
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...

# Stripe Price IDs (create in Stripe Dashboard → Products)
STRIPE_PRICE_BASE_MONTHLY=price_...        # $99/month
STRIPE_PRICE_SCOUT_ADDON_MONTHLY=price_... # $199/month

# ── AI PROVIDERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-...

# Get from: https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Get from: https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY=AI...

# ── CLOUDFLARE R2 ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: Cloudflare Dashboard → R2 → Manage R2 API tokens
CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=abc123...
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...

# Bucket names
R2_KNOWLEDGE_DOCS_BUCKET=know24-knowledge-docs
R2_PRODUCT_ASSETS_BUCKET=know24-product-assets
R2_GENERATED_IMAGES_BUCKET=know24-generated-images
R2_PUBLIC_PDFS_BUCKET=know24-public-pdfs

# Public R2 dev subdomain (for generated images CDN)
NEXT_PUBLIC_R2_PUBLIC_URL=https://pub-xxxx.r2.dev

# ── UPSTASH REDIS ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://console.upstash.com → Redis → Details
UPSTASH_REDIS_URL=rediss://default:password@hostname:6380
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=https://xxx.upstash.io
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=AX...

# ── SENTRY ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get from: https://sentry.io → Settings → Projects → Client Keys
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://xxx@xxx.ingest.sentry.io/xxx
SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=sntrys_...  # For source map uploads in CI
SENTRY_ORG=know24
SENTRY_PROJECT=know24-app

# ── EMAIL (Resend — for transactional emails) ────────────────
# Get from: https://resend.com/api-keys
RESEND_API_KEY=re_...
RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=noreply@know24.io

# ── CLOUDFLARE (for subdomain routing) ───────────────────────
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=...  # For Workers KV updates (slug → business_id mapping)
CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID=...

# ── INTERNAL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Secret for signing internal API calls from Railway workers to Next.js
INTERNAL_API_SECRET=...  # Generate: openssl rand -hex 32

# Cron secret (for Supabase Edge Function cron trigger)
CRON_SECRET=...  # Generate: openssl rand -hex 32
```

### Environment Separation

| Variable Category | Development | Staging | Production |
|---|---|---|---|
| Clerk keys | Test mode (`pk_test_`, `sk_test_`) | Test mode | Live mode (`pk_live_`, `sk_live_`) |
| Stripe keys | Test mode (`sk_test_`) | Test mode | Live mode (`sk_live_`) |
| Supabase project | Separate dev project | Separate staging project | Production project |
| OpenAI keys | Same key OK | Same key OK | Production key with spend limits |
| R2 buckets | `-dev` suffix buckets | `-staging` suffix buckets | Production buckets |
| `NODE_ENV` | `development` | `staging` | `production` |

### Secret Management Strategy

**Local development**: `.env.local` file (gitignored). Never committed.

**Vercel deployments**: All secrets stored in Vercel Project Settings → Environment Variables. Scoped per environment (Production/Preview/Development). Vercel injects them at build time and runtime.

**Railway workers**: Railway project environment variables (Dashboard → Variables). Synced manually or via Railway CLI:
```bash
railway variables set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
```

**Rotation procedure**:
1. Generate new secret in provider dashboard
2. Add new key to Vercel/Railway with `_NEW` suffix
3. Deploy code that reads both (old and new)
4. Verify in production
5. Remove old key from code
6. Delete old secret from Vercel/Railway

**Gitignore additions**:
```
.env.local
.env.staging
.env.production
.env*.local
*.pem
```

**Auditing**: Review Vercel and Railway env vars quarterly. Rotate API keys annually at minimum, or immediately upon any suspected compromise.


### Key File Details

**`src/app/layout.tsx`** — Root layout. Wraps everything in `<ClerkProvider>`, `<ThemeProvider>`, `<QueryProvider>`, and `<ToastProvider>`. Loads Inter font via `next/font/local`. Sets default metadata.

**`src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`** — Dashboard shell. Uses Clerk's `auth()` to enforce authentication server-side. Renders `<DashboardSidebar>` + `<DashboardHeader>` + `{children}`. The slide-overs render here via URL state or Zustand.

**`src/app/s/[slug]/layout.tsx`** — Storefront shell. Zero Know24 branding. Fetches `storefront_config` by slug at RSC level. Injects creator's brand colors via CSS custom properties on `<html>`.

**`src/workers/index.ts`** — Entry point for Railway. Imports all processors, registers them with their respective BullMQ `Worker` instances, starts them with appropriate concurrency settings (e.g., product-generation: concurrency 3; scout-scan: concurrency 2). Sets up graceful shutdown on SIGTERM.

**`supabase/migrations/00007_rls_policies.sql`** — The most critical security file. Every table's RLS policy is defined here, reviewed, and audited. No ad-hoc RLS anywhere else.

**`src/types/database.ts`** — Auto-generated by `supabase gen types typescript --project-id xxx > src/types/database.ts`. Run this after every migration. Provides full TypeScript types for all Supabase tables, views, and functions.

### TypeScript Configuration

```json
// tsconfig.json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

### Key `package.json` Scripts

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev --turbopack",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "worker:dev": "tsx watch src/workers/index.ts",
    "worker:build": "tsc -p tsconfig.worker.json",
    "worker:start": "node dist/workers/index.js",
    "db:types": "supabase gen types typescript --project-id $SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID > src/types/database.ts",
    "db:migrate": "supabase db push",
    "db:reset": "supabase db reset",
    "db:seed": "tsx supabase/seed.ts"
  }
}
```

# Know24 Build Plan — Part 2: Database Schema, API Design & Authentication

> **Audience:** AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor) and senior developers.
> Every SQL block is executable. Every API spec is implementable. Copy-paste into Supabase and build.

## 2.1 Database Schema (Complete SQL)

### Prerequisites & Extensions

```sql
-- Run once on your Supabase project before any migrations
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "vector";           -- pgvector for embeddings
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pg_trgm";          -- fuzzy text search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "btree_gin";        -- composite GIN indexes

-- Custom types
CREATE TYPE subscription_status AS ENUM (
  'trialing', 'active', 'past_due', 'canceled', 'unpaid', 'paused'
);

CREATE TYPE product_type_enum AS ENUM (
  'guide_ebook',
  'framework_template_pack',
  'cheat_sheet',
  'email_course',
  'assessment_quiz',
  'prompt_pack',
  'swipe_file',
  'resource_directory',
  'worksheet_workbook',
  'mini_course',
  'chatbot',
  'expert_engine'
);

CREATE TYPE pricing_model AS ENUM (
  'one_time',
  'subscription_monthly',
  'subscription_annual',
  'per_use',
  'free',
  'bundle'
);

CREATE TYPE knowledge_source_type AS ENUM (
  'url',
  'document_upload',
  'video_link',
  'ai_interview'
);

CREATE TYPE knowledge_status AS ENUM (
  'pending', 'processing', 'analyzed', 'failed'
);

CREATE TYPE product_status AS ENUM (
  'draft', 'generating', 'review', 'active', 'archived'
);

CREATE TYPE order_status AS ENUM (
  'pending', 'completed', 'refunded', 'disputed', 'canceled'
);

CREATE TYPE email_sequence_type AS ENUM (
  'welcome', 'nurture', 'product_launch', 're_engagement', 'custom'
);

CREATE TYPE scout_opportunity_type AS ENUM (
  'hot_thread', 'influencer_match', 'podcast_opportunity',
  'trending_topic', 'community_engagement', 'competitor_activity'
);

CREATE TYPE scout_opportunity_status AS ENUM (
  'pending', 'approved', 'dismissed', 'acted'
);

CREATE TYPE activity_event_type AS ENUM (
  'sale', 'subscription_started', 'subscription_canceled',
  'blog_published', 'product_created', 'product_updated',
  'scout_scan_completed', 'email_sequence_sent',
  'social_post_generated', 'referral_converted',
  'customer_signup', 'knowledge_ingested', 'storefront_published',
  'support_ticket_opened', 'support_ticket_closed',
  'ai_workspace_action'
);

CREATE TYPE support_ticket_status AS ENUM (
  'open', 'in_progress', 'waiting_customer', 'resolved', 'closed'
);

CREATE TYPE support_ticket_priority AS ENUM (
  'low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent'
);
```

---

### Table: `users`

```sql
-- Synced from Clerk via webhook. Single source of truth for identity.
CREATE TABLE public.users (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  clerk_user_id     TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- Clerk's user ID (user_xxxx)
  email             TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  email_verified    BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  first_name        TEXT,
  last_name         TEXT,
  display_name      TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
                      COALESCE(first_name || ' ' || last_name, first_name, last_name, email)
                    ) STORED,
  avatar_url        TEXT,
  bio               TEXT,
  timezone          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UTC',
  -- Know24 subscription (Stripe Billing)
  stripe_customer_id TEXT UNIQUE,
  -- Metadata
  onboarding_completed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  last_seen_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  deleted_at        TIMESTAMPTZ  -- soft delete
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.users IS 'Know24 platform users, synced from Clerk. Each user can own multiple businesses.';
COMMENT ON COLUMN public.users.clerk_user_id IS 'Clerk user_id, used to look up the local user record on every authenticated request.';

CREATE INDEX idx_users_clerk_user_id ON public.users (clerk_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON public.users (email);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_stripe_customer_id ON public.users (stripe_customer_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_deleted_at ON public.users (deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Auto-update updated_at
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION set_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = NOW();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_users_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

-- Row Level Security
ALTER TABLE public.users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "users_select_own" ON public.users
  FOR SELECT USING (clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub');

CREATE POLICY "users_update_own" ON public.users
  FOR UPDATE USING (clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub');

-- Service role bypasses RLS (used by Clerk webhook handler)
```

---

### Table: `organizations`

```sql
-- A Know24 organization maps to a Clerk organization (for team/RBAC support).
-- Solo users have an auto-created personal organization.
CREATE TABLE public.organizations (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  clerk_org_id      TEXT UNIQUE,                    -- NULL for personal orgs
  owner_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.users (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  name              TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug              TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  -- Know24 subscription
  stripe_customer_id         TEXT UNIQUE,
  stripe_subscription_id     TEXT UNIQUE,
  stripe_price_id            TEXT,
  subscription_status        subscription_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'trialing',
  subscription_current_period_end TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Scout add-on
  scout_enabled              BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  scout_stripe_subscription_id TEXT UNIQUE,
  -- Usage tracking
  ai_tokens_used_this_month  BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  ai_tokens_ceiling          BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 5000000,  -- generous default
  social_posts_used_this_month INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  social_posts_ceiling        INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 300,
  scout_scans_used_this_month INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  scout_scans_ceiling         INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 20,
  usage_reset_at             TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT DATE_TRUNC('month', NOW()) + INTERVAL '1 month',
  -- Metadata
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  deleted_at        TIMESTAMPTZ
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.organizations IS 'Top-level billing and team entity. Each org pays one Know24 subscription and may contain multiple businesses.';

CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_owner_id ON public.organizations (owner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_clerk_org_id ON public.organizations (clerk_org_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_stripe_customer_id ON public.organizations (stripe_customer_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_subscription_status ON public.organizations (subscription_status);
CREATE INDEX idx_organizations_deleted_at ON public.organizations (deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_organizations_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.organizations
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.organizations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Helper: return all org IDs the current user belongs to
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auth.user_org_ids()
RETURNS SETOF UUID LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
  SELECT o.id
  FROM public.organizations o
  JOIN public.organization_members om ON om.organization_id = o.id
  JOIN public.users u ON u.id = om.user_id
  WHERE u.clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub'
    AND o.deleted_at IS NULL
$$;

CREATE POLICY "orgs_select_member" ON public.organizations
  FOR SELECT USING (id IN (SELECT auth.user_org_ids()));

CREATE POLICY "orgs_update_owner" ON public.organizations
  FOR UPDATE USING (
    owner_id IN (SELECT id FROM public.users WHERE clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub')
  );
```

### Table: `organization_members`

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.organization_members (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('owner', 'admin', 'member')),
  invited_by      UUID REFERENCES public.users (id),
  joined_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (organization_id, user_id)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.organization_members IS 'Many-to-many: users <-> organizations with role-based access.';

CREATE INDEX idx_org_members_org_id ON public.organization_members (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_org_members_user_id ON public.organization_members (user_id);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_org_members_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.organization_members
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.organization_members ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "org_members_select" ON public.organization_members
  FOR SELECT USING (organization_id IN (SELECT auth.user_org_ids()));

CREATE POLICY "org_members_insert_owner_admin" ON public.organization_members
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    organization_id IN (
      SELECT om.organization_id FROM public.organization_members om
      JOIN public.users u ON u.id = om.user_id
      WHERE u.clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub'
        AND om.role IN ('owner', 'admin')
    )
  );
```

### Table: `businesses`

```sql
-- Each user/org can have N businesses. Each is a fully isolated knowledge business.
CREATE TABLE public.businesses (
  id                    UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  organization_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  owner_id              UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.users (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  -- Identity
  name                  TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug                  TEXT NOT NULL,               -- used in URLs
  tagline               TEXT,
  bio                   TEXT,
  niche                 TEXT,                        -- "Nurse Leadership", "Fitness Coaching"
  credibility_line      TEXT,                        -- "15 years in ICU & ER"
  avatar_url            TEXT,
  -- Status
  status                TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'setup'
                          CHECK (status IN ('setup', 'active', 'paused', 'archived')),
  onboarding_step       INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (onboarding_step BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
  onboarding_completed  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  -- Stripe Connect (for storefront payments)
  stripe_account_id     TEXT UNIQUE,                -- Stripe Connect account ID
  stripe_account_status TEXT CHECK (stripe_account_status IN ('pending', 'active', 'restricted')),
  -- Scout
  scout_enabled         BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  -- Metadata
  created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  deleted_at            TIMESTAMPTZ,
  UNIQUE (organization_id, slug)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.businesses IS 'An individual knowledge business. Each has its own knowledge base, products, storefront, and analytics.';

CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_organization_id ON public.businesses (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_owner_id ON public.businesses (owner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_slug ON public.businesses (slug);
CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_status ON public.businesses (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_stripe_account_id ON public.businesses (stripe_account_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_businesses_deleted_at ON public.businesses (deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_businesses_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.businesses
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.businesses ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Helper: return all business IDs accessible to current user
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auth.user_business_ids()
RETURNS SETOF UUID LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
  SELECT b.id
  FROM public.businesses b
  WHERE b.organization_id IN (SELECT auth.user_org_ids())
    AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
$$;

CREATE POLICY "businesses_select_member" ON public.businesses
  FOR SELECT USING (id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));

CREATE POLICY "businesses_insert_member" ON public.businesses
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (organization_id IN (SELECT auth.user_org_ids()));

CREATE POLICY "businesses_update_member" ON public.businesses
  FOR UPDATE USING (id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));

CREATE POLICY "businesses_delete_owner" ON public.businesses
  FOR DELETE USING (
    owner_id IN (SELECT id FROM public.users WHERE clerk_user_id = auth.jwt()->>'sub')
  );
```

### Table: `knowledge_items`

```sql
-- Each piece of ingested knowledge per business. Core of the RAG system.
CREATE TABLE public.knowledge_items (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Source
  source_type     knowledge_source_type NOT NULL,
  source_url      TEXT,
  source_filename TEXT,
  source_title    TEXT,
  -- Content
  raw_content     TEXT,                           -- original text
  processed_content TEXT,                         -- cleaned/chunked text
  summary         TEXT,                           -- AI-generated summary
  key_topics      TEXT[],                         -- extracted topics
  -- Embeddings (pgvector)
  embedding       VECTOR(1536),                   -- OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
  -- Processing
  status          knowledge_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  token_count     INT,
  error_message   TEXT,
  -- Metadata
  metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',    -- arbitrary source metadata
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  deleted_at      TIMESTAMPTZ
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.knowledge_items IS 'All ingested knowledge per business. Embeddings power RAG for chatbot, expert engine, and product generation.';

-- Standard indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_business_id ON public.knowledge_items (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_status ON public.knowledge_items (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_source_type ON public.knowledge_items (source_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_deleted_at ON public.knowledge_items (deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- HNSW vector index for fast ANN search (pgvector 0.5+)
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_embedding ON public.knowledge_items
  USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

-- Full-text search index
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_fts ON public.knowledge_items
  USING gin (to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(processed_content, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(summary, '')));

-- GIN index on key_topics array
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_items_key_topics ON public.knowledge_items USING gin (key_topics);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_knowledge_items_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.knowledge_items
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.knowledge_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "knowledge_items_business_member" ON public.knowledge_items
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `knowledge_chunks`

```sql
-- Chunked knowledge for RAG. Each item is split into overlapping chunks.
CREATE TABLE public.knowledge_chunks (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  knowledge_item_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.knowledge_items (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  chunk_index     INT NOT NULL,
  content         TEXT NOT NULL,
  token_count     INT,
  embedding       VECTOR(1536),
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.knowledge_chunks IS 'Overlapping text chunks of knowledge items for fine-grained RAG retrieval.';

CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_chunks_item_id ON public.knowledge_chunks (knowledge_item_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_chunks_business_id ON public.knowledge_chunks (business_id);

-- HNSW on chunks for semantic search
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_chunks_embedding ON public.knowledge_chunks
  USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

ALTER TABLE public.knowledge_chunks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "knowledge_chunks_business_member" ON public.knowledge_chunks
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

```sql
-- Static lookup table for the 12 product types. Seeded once.
CREATE TABLE public.product_types (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  slug            product_type_enum NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  display_name    TEXT NOT NULL,
  description     TEXT NOT NULL,
  icon_name       TEXT,                           -- lucide icon name
  sort_order      INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_digital      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  is_ai_product   BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, -- true for chatbot, expert_engine
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.product_types IS 'Static lookup for the 12 Know24 product types. No RLS needed — public read.';
```

### Table: `products`

```sql
-- Each saleable product per business.
CREATE TABLE public.products (
  id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_type_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.product_types (id),
  -- Identity
  title               TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug                TEXT NOT NULL,
  tagline             TEXT,
  description         TEXT,
  -- AI-generated content
  content             JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', -- type-specific: chapters, slides, prompts, etc.
  cover_image_url     TEXT,
  preview_content     TEXT,                        -- free preview snippet
  -- Pricing
  pricing_model       pricing_model NOT NULL DEFAULT 'one_time',
  price_cents         INT,                         -- in cents (USD)
  compare_price_cents INT,                         -- crossed-out original price
  stripe_price_id     TEXT,
  stripe_product_id   TEXT,
  -- Storefront
  is_lead_magnet      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, -- gated by email, free
  is_featured         BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  sort_order          INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  -- Status
  status              product_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  published_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Expert Engine config (type = expert_engine)
  ee_knowledge_scope  UUID[],                      -- specific knowledge_item IDs, NULL = all
  ee_description      TEXT,
  -- Chatbot config (type = chatbot)
  chatbot_system_prompt TEXT,
  chatbot_personality TEXT,
  chatbot_knowledge_scope UUID[],
  chatbot_recommend_products BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  -- Metadata
  ai_generation_prompt TEXT,                       -- prompt used to generate this product
  metadata            JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  deleted_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
  UNIQUE (business_id, slug)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.products IS 'All digital products, chatbots, and expert engines per business.';

CREATE INDEX idx_products_business_id ON public.products (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_product_type_id ON public.products (product_type_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_status ON public.products (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_is_lead_magnet ON public.products (is_lead_magnet);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_pricing_model ON public.products (pricing_model);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_stripe_product_id ON public.products (stripe_product_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_deleted_at ON public.products (deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Full-text search on products
CREATE INDEX idx_products_fts ON public.products
  USING gin (to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(title, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(description, '')));

CREATE TRIGGER trg_products_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.products
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.products ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "products_rw_business_member" ON public.products
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));

-- Public read for active products on storefronts (accessed via service role or separate schema)
-- See storefront_* tables and public API endpoints below
```

### Table: `storefronts`

```sql
-- One storefront per business. Config for the public-facing site.
CREATE TABLE public.storefronts (
  id                    UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id           UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE UNIQUE,
  -- Domain
  subdomain             TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,         -- e.g. "sarah-chen" → sarah-chen.know24.com
  custom_domain         TEXT UNIQUE,                  -- e.g. "nurseacademy.com"
  custom_domain_verified BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  -- Branding
  color_palette         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'teal', -- preset name
  primary_color         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#0891b2',
  secondary_color       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#1e293b',
  accent_color          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#f97316',
  font_family           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Inter',
  logo_url              TEXT,
  -- Hero section
  hero_title            TEXT,
  hero_tagline          TEXT,
  hero_credibility      TEXT,
  hero_cta_primary      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Browse Products',
  hero_cta_secondary    TEXT,
  -- About section
  about_title           TEXT,
  about_body            TEXT,
  about_photo_url       TEXT,
  -- Lead magnet
  lead_magnet_product_id UUID REFERENCES public.products (id),
  lead_magnet_headline  TEXT,
  -- Social links
  social_links          JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', -- {twitter, linkedin, instagram, ...}
  -- SEO
  meta_title            TEXT,
  meta_description      TEXT,
  -- Status
  is_published          BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  published_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Schema/GEO markup (JSON-LD)
  schema_markup         JSONB,
  -- Metadata
  created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.storefronts IS 'Public storefront configuration per business. Controls domain, branding, hero, and SEO.';

CREATE INDEX idx_storefronts_business_id ON public.storefronts (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_storefronts_subdomain ON public.storefronts (subdomain);
CREATE INDEX idx_storefronts_custom_domain ON public.storefronts (custom_domain);
CREATE INDEX idx_storefronts_is_published ON public.storefronts (is_published);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_storefronts_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.storefronts
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.storefronts ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "storefronts_rw_business_member" ON public.storefronts
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

```sql
-- Blog posts per business storefront (creator's blog, not Know24's blog).

  embedding       VECTOR(1536),

);

CREATE INDEX idx_blog_posts_fts ON public.blog_posts
  USING gin (to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(title, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(body, '')));

CREATE INDEX idx_blog_posts_embedding ON public.blog_posts
  USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_blog_posts_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.blog_posts
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.blog_posts ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "blog_posts_rw_business_member" ON public.blog_posts
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `customers`

```sql
-- People who purchase from storefronts. Separate from Know24 users (creators).
CREATE TABLE public.customers (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  -- A customer may purchase from multiple businesses; linked per-business by email
  email             TEXT NOT NULL,
  first_name        TEXT,
  last_name         TEXT,
  -- Stripe
  stripe_customer_id TEXT,                        -- may have different IDs per Connect account
  -- Referral
  referral_code_used TEXT,
  referred_by_customer_id UUID REFERENCES public.customers (id),
  -- Metadata
  metadata          JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (email)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.customers IS 'End customers who buy from storefronts. Separate identity from Know24 creator accounts.';

CREATE INDEX idx_customers_email ON public.customers (email);
CREATE INDEX idx_customers_stripe_customer_id ON public.customers (stripe_customer_id);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_customers_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.customers
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

-- Customers are read by creators; full table managed by service role
ALTER TABLE public.customers ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "customers_select_by_business_member" ON public.customers
  FOR SELECT USING (
    id IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT o.customer_id FROM public.orders o
      WHERE o.business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids())
    )
  );
```

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.orders (

  refunded_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  refund_reason       TEXT,

  metadata            JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Table: `order_items`

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.order_items (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  order_id        UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.orders (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  product_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.products (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  -- Snapshot at time of purchase (products can change)
  product_title   TEXT NOT NULL,
  product_type    TEXT NOT NULL,
  pricing_model   pricing_model NOT NULL,
  unit_price_cents INT NOT NULL,
  quantity        INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  total_cents     INT NOT NULL,
  -- Access
  access_granted  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  access_granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  download_url    TEXT,                           -- R2 signed URL for digital downloads
  download_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Metadata
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.order_items IS 'Line items for orders. Includes a snapshot of product info at purchase time.';

CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order_id ON public.order_items (order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_business_id ON public.order_items (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_product_id ON public.order_items (product_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_customer ON public.order_items (order_id, product_id);

ALTER TABLE public.order_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "order_items_select_business_member" ON public.order_items
  FOR SELECT USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```
