### Table: `subscriptions`

```sql
-- Customer subscriptions to chatbot or other recurring products.
CREATE TABLE public.subscriptions (
  id                        UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id               UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  customer_id               UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.customers (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  product_id                UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.products (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  -- Stripe
  stripe_subscription_id    TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  stripe_price_id           TEXT NOT NULL,
  stripe_current_period_start TIMESTAMPTZ,
  stripe_current_period_end   TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Status
  status                    subscription_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  canceled_at               TIMESTAMPTZ,
  cancel_at_period_end      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  -- Metadata
  metadata                  JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at                TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at                TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.subscriptions IS 'Active and historical customer subscriptions to recurring products (chatbot, etc.).';

CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_business_id ON public.subscriptions (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_customer_id ON public.subscriptions (customer_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_product_id ON public.subscriptions (product_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_status ON public.subscriptions (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_stripe_subscription_id ON public.subscriptions (stripe_subscription_id);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_subscriptions_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.subscriptions
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.subscriptions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "subscriptions_select_business_member" ON public.subscriptions
  FOR SELECT USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

```sql
-- Email list per business.
CREATE TABLE public.email_subscribers (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  customer_id       UUID REFERENCES public.customers (id),
  email             TEXT NOT NULL,
  first_name        TEXT,
  -- Status

  subscribed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),

  unsubscribe_reason TEXT,

  source            TEXT,                         -- "lead_magnet", "checkout", "referral", "manual"
  lead_magnet_id    UUID REFERENCES public.products (id),

  tags              TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- Metadata
  metadata          JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (business_id, email)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_email_subscribers_is_subscribed ON public.email_subscribers (is_subscribed);
CREATE INDEX idx_email_subscribers_tags ON public.email_subscribers USING gin (tags);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_email_subscribers_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.email_subscribers
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.email_subscribers ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "email_subscribers_rw_business_member" ON public.email_subscribers
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `scout_scans`

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.scout_scans (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Execution
  status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed')),
  started_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  completed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  error_message   TEXT,
  -- Results
  opportunities_found INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  platforms_scanned   TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', -- ['reddit', 'x', 'linkedin', ...]
  -- Metadata
  triggered_by    UUID REFERENCES public.users (id), -- NULL = auto-scan
  metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.scout_scans IS 'Execution records for Scout market intelligence scans. Limited to 20/month per business.';

CREATE INDEX idx_scout_scans_business_id ON public.scout_scans (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_scans_status ON public.scout_scans (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_scans_created_at ON public.scout_scans (created_at DESC);

ALTER TABLE public.scout_scans ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "scout_scans_rw_business_member" ON public.scout_scans
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `scout_opportunities`

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.scout_opportunities (
  id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  scan_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.scout_scans (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  business_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Classification
  opportunity_type    scout_opportunity_type NOT NULL,
  platform            TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'reddit', 'x', 'linkedin', 'podcast', etc.
  -- Content
  title               TEXT NOT NULL,
  context             TEXT NOT NULL,               -- "What's happening, where, and why it matters"
  why_it_matters      TEXT,
  url                 TEXT,
  author_handle       TEXT,
  -- Scoring
  relevance_score     NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, -- 0–100
  -- AI suggestions
  suggested_moves     TEXT[],
  draft_content       TEXT,                        -- AI-written response/post/outreach
  -- Status
  status              scout_opportunity_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  acted_at            TIMESTAMPTZ,
  dismissed_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Related product
  related_product_id  UUID REFERENCES public.products (id),
  -- Metadata
  raw_data            JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.scout_opportunities IS 'Individual opportunities surfaced by Scout scans. Each has a draft action ready.';

CREATE INDEX idx_scout_opportunities_scan_id ON public.scout_opportunities (scan_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_opportunities_business_id ON public.scout_opportunities (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_opportunities_status ON public.scout_opportunities (status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_opportunities_relevance ON public.scout_opportunities (relevance_score DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_scout_opportunities_type ON public.scout_opportunities (opportunity_type);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_scout_opportunities_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.scout_opportunities
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.scout_opportunities ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "scout_opportunities_rw_business_member" ON public.scout_opportunities
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `referrals`

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.referrals (
  id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Referrer (the affiliate/customer doing the referring)
  referrer_customer_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.customers (id),
  referral_code       TEXT NOT NULL,
  -- The referred purchase
  referred_customer_id UUID REFERENCES public.customers (id),
  referred_order_id   UUID REFERENCES public.orders (id),
  -- Commission
  commission_rate     NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL,       -- e.g. 20.00 = 20%
  commission_cents    INT,                         -- calculated on conversion
  -- Status
  status              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                        CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'converted', 'paid', 'rejected')),
  converted_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  paid_at             TIMESTAMPTZ,
  stripe_transfer_id  TEXT,                        -- Stripe Connect payout
  -- Metadata
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.referrals IS 'Referral tracking. Commission paid out via Stripe Connect transfers.';

CREATE INDEX idx_referrals_business_id ON public.referrals (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_referrals_referrer_customer_id ON public.referrals (referrer_customer_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_referrals_referral_code ON public.referrals (referral_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_referrals_status ON public.referrals (status);

-- Back-fill FK on orders
ALTER TABLE public.orders ADD CONSTRAINT fk_orders_referral
  FOREIGN KEY (referral_id) REFERENCES public.referrals (id);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_referrals_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.referrals
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.referrals ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "referrals_rw_business_member" ON public.referrals
  FOR ALL USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

### Table: `ai_usage`

```sql
-- Metered AI token usage per business, per month.
CREATE TABLE public.ai_usage (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Period
  period_year     INT NOT NULL,
  period_month    INT NOT NULL,
  -- Token counts by feature
  tokens_knowledge_ingestion  BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_product_generation   BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_chatbot_queries      BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_expert_engine        BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_blog_generation      BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_email_generation     BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_social_generation    BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_scout                BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_workspace_chat       BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_other                BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_total                BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
    tokens_knowledge_ingestion + tokens_product_generation +
    tokens_chatbot_queries + tokens_expert_engine +
    tokens_blog_generation + tokens_email_generation +
    tokens_social_generation + tokens_scout +
    tokens_workspace_chat + tokens_other
  ) STORED,
  -- Overage billing
  overage_tokens  BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  overage_charged BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  overage_stripe_invoice_id TEXT,
  -- Metadata
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (business_id, period_year, period_month)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.ai_usage IS 'Monthly AI token consumption per business. Used for metered billing and ceiling enforcement.';

CREATE INDEX idx_ai_usage_business_id ON public.ai_usage (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ai_usage_organization_id ON public.ai_usage (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ai_usage_period ON public.ai_usage (period_year, period_month);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_ai_usage_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.ai_usage
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.ai_usage ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "ai_usage_select_business_member" ON public.ai_usage
  FOR SELECT USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

```sql
  output_tokens   INT,

);
```

### Table: `activity_log`

```sql
-- Immutable event stream per business. Powers the Activity dashboard page.
CREATE TABLE public.activity_log (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Event
  event_type      activity_event_type NOT NULL,
  title           TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- human-readable: "New sale: Charge Nurse Playbook"
  description     TEXT,
  -- References (all nullable — only relevant FK is set)
  actor_user_id   UUID REFERENCES public.users (id),
  actor_customer_id UUID REFERENCES public.customers (id),
  product_id      UUID REFERENCES public.products (id),
  order_id        UUID REFERENCES public.orders (id),
  blog_post_id    UUID REFERENCES public.blog_posts (id),
  scout_scan_id   UUID REFERENCES public.scout_scans (id),
  -- Link (for click-through from activity feed)
  action_url      TEXT,                           -- e.g. "/dashboard?business=xxx&panel=sales"
  -- Payload
  metadata        JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- Revenue (for financial events)
  amount_cents    INT,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.activity_log IS 'Immutable event stream per business. Append-only. Powers the Activity page and real-time notifications.';

CREATE INDEX idx_activity_log_business_id ON public.activity_log (business_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_log_organization_id ON public.activity_log (organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_log_event_type ON public.activity_log (event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_log_actor_user_id ON public.activity_log (actor_user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_log_order_id ON public.activity_log (order_id);

ALTER TABLE public.activity_log ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "activity_log_select_business_member" ON public.activity_log
  FOR SELECT USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));

-- Only service role inserts into activity log (never client-side)
CREATE POLICY "activity_log_insert_service" ON public.activity_log
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (false); -- blocked for authenticated users; service role bypasses
```

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.product_reviews (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  product_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.products (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  business_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.businesses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  customer_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.customers (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  order_id        UUID REFERENCES public.orders (id),
  -- Review
  rating          INT NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
  title           TEXT,
  body            TEXT,
  -- Moderation
  is_approved     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  approved_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  -- Metadata
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (product_id, customer_id)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.product_reviews IS 'Customer reviews on products. Moderated before appearing on storefront.';

CREATE INDEX idx_product_reviews_product_id ON public.product_reviews (product_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_product_reviews_business_id ON public.product_reviews (business_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_product_reviews_is_approved ON public.product_reviews (is_approved);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_product_reviews_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.product_reviews
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.product_reviews ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "product_reviews_select_business_member" ON public.product_reviews
  FOR SELECT USING (business_id IN (SELECT auth.user_business_ids()));
```

---

### Semantic Search Helper Function

```sql
-- Used by chatbot, expert engine, and AI workspace for RAG retrieval
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION match_knowledge_chunks(
  p_business_id    UUID,
  p_query_embedding VECTOR(1536),
  p_match_count    INT DEFAULT 5,
  p_match_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.7
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id              UUID,
  knowledge_item_id UUID,
  content         TEXT,
  similarity      FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    kc.id,
    kc.knowledge_item_id,
    kc.content,
    1 - (kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding) AS similarity
  FROM public.knowledge_chunks kc
  WHERE kc.business_id = p_business_id
    AND kc.embedding IS NOT NULL
    AND 1 - (kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding) >= p_match_threshold
  ORDER BY kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding
  LIMIT p_match_count;
END;
$$;

COMMENT ON FUNCTION match_knowledge_chunks IS 'Cosine similarity search over knowledge chunks for a given business. Called by RAG pipeline.';
```

---

## 2.2 API Route Design

> **Conventions:**
> - All routes are Next.js App Router API routes under `/app/api/`
> - Auth: `🔒 Creator` = Clerk JWT required | `🌐 Public` = no auth | `🔑 API Key` = Expert Engine key | `⚙️ Webhook` = signature verified
> - All responses: `{ data, error, meta }` envelope
> - All errors: `{ error: { code, message, details } }` with appropriate HTTP status
> - Rate limits enforced via Upstash Redis + `@upstash/ratelimit`
> - Request/response bodies are TypeScript types (use `zod` for validation)

---

### Auth & Users

#### `POST /api/webhooks/clerk`
```
Auth:        ⚙️ Webhook (svix signature verification)
Rate Limit:  N/A (server-to-server)
Purpose:     Sync user creates/updates/deletes from Clerk to local users table.

Events handled:
  user.created    → INSERT into users
  user.updated    → UPDATE users SET ... WHERE clerk_user_id = event.data.id
  user.deleted    → UPDATE users SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE clerk_user_id = event.data.id
  org.created     → INSERT into organizations
  org.updated     → UPDATE organizations
  org.deleted     → soft delete organizations
  orgMembership.created → INSERT into organization_members
  orgMembership.deleted → DELETE from organization_members

Request Headers:
  svix-id:        string
  svix-timestamp: string
  svix-signature: string

Request Body:   Clerk webhook payload (event object)

Response 200:   { received: true }
Response 400:   Invalid signature
Response 500:   Sync error (retry by Clerk)
```

#### `GET /api/users/me`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Purpose:     Return the current user's profile + org/business summary.

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    user: User,
    organizations: Organization[],
    businesses: { id, name, status, scout_enabled }[]
  }
}
```

#### `PATCH /api/users/me`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  20/min
Purpose:     Update profile fields (bio, timezone, avatar_url).

Request Body:
{
  first_name?:  string
  last_name?:   string
  bio?:         string
  timezone?:    string
  avatar_url?:  string
}

Response 200: { data: { user: User } }
```

### Knowledge Intake

#### `GET /api/businesses/[id]/knowledge`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Response 200: { data: { items: KnowledgeItem[] } }
```

#### `POST /api/businesses/[id]/knowledge`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  10/min
Purpose:     Ingest knowledge from any source. Queues background job.

Request Body (multipart/form-data OR application/json):
{
  source_type:      'url' | 'document_upload' | 'video_link' | 'ai_interview'
  url?:             string           (for url, video_link)
  file?:            File             (for document_upload — PDF, DOCX, TXT)
  interview_data?:  { messages: { role, content }[] }  (for ai_interview)
  title?:           string
}

Response 202:
{
  data: {
    knowledge_item: KnowledgeItem   (status: 'pending')
    job_id: string
  }
}

Background job (BullMQ):
  1. Extract text from source
  2. Chunk text (512 token chunks, 50 token overlap)
  3. Generate embeddings for each chunk (text-embedding-3-small)
  4. Extract key topics, generate summary (GPT-4o-mini)
  5. Update knowledge_item status → 'analyzed'
  6. Log to activity_log
```

#### `POST /api/businesses/[id]/knowledge/analyze`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  5/min
Purpose:     Trigger AI analysis of all ingested knowledge → product recommendations.

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    summary:        string           (overall knowledge summary)
    topics:         string[]         (key topic areas)
    recommendations: {
      product_type: product_type_enum
      title:        string
      description:  string
      price_cents:  number
      rationale:    string
    }[]
  }
}
```

#### `DELETE /api/businesses/[id]/knowledge/[itemId]`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  20/min
Response 200: { data: { deleted: true } }
```

### Orders & Payments

#### `POST /api/storefront/[subdomain]/checkout`
```
Auth:        🌐 Public
Rate Limit:  10/min per IP
Purpose:     Create a Stripe Checkout Session for product purchase.

Request Body:
{
  product_id:       UUID
  customer_email:   string
  success_url:      string
  cancel_url:       string
  referral_code?:   string
}

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    checkout_url:  string   (Stripe hosted checkout URL)
    session_id:    string
  }
}

Implementation:
  - Use Stripe Connect: payment_intent_data.application_fee_amount (Know24 take)
  - on_behalf_of: creator's Stripe Connect account ID
  - metadata: { business_id, product_id, customer_email, referral_code }
```

#### `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`
```
Auth:        ⚙️ Webhook (Stripe-Signature header verification)
Rate Limit:  N/A

Events handled:
  checkout.session.completed:
    - Create/update customer record
    - Create order + order_items
    - Grant product access (generate download URL or API key)
    - Trigger welcome email sequence
    - Log to activity_log
    - Process referral if referral_code present

  payment_intent.payment_failed:
    - Update order status → 'pending' (allow retry)
    - Notify creator

  customer.subscription.created:
    - Create subscriptions record
    - Grant chatbot/EE access
    - Log to activity_log

  customer.subscription.updated:
    - Update subscription status/period

  customer.subscription.deleted:
    - Update subscription status → 'canceled'
    - Revoke access

  invoice.payment_succeeded:
    - Record payment for metered billing
    - Renew access period

  invoice.payment_failed:
    - Update subscription status → 'past_due'
    - Send dunning notification

  charge.dispute.created:
    - Alert creator
    - Flag order for review

  transfer.created:
    - Update referral status → 'paid'

Response 200: { received: true }
```

#### `POST /api/webhooks/stripe-connect`
```
Auth:        ⚙️ Webhook (Stripe-Signature, account-specific)
Rate Limit:  N/A
Purpose:     Handle Connect account events (creator onboarding, payout status).

Events handled:
  account.updated:
    - Update stripe_account_status on businesses
  payout.paid:
    - Log to activity_log
```

#### `GET /api/businesses/[id]/orders`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Query Params: page, limit, status, date_from, date_to, customer_email

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    orders: (Order & { items: OrderItem[], customer: Customer })[],
    meta:   { total, page, limit }
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/businesses/[id]/orders/[orderId]/refund`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  10/min
Request Body: { reason?: string, amount_cents?: number }
Response 200: { data: { order: Order, stripe_refund_id: string } }
```

```
Request Body: { business_id: UUID }

    onboarding_url: string  (Stripe Connect onboarding link)
  }
}
```

```
Response 202:
{
  data: {
    scan: ScoutScan  (status: 'pending')
    job_id: string
  }
}

Background job:
  1. Check scout_scans_used_this_month < ceiling
  2. Parallel web searches: Reddit, X, Quora, LinkedIn, Google News, podcast directories
  3. Rank results by relevance to business knowledge/products
  4. Generate opportunity cards with AI (draft content, suggested moves)
  5. INSERT scout_opportunities
  6. UPDATE scout_scans status → 'completed'
  7. Log to activity_log, send push notification
```

### AI Workspace

#### `POST /api/businesses/[id]/workspace/chat`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  30/min (per user per business)
Purpose:     Streaming chat with the business AI. Executes actions if requested.

Request Body:
{
  messages: { role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string }[]
}

Response: text/event-stream (Server-Sent Events via Vercel AI SDK)
  data: { type: 'text', text: '...' }
  data: { type: 'tool_call', name: '...', args: {...} }
  data: { type: 'tool_result', name: '...', result: {...} }
  data: { type: 'done', usage: { input_tokens, output_tokens } }

Available tools (AI can call these mid-conversation):
  - update_product_price(product_id, price_cents)
  - create_blog_post(topic, target_keyword?)
  - generate_social_post(product_id, length)
  - get_business_stats(period)
  - update_storefront_config(fields)
  - create_product(type, title, pricing_model)
  - trigger_scout_scan()
  - get_email_sequence_stats(sequence_id?)
  - update_email_sequence(sequence_id, updates)
```

#### `GET /api/businesses/[id]/workspace/messages`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Query Params: limit (default 50), before (cursor for pagination)

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    messages: AIWorkspaceMessage[]
    meta: { has_more, next_cursor }
  }
}
```

### Analytics

#### `GET /api/businesses/[id]/analytics/dashboard`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Query Params: period ('7d' | '30d' | '90d', default '30d')

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    revenue: {
      total_cents: number,
      period_cents: number,
      delta_pct: number,
      chart: { date: string, amount_cents: number }[]
    },
    orders: {

      period: number,
      delta_pct: number
    },
    subscribers: {
      active: number,
      new_this_period: number,
      mrr_cents: number
    },
    email_list: {
      total: number,
      new_this_period: number,
      avg_open_rate: number
    },
    top_products: { product: Product, revenue_cents: number, orders: number }[],
    scout_stats: { scans_used: number, opportunities_found: number } | null
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/businesses/[id]/analytics/activity`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator
Rate Limit:  60/min
Query Params: limit (default 50), before (cursor), event_type?

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    events: ActivityLog[],
    meta: { has_more, next_cursor }
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/organizations/[orgId]/analytics`
```
Auth:        🔒 Creator (admin/owner)
Rate Limit:  30/min
Purpose:     Cross-business rollup for orgs with multiple businesses.

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    total_revenue_cents:  number,
    total_orders:         number,
    active_subscriptions: number,
    ai_usage:             { used, ceiling },
    per_business:         { business_id, name, revenue_cents, orders }[]
  }
}
```

---

### Expert Engine (API Access for Buyers)

#### `POST /api/ee/[businessSlug]/query`
```
Auth:        🔑 API Key (Bearer token in Authorization header)
Rate Limit:  Configurable per key (default 100/hour)
Purpose:     Query the Expert Engine. Returns structured knowledge response.

Request Body:
{
  query:        string
  context?:     string          (caller's context)
  max_tokens?:  number          (default 500)
  format?:      'text' | 'json' (default 'text')
}

Response 200:
{
  data: {
    answer:       string,
    sources:      { knowledge_item_id: UUID, excerpt: string, similarity: number }[],
    product_refs?: { product_id: UUID, title: string, url: string }[]  (if chatbot upsell enabled)
  },
  meta: {
    tokens_used:  number,
    latency_ms:   number
  }
}

Response 401: Invalid or expired API key
Response 429: Rate limit exceeded
Response 402: Subscription required or expired
```

---

### Jobs & Background Tasks (Internal)

> These routes are called by Railway workers (or Vercel Cron). Protected by `INTERNAL_API_SECRET` header.

#### `POST /api/internal/knowledge/process-job`
```
Auth:        Internal secret
Purpose:     Process a single knowledge item (embedding, chunking, summarization).
Request Body: { knowledge_item_id: UUID }
```

#### `POST /api/internal/products/generate-job`
```
Auth:        Internal secret
Purpose:     Generate product content from knowledge base.
Request Body: { product_id: UUID }
```

#### `POST /api/internal/scout/run-scan`
```
Auth:        Internal secret
Purpose:     Execute a Scout scan.
Request Body: { scan_id: UUID }
```

#### `POST /api/internal/billing/reset-monthly-usage`
```
Auth:        Internal secret (Vercel Cron — runs 1st of each month)
Purpose:     Reset monthly usage counters for all organizations.
```

#### `POST /api/internal/blog/auto-generate`
```
Auth:        Internal secret (Vercel Cron — runs weekly)
Purpose:     Auto-generate blog posts for active businesses that have < N posts this month.
```

## 2.3 Authentication & Authorization

### Clerk Configuration

```typescript
// app/layout.tsx — wrap entire app
import { ClerkProvider } from '@clerk/nextjs'

// middleware.ts — protect all /dashboard, /api/businesses, etc.
import { authMiddleware } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'

export default authMiddleware({
  publicRoutes: [
    '/',
    '/pricing',
    '/blog(.*)',
    '/help(.*)',
    '/api/webhooks/clerk',
    '/api/webhooks/stripe',
    '/api/webhooks/stripe-connect',
    '/api/storefront/(.*)',          // public storefront reads
    '/api/ee/(.*)',                   // EE uses its own API key auth
    '/[subdomain](.*)',              // storefront SSR pages
  ],
  ignoredRoutes: ['/api/internal/(.*)'],
})
```

#### Clerk Organization Structure

```
Know24 Clerk Application
├── Personal accounts (auto org per user)
│   └── Solo creators: each user has one "personal" org
└── Team accounts (optional, Clerk org)
    ├── Role: owner   → full access to all businesses in org
    ├── Role: admin   → manage products, storefront, marketing; no billing
    └── Role: member  → read-only + AI workspace access
```

#### Role Permission Matrix

| Action | owner | admin | member |
|---|---|---|---|
| Create/delete business | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Edit products / storefront | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| View analytics | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| AI Workspace chat | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Manage email sequences | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Issue refunds | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Billing / subscription | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Invite team members | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Delete org | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

### Middleware Chain

```typescript
// lib/middleware/chain.ts
// Every authenticated API request passes through this chain:

// 1. Clerk Auth — verify JWT, extract userId
const { userId, orgId } = await auth()
if (!userId) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 })

// 2. User Resolution — map Clerk userId → local users row
const user = await db.users.findUnique({ where: { clerk_user_id: userId } })
if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'User not found' }, { status: 404 })

// 3. Org Resolution (for org-scoped routes)
const org = await db.organizations.findFirst({
  where: { clerk_org_id: orgId, deleted_at: null }
})

// 4. Business Scoping (for /api/businesses/[id]/* routes)
const business = await db.businesses.findFirst({
  where: {
    id: params.id,
    organization_id: { in: user_org_ids },
    deleted_at: null,
  }
})
if (!business) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 })

// 5. Role Check (for privileged operations)
const member = await db.organization_members.findFirst({
  where: { user_id: user.id, organization_id: business.organization_id }
})
if (requiredRole === 'owner' && member.role !== 'owner') {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 })
}

// 6. Subscription Check (for Scout and other add-on routes)
if (requiresScout && !business.scout_enabled) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Scout add-on required' }, { status: 402 })
}

// 7. RLS — Supabase queries automatically enforce RLS via JWT claim
// The Supabase client is initialized with the user's Clerk JWT
const supabase = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!, {
  global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${clerkToken}` } }
})
```

---

### Supabase JWT Integration with Clerk

```sql
-- Supabase uses auth.jwt() to read the Clerk JWT claims
-- Configure in Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → JWT Settings:
-- Use Clerk's JWKS endpoint: https://[your-clerk-app].clerk.accounts.dev/.well-known/jwks.json

-- The RLS helper functions read from the JWT:
-- auth.jwt()->>'sub'  → Clerk user_id (maps to users.clerk_user_id)
```

```typescript
// lib/supabase/server.ts
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
import { auth } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'

export async function createSupabaseClient() {
  const { getToken } = await auth()
  const token = await getToken({ template: 'supabase' })  // Clerk JWT template for Supabase

  return createClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    {
      global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } },
      auth: { persistSession: false }
    }
  )
}

// For webhooks and background jobs use service role (bypasses RLS):
export function createSupabaseServiceClient() {
  return createClient(
    process.env.SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!
  )
}
```

---

### Expert Engine API Key Authentication

```typescript
// middleware/expert-engine-auth.ts
import { createHash } from 'crypto'

export async function verifyExpertEngineKey(
  authHeader: string | null,
  businessSlug: string
): Promise<{ valid: boolean; key_record?: ExpertEngineApiKey }> {
  if (!authHeader?.startsWith('Bearer ')) return { valid: false }

  const rawKey = authHeader.slice(7)
  const keyHash = createHash('sha256').update(rawKey).digest('hex')

  const supabase = createSupabaseServiceClient()
  const { data: keyRecord } = await supabase
    .from('expert_engine_api_keys')
    .select('*, product:products(business:businesses(slug))')
    .eq('key_hash', keyHash)
    .eq('is_active', true)
    .single()

  if (!keyRecord) return { valid: false }
  if (keyRecord.product.business.slug !== businessSlug) return { valid: false }
  if (keyRecord.expires_at && new Date(keyRecord.expires_at) < new Date()) return { valid: false }
  if (!keyRecord.subscription_id) return { valid: false } // Must have active subscription

  // Update last_used_at async (don't block response)
  supabase
    .from('expert_engine_api_keys')
    .update({ last_used_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .eq('id', keyRecord.id)

  return { valid: true, key_record: keyRecord }
}
```

---

### Storefront Customer Authentication

Storefront customers (buyers) do **not** use Clerk. They use a lightweight JWT issued by Know24:

```typescript
// lib/storefront-auth.ts
import { SignJWT, jwtVerify } from 'jose'

const STOREFRONT_SECRET = new TextEncoder().encode(process.env.STOREFRONT_JWT_SECRET!)

// Issued at checkout.session.completed
export async function issueCustomerToken(customer: Customer, business_id: string) {
  return new SignJWT({
    sub: customer.id,
    email: customer.email,
    business_id,
    type: 'storefront_customer'
  })
    .setProtectedHeader({ alg: 'HS256' })
    .setExpirationTime('30d')
    .sign(STOREFRONT_SECRET)
}

// Used to gate chatbot/EE access pages
export async function verifyCustomerToken(token: string) {
  const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, STOREFRONT_SECRET)
  return payload
}
```

---

### Webhook Signature Verification

```typescript
// lib/webhooks/clerk.ts
import { Webhook } from 'svix'

export function verifyClerkWebhook(headers: Headers, rawBody: string): ClerkWebhookEvent {
  const wh = new Webhook(process.env.CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)
  return wh.verify(rawBody, {
    'svix-id': headers.get('svix-id')!,
    'svix-timestamp': headers.get('svix-timestamp')!,
    'svix-signature': headers.get('svix-signature')!,
  }) as ClerkWebhookEvent
}

// lib/webhooks/stripe.ts
import Stripe from 'stripe'
const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!)

export function verifyStripeWebhook(rawBody: string, signature: string): Stripe.Event {
  return stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(
    rawBody,
    signature,
    process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!
  )
}
```

---

## 2.4 Stripe Integration Architecture

### Overview

Know24 uses **two distinct Stripe integrations** operating simultaneously:

| Integration | Purpose | Account |
|---|---|---|
| **Stripe Billing** | Know24 subscriptions ($99/mo, $199/mo Scout) | Know24's Stripe account |
| **Stripe Connect** | Creator product sales → creator's bank | Creator's connected Stripe account |

---

### Know24 Subscription Products (Stripe Billing)

```typescript
// Seed once in Stripe Dashboard or via API:

// Product: Know24 Base
const baseProduct = await stripe.products.create({
  name: 'Know24 Base',
  description: 'Full knowledge business platform — per business',
})

const basePrice = await stripe.prices.create({
  product: baseProduct.id,
  unit_amount: 9900,       // $99.00
  currency: 'usd',
  recurring: { interval: 'month' },
  lookup_key: 'know24_base_monthly',
})

// Product: Scout Add-On
const scoutProduct = await stripe.products.create({
  name: 'Know24 Scout Add-On',
  description: 'AI market intelligence — 20 scans/month',
})

const scoutPrice = await stripe.prices.create({
  product: scoutProduct.id,
  unit_amount: 19900,      // $199.00
  currency: 'usd',
  recurring: { interval: 'month' },
  lookup_key: 'know24_scout_monthly',
})

// Metered overage pricing (AI tokens beyond ceiling)
const overagePrice = await stripe.prices.create({
  product: baseProduct.id,
  unit_amount: 2,          // $0.002 per 1k tokens (example)
  currency: 'usd',
  recurring: {
    interval: 'month',
    usage_type: 'metered',
    aggregate_usage: 'sum',
  },
  lookup_key: 'know24_ai_overage',
})
```

---

### Subscription Flow

```
Creator signs up
  → Clerk creates user
  → Clerk webhook → create users + organization records
  → Creator completes setup flow
  → POST /api/stripe/subscription/create
    → stripe.customers.create({ email, metadata: { org_id } })
    → stripe.subscriptions.create({
        customer: stripe_customer_id,
        items: [{ price: KNOW24_BASE_PRICE_ID }],
        metadata: { org_id, business_id }
      })
    → UPDATE organizations SET stripe_subscription_id, subscription_status = 'active'
  → Webhook: customer.subscription.created
    → Activate organization
```

#### Know24 Subscription API Routes

```typescript
// POST /api/stripe/subscription/create
// Body: { organization_id, price_id }
// → Create Stripe customer if not exists
// → Create subscription
// → Return { subscription_id, client_secret (for payment) }

// POST /api/stripe/subscription/add-scout
// Body: { organization_id }
// → Add scout price to existing subscription
// → UPDATE organizations SET scout_enabled = true, scout_stripe_subscription_id

// POST /api/stripe/subscription/cancel
// Body: { organization_id, at_period_end: boolean }
// → stripe.subscriptions.update({ cancel_at_period_end: true })
// → UPDATE organizations SET subscription_status = 'canceled'

// GET /api/stripe/subscription/portal
// Body: { organization_id }
// → stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({ customer, return_url })
// → Return { portal_url } (redirect creator to Stripe's self-serve portal)
```

---

### Stripe Connect — Creator Storefront Payments

```typescript
// POST /api/stripe/connect/onboard
// Creates Express account for creator, returns onboarding link

const account = await stripe.accounts.create({
  type: 'express',
  country: 'US',
  capabilities: {
    card_payments: { requested: true },
    transfers: { requested: true },
  },
  metadata: { business_id: params.business_id }
})

// UPDATE businesses SET stripe_account_id = account.id

const accountLink = await stripe.accountLinks.create({
  account: account.id,
  refresh_url: `${BASE_URL}/dashboard?stripe_refresh=true`,
  return_url: `${BASE_URL}/dashboard?stripe_success=true`,
  type: 'account_onboarding',
})

return { onboarding_url: accountLink.url }
```

#### Checkout Session with Connect

```typescript
// POST /api/storefront/[subdomain]/checkout
const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({
  mode: 'payment',  // or 'subscription' for chatbot
  payment_intent_data: {
    application_fee_amount: calculatePlatformFee(price_cents), // Know24's cut (0% for now, configurable)
    transfer_data: {
      destination: business.stripe_account_id,  // creator's Express account
    },
  },
  line_items: [{
    price_data: {
      currency: 'usd',
      product_data: { name: product.title },
      unit_amount: product.price_cents,
    },
    quantity: 1,
  }],
  customer_email: body.customer_email,
  success_url: body.success_url + '?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}',
  cancel_url: body.cancel_url,
  metadata: {
    business_id: business.id,
    product_id: product.id,
    customer_email: body.customer_email,
    referral_code: body.referral_code ?? '',
  },
})
```

---

### Metered AI Billing

```typescript
// lib/billing/track-usage.ts
export async function trackAIUsage(
  businessId: string,
  feature: AIUsageFeature,
  tokenCount: number
) {
  const now = new Date()
  const year = now.getFullYear()
  const month = now.getMonth() + 1

  // Upsert monthly usage record
  await supabase.rpc('increment_ai_usage', {
    p_business_id: businessId,
    p_feature: feature,
    p_tokens: tokenCount,
    p_year: year,
    p_month: month,
  })

  // Check if ceiling breached → report to Stripe metered price
  const org = await getOrganizationForBusiness(businessId)
  if (org.ai_tokens_used_this_month > org.ai_tokens_ceiling) {
    const overageTokens = org.ai_tokens_used_this_month - org.ai_tokens_ceiling
    await stripe.subscriptionItems.createUsageRecord(
      org.stripe_metered_item_id,
      {
        quantity: Math.ceil(overageTokens / 1000),  // report per 1k tokens
        timestamp: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),
        action: 'set',
      }
    )
  }
}
```

```sql
-- Stored procedure for atomic usage increment
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION increment_ai_usage(
  p_business_id UUID,
  p_feature     TEXT,
  p_tokens      INT,
  p_year        INT,
  p_month       INT
)
RETURNS VOID LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER AS $$
DECLARE
  v_org_id UUID;
BEGIN
  SELECT organization_id INTO v_org_id FROM public.businesses WHERE id = p_business_id;

  INSERT INTO public.ai_usage (business_id, organization_id, period_year, period_month)
  VALUES (p_business_id, v_org_id, p_year, p_month)
  ON CONFLICT (business_id, period_year, period_month) DO NOTHING;

  EXECUTE format(
    'UPDATE public.ai_usage SET tokens_%s = tokens_%s + $1, updated_at = NOW()
     WHERE business_id = $2 AND period_year = $3 AND period_month = $4',
    p_feature, p_feature
  ) USING p_tokens, p_business_id, p_year, p_month;

  -- Also update org-level counter
  UPDATE public.organizations
  SET ai_tokens_used_this_month = ai_tokens_used_this_month + p_tokens
  WHERE id = v_org_id;
END;
$$;
```

### Webhook Event Handler Map

```typescript
// app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts

const HANDLERS: Record<string, (event: Stripe.Event) => Promise<void>> = {
  'checkout.session.completed':          handleCheckoutCompleted,
  'customer.subscription.created':       handleSubscriptionCreated,
  'customer.subscription.updated':       handleSubscriptionUpdated,
  'customer.subscription.deleted':       handleSubscriptionDeleted,
  'invoice.payment_succeeded':           handleInvoicePaid,
  'invoice.payment_failed':              handleInvoiceFailed,
  'payment_intent.payment_failed':       handlePaymentFailed,
  'charge.dispute.created':              handleDisputeCreated,
  'charge.refunded':                     handleRefunded,
  'transfer.created':                    handleTransferCreated,
  // Connect account events (via separate webhook endpoint):
  'account.updated':                     handleAccountUpdated,
  'payout.paid':                         handlePayoutPaid,
}
```

---

### Refund & Dispute Handling

```typescript
// POST /api/businesses/[id]/orders/[orderId]/refund

async function handleRefund(orderId: string, amountCents?: number, reason?: string) {
  const order = await getOrder(orderId)

  // Issue refund via Stripe Connect
  const refund = await stripe.refunds.create({
    payment_intent: order.stripe_payment_intent_id,
    amount: amountCents,  // undefined = full refund
    reason: mapRefundReason(reason),  // 'duplicate' | 'fraudulent' | 'requested_by_customer'
    reverse_transfer: true,  // also reverses the transfer to creator's account
    refund_application_fee: false,  // Know24 absorbs platform fee on refunds
  })

  // Revoke product access
  await revokeProductAccess(order.id)

  // Update database
  await supabase.from('orders').update({
    status: 'refunded',
    refunded_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    refund_reason: reason,
  }).eq('id', orderId)

  // Log event
  await logActivity(order.business_id, 'sale', `Refund issued: ${formatCents(amountCents ?? order.total_cents)}`)
}
```

---

### Revenue Reporting

```typescript
// GET /api/businesses/[id]/analytics/revenue
// Queries orders table directly (source of truth is Supabase, reconciled with Stripe)

// Monthly revenue breakdown query:
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('orders')
  .select('total_cents, created_at, status')
  .eq('business_id', businessId)
  .eq('status', 'completed')
  .gte('created_at', startDate)
  .lte('created_at', endDate)

// For Stripe reconciliation (audits): use stripe.balanceTransactions.list()
// Cross-reference with orders by payment_intent_id
```

## 2.5 Data Migration Strategy

### File Structure

```
supabase/
├── migrations/
│   ├── 20260101000001_extensions_and_types.sql
│   ├── 20260101000002_users.sql
│   ├── 20260101000003_organizations.sql
│   ├── 20260101000004_organization_members.sql
│   ├── 20260101000005_businesses.sql
│   ├── 20260101000006_knowledge_items.sql
│   ├── 20260101000007_knowledge_chunks.sql
│   ├── 20260101000008_product_types.sql
│   ├── 20260101000009_products.sql
│   ├── 20260101000010_storefronts.sql
│   ├── 20260101000011_storefront_testimonials.sql
│   ├── 20260101000012_blog_posts.sql
│   ├── 20260101000013_customers.sql

│   ├── 20260101000016_subscriptions.sql
│   ├── 20260101000017_email_subscribers.sql

│   ├── 20260101000019_email_sequence_steps.sql
│   ├── 20260101000020_social_posts.sql
│   ├── 20260101000021_scout_scans.sql
│   ├── 20260101000022_scout_opportunities.sql
│   ├── 20260101000023_referrals.sql
│   ├── 20260101000024_ai_usage.sql
│   ├── 20260101000025_ai_workspace_messages.sql
│   ├── 20260101000026_support_tickets.sql
│   ├── 20260101000027_activity_log.sql
│   ├── 20260101000028_expert_engine_api_keys.sql
│   ├── 20260101000029_product_reviews.sql
│   ├── 20260101000030_helper_functions.sql
│   ├── 20260101000031_rls_policies.sql          ← consolidated RLS
│   └── 20260101000032_seed_product_types.sql
├── seed/
│   ├── dev_seed.sql                             ← development test data
│   └── stripe_products.ts                       ← Stripe product seeding script
└── config.toml
```

### Running Migrations

```bash
# Install Supabase CLI
npm install -g supabase

# Link to your project
supabase link --project-ref <your-project-ref>

# Push all migrations
supabase db push

# Or run locally with Docker
supabase start
supabase db reset  # wipes + re-runs all migrations + seeds

# Generate TypeScript types from schema
supabase gen types typescript --linked > types/supabase.ts
```

---

### Rollback Procedures

```bash
# Rollback to a specific migration
supabase db reset --version 20260101000010   # reverts everything after this point

# Manual rollback for a specific migration (use DOWN section in each file)
# Each migration file should have an undo section:

-- ====== UP ======
CREATE TABLE public.social_posts ( ... );

-- ====== DOWN ======
-- To rollback: psql -f rollback_20260101000020.sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS public.social_posts CASCADE;
```

#### Rollback Strategy for Production

```
1. Zero-downtime rollback:
   - All schema changes must be backward-compatible for at least one deploy cycle
   - Never DROP columns directly — first add new, deploy, migrate data, then drop in next cycle
   - Use Supabase's migration locking to prevent concurrent migration runs

2. Data rollback:
   - Supabase Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) enabled on paid plans
   - Before any destructive migration: pg_dump backup to R2
   - Keep migration history in Git; tag releases with migration version

3. Rollback decision tree:
   - Bug in migration (no data written): revert migration file, run supabase db push
   - Bug after data written: write compensating migration (don't modify existing ones)
   - Catastrophic failure: restore from PITR or pg_dump backup

4. Staging environment:
   - Always run migrations on staging first: supabase db push --db-url $STAGING_DB_URL
   - Run integration tests against staging before promoting to production
```

## 3.1 AI Architecture Overview

### Model Strategy

Know24 uses Vercel AI SDK as the unified orchestration layer, routing requests to OpenAI and Anthropic based on task type, latency requirement, and cost. Google Gemini is available as a third provider for fallback. All provider credentials are stored in environment variables and never exposed client-side.

```
OPENAI_API_KEY=...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...
GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY=...
VOYAGE_API_KEY=...
```

#### Model Tier Assignments

| Tier | Provider / Model | Context Window | Use Cases |
|------|-----------------|----------------|-----------|
| **Heavy** | `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` | 200k | Knowledge analysis, full product generation, Scout scans |
| **Heavy (alt)** | `openai/gpt-4o` | 128k | Heavy fallback, image-capable tasks |
| **Light** | `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5` | 200k | Chat responses, short content, intent classification |
| **Light (alt)** | `openai/gpt-4o-mini` | 128k | Light fallback, embeddings preprocessing |
| **Embeddings** | `openai/text-embedding-3-small` | 8191 | All vector embeddings (primary) |
| **Embeddings (alt)** | `voyageai/voyage-3-lite` | 32k | Embedding fallback, longer documents |
| **Image** | `openai/dall-e-3` | — | Product covers, social media images |

#### Fallback Routing Logic

Implement provider fallback in `/lib/ai/router.ts`:

```typescript
import { createOpenAI } from '@ai-sdk/openai';
import { createAnthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic';
import { createGoogleGenerativeAI } from '@ai-sdk/google';

export type ModelTier = 'heavy' | 'light' | 'embedding';

const primaryModels: Record<ModelTier, string> = {
  heavy: 'claude-sonnet-4-5',
  light: 'claude-haiku-4-5',
  embedding: 'text-embedding-3-small',
};

const fallbackModels: Record<ModelTier, { provider: string; model: string }> = {
  heavy: { provider: 'openai', model: 'gpt-4o' },
  light: { provider: 'openai', model: 'gpt-4o-mini' },
  embedding: { provider: 'voyageai', model: 'voyage-3-lite' },
};

export async function getModel(tier: ModelTier) {
  try {
    const anthropic = createAnthropic();
    // Test connectivity — actual call gated on usage check
    return anthropic(primaryModels[tier]);
  } catch (err) {
    // Circuit breaker: on 3 consecutive failures within 60s, flip to fallback
    const fb = fallbackModels[tier];
    if (fb.provider === 'openai') {
      const openai = createOpenAI();
      return openai(fb.model);
    }
    throw new Error(`All providers failed for tier: ${tier}`);
  }
}
```

Circuit breaker state is tracked in Upstash Redis with a 60-second TTL. Key: `circuit:anthropic:failures`. If the integer exceeds 3, all requests route to OpenAI until the key expires.

**Token pricing basis (as of 2026):**
- Claude Haiku: $0.80/M input, $4.00/M output
- Claude Sonnet: $3.00/M input, $15.00/M output
- GPT-4o: $2.50/M input, $10.00/M output
