---

### Vercel AI SDK Integration Pattern

All AI calls in Know24 use Vercel AI SDK's `streamText`, `generateText`, and `generateObject` functions. Structured outputs use `generateObject` with Zod schemas to guarantee JSON shape.

```typescript
// /lib/ai/generate.ts
import { generateObject, streamText, generateText } from 'ai';
import { z } from 'zod';
import { getModel } from './router';

// Streaming response (chat, workspace)
export async function streamAIResponse(systemPrompt: string, userMessage: string) {
  const model = await getModel('light');
  return streamText({
    model,
    system: systemPrompt,
    prompt: userMessage,
    maxTokens: 1000,
    temperature: 0.7,
  });
}

// Structured output (product generation, analysis)
export async function generateStructuredOutput<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  systemPrompt: string,
  userPrompt: string,
  tier: 'heavy' | 'light' = 'heavy'
): Promise<T> {
  const model = await getModel(tier);
  const result = await generateObject({
    model,
    schema,
    system: systemPrompt,
    prompt: userPrompt,
    temperature: 0.3,
  });
  return result.object;
}
```

---

## 3.2 Knowledge Processing Pipeline

The knowledge pipeline is the core engine of Know24. It transforms raw input (URLs, documents, videos, AI interview transcripts) into a structured, queryable knowledge base that all product generation and content systems draw from.

### Architecture Overview

```
Input Sources
    │
    ▼
[Ingestion Workers] (Railway BullMQ)
    │
    ├── URL → Firecrawl scrape → clean text
    ├── Document → PDF/DOCX parser → clean text
    ├── Video → Whisper transcription → clean text
    └── AI Interview → structured Q&A → clean text
    │
    ▼
[Chunking Engine]
    │
    ├── Semantic chunking (primary)
    └── Fixed-size fallback
    │
    ▼
[Metadata Extraction]  ← Light model call per chunk batch
    │
    ▼
[Embedding Generation] ← text-embedding-3-small
    │
    ▼
[pgvector Storage]     ← Supabase PostgreSQL
    │
    ▼
[Knowledge Analysis]   ← Heavy model call
    │
    └── expertise_summary, sellable_topics[], product_recommendations[]
```

---

### 3.2.1 Ingestion — Four Input Paths

#### Path 1: URL Scraping

Use **Firecrawl** (`@mendable/firecrawl-js`) for clean markdown extraction from URLs. Firecrawl handles JavaScript-rendered pages, removes nav/footer boilerplate, and returns clean body text.

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/url-scraper.ts
import FirecrawlApp from '@mendable/firecrawl-js';

const firecrawl = new FirecrawlApp({ apiKey: process.env.FIRECRAWL_API_KEY! });

export interface ScrapeResult {
  url: string;
  title: string;
  content: string; // clean markdown
  wordCount: number;
  scrapedAt: Date;
}

export async function scrapeUrl(url: string): Promise<ScrapeResult> {
  const result = await firecrawl.scrapeUrl(url, {
    formats: ['markdown'],
    onlyMainContent: true,
    timeout: 30000,
  });

  if (!result.success) {
    throw new Error(`Scrape failed for ${url}: ${result.error}`);
  }

  const content = result.markdown ?? '';
  return {
    url,
    title: result.metadata?.title ?? 'Untitled',
    content,
    wordCount: content.split(/\s+/).length,
    scrapedAt: new Date(),
  };
}

// For multiple URLs (sitemap scraping during onboarding)
export async function scrapeMultipleUrls(urls: string[]): Promise<ScrapeResult[]> {
  // Rate limit: 5 concurrent scrapes max
  const results: ScrapeResult[] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < urls.length; i += 5) {
    const batch = urls.slice(i, i + 5);
    const batchResults = await Promise.allSettled(batch.map(scrapeUrl));
    for (const r of batchResults) {
      if (r.status === 'fulfilled') results.push(r.value);
    }
  }
  return results;
}
```

**Fallback**: If Firecrawl fails or times out, fall back to `cheerio`-based extraction (strips HTML tags, preserves paragraph structure).

#### Path 2: Document Upload (PDF, DOCX, TXT)

Files are uploaded to Cloudflare R2 first, then processed by a background worker.

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/document-parser.ts
import { PDFExtract } from 'pdf.js-extract';
import mammoth from 'mammoth';

export async function parseDocument(
  buffer: Buffer,
  mimeType: string
): Promise<string> {
  switch (mimeType) {
    case 'application/pdf': {
      const pdfExtract = new PDFExtract();
      const data = await pdfExtract.extractBuffer(buffer, {});
      return data.pages
        .map(p => p.content.map(c => c.str).join(' '))
        .join('\n\n');
    }
    case 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document': {
      const result = await mammoth.extractRawText({ buffer });
      return result.value;
    }
    case 'text/plain':
      return buffer.toString('utf-8');
    default:
      throw new Error(`Unsupported document type: ${mimeType}`);
  }
}
```

**Dependencies**: `pdf.js-extract`, `mammoth`
**Max file size**: 50MB per document, 200MB total per onboarding session
**Storage path in R2**: `uploads/{businessId}/{timestamp}/{filename}`

#### Path 3: Video Link → Whisper Transcription

Accepts YouTube URLs, Loom URLs, Vimeo URLs, or direct video file uploads (.mp4, .mov, .webm).

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/video-transcriber.ts
import ytdl from '@distube/ytdl-core';
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { Readable } from 'stream';

const openai = new OpenAI();

export async function transcribeVideoUrl(url: string): Promise<string> {
  // Step 1: Download audio stream
  const audioBuffer = await downloadAudio(url);

  // Step 2: Transcribe with Whisper
  const file = new File([audioBuffer], 'audio.mp3', { type: 'audio/mpeg' });
  const transcription = await openai.audio.transcriptions.create({
    file,
    model: 'whisper-1',
    response_format: 'text',
    language: 'en',
  });

  return transcription;
}

async function downloadAudio(url: string): Promise<Buffer> {
  if (url.includes('youtube.com') || url.includes('youtu.be')) {
    return downloadYouTubeAudio(url);
  }
  // For Loom/Vimeo/direct: use yt-dlp via shell command
  return downloadWithYtDlp(url);
}

async function downloadYouTubeAudio(url: string): Promise<Buffer> {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
    const stream = ytdl(url, { quality: 'lowestaudio', filter: 'audioonly' });
    stream.on('data', chunk => chunks.push(chunk));
    stream.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));
    stream.on('error', reject);
  });
}
```

**Whisper cost**: ~$0.006/minute of audio. A 60-minute video costs ~$0.36.
**Max video length**: 3 hours
**Fallback**: If video download fails, prompt user to paste transcript manually.

#### Path 4: AI Interview

The AI Interview is a guided conversational intake conducted in the Setup My Business flow. It uses streaming responses and stores the complete Q&A transcript.

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/ai-interview.ts
// Interview orchestrator — manages question flow

export const INTERVIEW_QUESTION_BANK = [
  "Tell me about your professional background. What's your area of expertise and how long have you been working in it?",
  "What are the most common problems or challenges your clients/colleagues come to you for help with?",
  "Walk me through a specific transformation you've helped someone achieve. What did they struggle with before, and what was the outcome?",
  "What do most people get wrong about your field that you have strong opinions on?",
  "What's the #1 thing you wish you had known when you were just starting out?",
  "What frameworks, systems, or mental models do you use that others in your field don't?",
  "If you were training someone to do what you do, what would be on your curriculum?",
  "What tools, resources, or references do you recommend most often?",
  "Who is the ideal person you can help most? Describe them specifically.",
  "What results can someone realistically expect if they apply your knowledge correctly?",
];

export interface InterviewMessage {
  role: 'assistant' | 'user';
  content: string;
  timestamp: Date;
}

// The interview runs as a streaming chat — questions are pre-planned
// but the AI adapts follow-ups based on responses
export const INTERVIEW_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are an expert knowledge extractor for Know24. Your job is to conduct a structured interview to capture a subject matter expert's knowledge in a format suitable for creating digital products.

You have a set of core questions to cover, but you MUST:
1. Ask ONE question at a time
2. Follow up on interesting details before moving to the next question
3. Probe for specific examples, frameworks, and transferable insights
4. Keep responses warm, encouraging, and conversational
5. After 8-12 exchanges, wrap up with: "Thank you — I have a rich picture of your expertise. Shall we move on to analyzing what's sellable?"

Core questions to weave into the conversation naturally:
${INTERVIEW_QUESTION_BANK.map((q, i) => `${i + 1}. ${q}`).join('\n')}

Start with: "Let's capture your expertise. I'll ask you a series of questions — just answer naturally. There are no wrong answers. To start: tell me about your professional background and what you're the go-to person for."`;
```

The full interview transcript is stored in the `knowledge_sources` table as a single document of type `ai_interview`.

---

### 3.2.2 Chunking Strategy

**Primary method: Semantic chunking** using `langchain/text_splitter` with `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` configured for semantic boundaries (paragraph → sentence → character fallback).

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/chunker.ts
import { RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter } from 'langchain/text_splitter';

export interface Chunk {
  content: string;
  tokenCount: number;
  charStart: number;
  charEnd: number;
  chunkIndex: number;
  sourceId: string;
}

const splitter = new RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter({
  chunkSize: 512,       // target tokens per chunk
  chunkOverlap: 64,     // 64-token overlap for context continuity
  separators: [
    '\n\n',   // paragraph break (highest priority)
    '\n',     // line break
    '. ',     // sentence end
    '? ',     // question end
    '! ',     // exclamation end
    ', ',     // clause break
    ' ',      // word break (fallback)
    '',       // character break (last resort)
  ],
  lengthFunction: approximateTokenCount,
});

function approximateTokenCount(text: string): number {
  // ~4 characters per token for English text
  return Math.ceil(text.length / 4);
}

export async function chunkDocument(
  text: string,
  sourceId: string
): Promise<Chunk[]> {
  const rawChunks = await splitter.splitText(text);
  let charOffset = 0;

  return rawChunks.map((content, index) => {
    const start = text.indexOf(content, charOffset);
    const end = start + content.length;
    charOffset = start + 1; // advance past current position

    return {
      content: content.trim(),
      tokenCount: approximateTokenCount(content),
      charStart: start,
      charEnd: end,
      chunkIndex: index,
      sourceId,
    };
  });
}
```

**Chunking parameters rationale:**
- **512 tokens**: Optimal for `text-embedding-3-small` (max 8191, but smaller chunks have higher retrieval precision)
- **64-token overlap**: Prevents context loss at chunk boundaries; critical for multi-part concepts
- **Semantic separators**: Preserves sentence integrity, avoids mid-sentence splits

**Special handling by content type:**
- **Interview transcripts**: Split on speaker turns first, then sub-chunk long responses
- **PDFs with tables**: Detect table-like formatting (pipe characters, aligned spaces) and keep tables as single chunks
- **Code/templates**: Keep code blocks intact; never split inside a code fence

---

### 3.2.3 Metadata Extraction Per Chunk

After chunking, a batch call to the light model extracts structured metadata for each chunk. This metadata is stored alongside embeddings and used for RAG filtering (e.g., "only retrieve chunks about pricing frameworks").

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/metadata-extractor.ts

export interface ChunkMetadata {
  topic: string;          // e.g., "team management"
  subtopics: string[];    // e.g., ["delegation", "conflict resolution"]
  keyConcepts: string[];  // e.g., ["RACI matrix", "psychological safety"]
  contentType: 'framework' | 'example' | 'how-to' | 'definition' | 'opinion' | 'resource';
  expertiseLevel: 'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced';
  actionable: boolean;    // does this chunk contain actionable advice?
  quotable: boolean;      // is this a strong, citable statement?
}

// Process in batches of 20 chunks per API call (prompt includes all 20)
export async function extractChunkMetadata(
  chunks: Chunk[],
  expertiseDomain: string
): Promise<ChunkMetadata[]> {
  // Batch into groups of 20
  const batchSize = 20;
  const results: ChunkMetadata[] = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < chunks.length; i += batchSize) {
    const batch = chunks.slice(i, i + batchSize);
    const batchResults = await extractBatch(batch, expertiseDomain);
    results.push(...batchResults);
  }

  return results;
}
```

The metadata extraction system prompt formats 20 chunks as a numbered list and requests a JSON array of metadata objects, reducing API calls by 20x versus per-chunk extraction.

---

### 3.2.4 Embedding & pgvector Storage

#### Database Schema

```sql
-- /supabase/migrations/003_knowledge_embeddings.sql

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

-- Knowledge sources (raw ingestion records)
CREATE TABLE knowledge_sources (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  source_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (source_type IN ('url', 'document', 'video', 'ai_interview')),
  source_url TEXT,
  original_filename TEXT,
  raw_content TEXT NOT NULL,
  word_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  processing_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
    CHECK (processing_status IN ('pending', 'processing', 'complete', 'failed')),
  error_message TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

-- Chunked knowledge with embeddings
CREATE TABLE knowledge_chunks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES knowledge_sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  token_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  chunk_index INTEGER NOT NULL,
  -- Metadata
  topic TEXT,
  subtopics TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  key_concepts TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  content_type TEXT,
  expertise_level TEXT,
  actionable BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  quotable BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  -- Embedding
  embedding vector(1536),  -- text-embedding-3-small dimensions
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- HNSW index for fast approximate nearest-neighbor search
CREATE INDEX knowledge_chunks_embedding_hnsw_idx
  ON knowledge_chunks
  USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

-- Index for business-scoped queries (always filter by business_id first)
CREATE INDEX knowledge_chunks_business_id_idx
  ON knowledge_chunks (business_id);

-- Composite for topic filtering
CREATE INDEX knowledge_chunks_business_topic_idx
  ON knowledge_chunks (business_id, topic);
```

**Index choice rationale:**
- **HNSW over IVFFlat**: HNSW provides better recall at query time and doesn't require a training step. For business-scoped knowledge bases (typically 500–5,000 chunks per business), HNSW is optimal.
- **Parameters**: `m=16` (16 connections per node, balances speed/accuracy), `ef_construction=64` (build-time quality)
- **Query parameter**: Use `SET hnsw.ef_search = 40` at query time for higher recall when generating products (lower values for chatbot real-time responses)

#### Embedding Generation

```typescript
// /lib/ingestion/embedder.ts
import OpenAI from 'openai';

const openai = new OpenAI();

export async function generateEmbeddings(texts: string[]): Promise<number[][]> {
  // text-embedding-3-small accepts up to 2048 inputs per request
  const batchSize = 100;
  const allEmbeddings: number[][] = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < texts.length; i += batchSize) {
    const batch = texts.slice(i, i + batchSize);
    const response = await openai.embeddings.create({
      model: 'text-embedding-3-small',
      input: batch,
      dimensions: 1536, // explicitly set; default is 1536
    });
    allEmbeddings.push(...response.data.map(d => d.embedding));
  }

  return allEmbeddings;
}

export async function embedAndStore(
  chunks: Chunk[],
  metadata: ChunkMetadata[],
  businessId: string,
  sourceId: string,
  supabase: SupabaseClient
): Promise<void> {
  const texts = chunks.map(c => c.content);
  const embeddings = await generateEmbeddings(texts);

  const rows = chunks.map((chunk, i) => ({
    business_id: businessId,
    source_id: sourceId,
    content: chunk.content,
    token_count: chunk.tokenCount,
    chunk_index: chunk.chunkIndex,
    topic: metadata[i].topic,
    subtopics: metadata[i].subtopics,
    key_concepts: metadata[i].keyConcepts,
    content_type: metadata[i].contentType,
    expertise_level: metadata[i].expertiseLevel,
    actionable: metadata[i].actionable,
    quotable: metadata[i].quotable,
    embedding: `[${embeddings[i].join(',')}]`,
  }));

  // Batch insert in groups of 100
  for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 100) {
    await supabase
      .from('knowledge_chunks')
      .insert(rows.slice(i, i + 100));
  }
}
```

#### Similarity Search Implementation

```typescript
// /lib/knowledge/retriever.ts

export interface RetrievalOptions {
  businessId: string;
  query: string;
  topK?: number;               // default: 8
  similarityThreshold?: number; // default: 0.70
  filterTopic?: string;        // optional: narrow to specific topic
  filterContentType?: string;  // optional: 'framework' | 'how-to' | etc.
  efSearch?: number;           // HNSW ef_search parameter, default: 40
}

export interface RetrievedChunk {
  id: string;
  content: string;
  similarity: number;
  topic: string;
  contentType: string;
  actionable: boolean;
}

export async function retrieveRelevantChunks(
  options: RetrievalOptions,
  supabase: SupabaseClient
): Promise<RetrievedChunk[]> {
  const {
    businessId,
    query,
    topK = 8,
    similarityThreshold = 0.70,
    filterTopic,
    efSearch = 40,
  } = options;

  // Embed the query
  const [queryEmbedding] = await generateEmbeddings([query]);

  // Use Supabase RPC for vector similarity search
  const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('match_knowledge_chunks', {
    p_business_id: businessId,
    p_query_embedding: queryEmbedding,
    p_match_threshold: similarityThreshold,
    p_match_count: topK,
    p_filter_topic: filterTopic ?? null,
    p_ef_search: efSearch,
  });

  if (error) throw error;
  return data as RetrievedChunk[];
}
```

```sql
-- Supabase RPC function
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION match_knowledge_chunks(
  p_business_id UUID,
  p_query_embedding vector(1536),
  p_match_threshold FLOAT,
  p_match_count INT,
  p_filter_topic TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  p_ef_search INT DEFAULT 40
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id UUID,
  content TEXT,
  similarity FLOAT,
  topic TEXT,
  content_type TEXT,
  actionable BOOLEAN
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Set HNSW ef_search for this query
  EXECUTE format('SET LOCAL hnsw.ef_search = %s', p_ef_search);

  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    kc.id,
    kc.content,
    1 - (kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding) AS similarity,
    kc.topic,
    kc.content_type,
    kc.actionable
  FROM knowledge_chunks kc
  WHERE
    kc.business_id = p_business_id
    AND (p_filter_topic IS NULL OR kc.topic = p_filter_topic)
    AND 1 - (kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding) > p_match_threshold
  ORDER BY kc.embedding <=> p_query_embedding
  LIMIT p_match_count;
END;
$$;
```

---

### 3.2.5 Knowledge Analysis

After all sources are processed and embedded, a single heavy-model call synthesizes everything into an `expertise_profile` stored on the business record.

```typescript
// /lib/knowledge/analyzer.ts

export interface ExpertiseProfile {
  summary: string;           // 2-3 sentence summary of expertise
  domains: string[];         // primary knowledge domains (e.g., ["nurse leadership", "ICU management"])
  coreConcepts: string[];    // most represented concepts across all chunks
  uniqueInsights: string[];  // distinctive angles the creator holds
  targetAudience: string;    // who benefits most from this expertise
  confidenceScore: number;   // 0.0–1.0: how complete the knowledge base is
  sellableTopics: SellableTopic[];
  productRecommendations: ProductRecommendation[];
}

export interface SellableTopic {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  targetProblem: string;
  targetAudience: string;
  chunkIds: string[];        // which knowledge chunks support this topic
}

export interface ProductRecommendation {
  productType: ProductType;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  suggestedPrice: number;
  pricingModel: 'one_time' | 'subscription' | 'free';
  rationale: string;
  confidence: number;        // 0.0–1.0
  topicRef: string;          // which sellable topic this draws from
}

export async function analyzeKnowledge(
  businessId: string,
  supabase: SupabaseClient
): Promise<ExpertiseProfile> {
  // Retrieve all chunks for business (or top 150 by content quality)
  const { data: chunks } = await supabase
    .from('knowledge_chunks')
    .select('content, topic, key_concepts, actionable, quotable')
    .eq('business_id', businessId)
    .order('created_at', { ascending: true })
    .limit(150);

  // Assemble context from representative chunks
  const contextText = chunks
    .map(c => `[${c.topic}] ${c.content}`)
    .join('\n\n---\n\n');

  const profile = await generateStructuredOutput(
    ExpertiseProfileSchema,
    KNOWLEDGE_ANALYSIS_PROMPT, // see Section 3.8
    `Analyze the following expert knowledge base and generate a complete expertise profile:\n\n${contextText}`,
    'heavy'
  );

  // Persist to businesses table
  await supabase
    .from('businesses')
    .update({ expertise_profile: profile })
    .eq('id', businessId);

  return profile;
}
```

---

## 3.3 Product Generation System

Each of the 10 digital product types + chatbot follows a common generation pattern:

1. **RAG retrieval**: Query knowledge base with product-specific queries
2. **Context assembly**: Format retrieved chunks into a coherent context block
3. **Generation**: Call AI model with product-specific prompt + context
4. **Structured output**: Parse into typed schema
5. **Asset creation**: Generate cover image, render PDF if needed
6. **Storage**: Save to R2, create product record in database

### 3.3.1 Common RAG Assembly

```typescript
// /lib/products/context-assembler.ts

export async function assembleProductContext(
  businessId: string,
  queries: string[],  // multiple angles to query
  maxChunks: number = 20
): Promise<string> {
  // Run all queries in parallel
  const chunkSets = await Promise.all(
    queries.map(q =>
      retrieveRelevantChunks({
        businessId,
        query: q,
        topK: Math.ceil(maxChunks / queries.length),
        similarityThreshold: 0.65,
        efSearch: 60, // higher recall for product generation
      }, supabase)
    )
  );

  // Deduplicate by chunk ID, keep highest similarity
  const chunkMap = new Map<string, RetrievedChunk>();
  for (const chunks of chunkSets) {
    for (const chunk of chunks) {
      const existing = chunkMap.get(chunk.id);
      if (!existing || chunk.similarity > existing.similarity) {
        chunkMap.set(chunk.id, chunk);
      }
    }
  }

  // Sort by similarity descending, take top maxChunks
  const sorted = [...chunkMap.values()]
    .sort((a, b) => b.similarity - a.similarity)
    .slice(0, maxChunks);

  return sorted
    .map(c => `### ${c.topic} (${c.contentType})\n${c.content}`)
    .join('\n\n');
}
```

---

### 3.3.2 Guide / eBook Generation

The flagship product type. Generates a structured, multi-chapter guide as markdown, then renders to a PDF cover + PDF body.

**Queries for RAG**: `["main frameworks and methodologies", "step-by-step processes", "common mistakes and how to avoid them", "practical examples and case studies", "tools and resources"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const GuideSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  subtitle: z.string(),
  tagline: z.string(),           // one punchy sentence
  tableOfContents: z.array(z.object({
    chapterNumber: z.number(),
    chapterTitle: z.string(),
    summary: z.string(),
  })),
  introduction: z.string(),      // 300–500 words
  chapters: z.array(z.object({
    chapterNumber: z.number(),
    title: z.string(),
    content: z.string(),         // 800–1200 words of markdown
    keyTakeaways: z.array(z.string()),
  })),
  conclusion: z.string(),        // 200–300 words
  aboutAuthor: z.string(),       // 100 words
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
  coverImagePrompt: z.string(),  // DALL-E prompt for cover
});
```

**Generation approach**: Generate chapter by chapter in parallel (Chapters 1–3 simultaneously, then 4–6) to stay within max output tokens. Each chapter generation is a separate API call with shared context.

**PDF Rendering**: Use Puppeteer (via a dedicated Railway worker, not Vercel serverless) to render HTML → PDF. Template is an Nunjucks HTML template with the creator's brand colors injected.

```typescript
// /workers/pdf-renderer.ts
import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';
import nunjucks from 'nunjucks';

export async function renderGuidePDF(
  guide: Guide,
  brandColors: BrandColors
): Promise<Buffer> {
  const html = nunjucks.render('templates/guide.njk', {
    guide,
    colors: brandColors,
    generatedAt: new Date().toLocaleDateString(),
  });

  const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ args: ['--no-sandbox'] });
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  await page.setContent(html, { waitUntil: 'networkidle0' });
  const pdf = await page.pdf({
    format: 'A4',
    printBackground: true,
    margin: { top: '1in', right: '1in', bottom: '1in', left: '1in' },
  });
  await browser.close();
  return Buffer.from(pdf);
}
```

**Token budget**: ~8,000 input + ~8,000 output = ~$0.11–0.18 total.

---

### 3.3.3 Framework / Template Pack Generation

Generates fillable templates, checklists, and SOPs in structured markdown, rendered to a multi-page PDF.

**RAG queries**: `["processes and workflows", "checklists and step-by-step guides", "frameworks and models", "templates used in practice"]`

**Output schema**: An array of 5–10 templates, each with:
- `templateName`, `purpose`, `whenToUse`
- `template` (markdown table or checklist structure)
- `instructions` (how to fill it in)
- `example` (completed example)

**Special handling**: Tables are preserved as HTML tables in the PDF template for proper formatting.

---

### 3.3.4 Cheat Sheet / Quick Reference Generation

A 1–3 page dense reference. Light model is sufficient given the concise output.

**RAG queries**: `["most important concepts", "quick reference information", "rules of thumb and heuristics"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const CheatSheetSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  subtitle: z.string(),
  sections: z.array(z.object({
    heading: z.string(),
    items: z.array(z.object({
      label: z.string(),
      value: z.string(),
    })),
  })),
  quickRules: z.array(z.string()),   // "The 5 rules of..."
  dosDonts: z.object({
    dos: z.array(z.string()),
    donts: z.array(z.string()),
  }).optional(),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
  coverImagePrompt: z.string(),
});
```

**PDF template**: Single-column or two-column layout, dense typography, heavy use of color-coded sections. Max 3 pages.
**Model**: Light (Claude Haiku). Typical cost: ~$0.006.

---

### 3.3.5 Email Course (Drip) Generation

Generates a 5–7 email sequence, each email self-contained and actionable.

**RAG queries**: `["foundational concepts for beginners", "common mistakes and misconceptions", "transformation and outcomes", "advanced techniques", "tools and resources", "mindset and motivation"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const EmailCourseSchema = z.object({
  courseTitle: z.string(),
  courseDescription: z.string(),
  targetAudience: z.string(),
  emails: z.array(z.object({
    dayNumber: z.number(),         // 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 (or up to 7)
    subjectLine: z.string(),
    previewText: z.string(),       // 50-char email preview
    body: z.string(),              // 400–600 words, markdown
    ctaText: z.string(),
    ctaType: z.enum(['product_link', 'reply', 'exercise', 'resource']),
    homework: z.string().optional(), // optional action for reader
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
  dripSchedule: z.object({
    startDelay: z.number(),        // hours after signup
    intervalDays: z.number(),      // days between emails
  }),
});
```

**Drip schedule defaults**: Day 0 (immediate), Day 1, Day 3, Day 5, Day 7 (5-email version). Stored in the email platform integration (Resend transactional API or integrated ESP).

---

### 3.3.6 Assessment / Quiz Generation

Generates a self-evaluation tool with scored results and personalized recommendations.

**RAG queries**: `["skill levels and progression", "common challenges at each level", "diagnostic indicators of expertise", "recommendations based on situation"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const AssessmentSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  instructions: z.string(),
  questions: z.array(z.object({
    id: z.string(),
    questionText: z.string(),
    questionType: z.enum(['multiple_choice', 'likert_scale', 'yes_no']),
    options: z.array(z.object({
      label: z.string(),
      value: z.number(),           // numeric score contribution
      nextQuestion: z.string().optional(), // conditional branching
    })),
    weight: z.number(),            // 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = double weight
    category: z.string(),          // groups questions for sub-scores
  })),
  scoringRanges: z.array(z.object({
    label: z.string(),             // e.g., "New Leader", "Emerging Manager"
    minScore: z.number(),
    maxScore: z.number(),
    headline: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),       // 200-word personalized result
    recommendations: z.array(z.string()),
    productCta: z.string(),        // soft product recommendation
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

**Scoring system**: Sum of `option.value × question.weight` across all answers. Range 0–100 normalized.
**Minimum questions**: 10. Maximum: 25.
**Result delivery**: Rendered as a styled HTML page (served by Know24, gated by purchase) with score breakdown, category sub-scores, and product recommendations.

---

### 3.3.7 Prompt Pack / Toolkit Generation

Generates 20–40 curated AI prompts organized by workflow stage.

**RAG queries**: `["specific tasks and workflows", "common use cases", "prompts already used in practice", "AI tools relevant to domain"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const PromptPackSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  targetAITools: z.array(z.string()), // e.g., ["ChatGPT", "Claude", "Gemini"]
  categories: z.array(z.object({
    name: z.string(),
    prompts: z.array(z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      prompt: z.string(),        // the actual prompt text, with [placeholders]
      instructions: z.string(), // how to use it
      expectedOutput: z.string(), // what result to expect
      proTip: z.string().optional(),
    })),
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

**Prompt format**: All prompts include `[PLACEHOLDER]` tokens for personalization. The PDF renders with clearly visible placeholder highlighting.

---

### 3.3.8 Swipe File / Script Library Generation

A collection of copy-paste scripts for common high-stakes situations.

**RAG queries**: `["scripts and templated language", "difficult conversations", "common communication scenarios", "phrases and language patterns"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const SwipeFileSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  scripts: z.array(z.object({
    title: z.string(),           // e.g., "Addressing Performance Issues"
    situation: z.string(),       // when to use this script
    script: z.string(),          // the actual script text
    keyPhrases: z.array(z.string()), // extractable power phrases
    variants: z.array(z.object({
      label: z.string(),         // e.g., "More Formal", "Gentler Approach"
      script: z.string(),
    })).optional(),
    notes: z.string(),
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

---

### 3.3.9 Resource Directory Generation

A curated, annotated list of tools, resources, and references.

**RAG queries**: `["tools and software mentioned", "resources and references", "recommended reading", "websites and communities"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const ResourceDirectorySchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  categories: z.array(z.object({
    name: z.string(),
    resources: z.array(z.object({
      name: z.string(),
      url: z.string().optional(),
      type: z.enum(['tool', 'book', 'website', 'community', 'podcast', 'course', 'template']),
      description: z.string(),  // 50–100 words
      whyRecommended: z.string(),
      pricingNote: z.string().optional(), // "Free", "$29/mo", etc.
      difficulty: z.enum(['beginner', 'intermediate', 'advanced']).optional(),
    })),
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

**Note on URLs**: The AI will generate plausible resource names and descriptions. URLs should be validated with a HEAD request after generation; broken links are flagged for manual review before product goes live.

---

### 3.3.10 Worksheet / Workbook Generation

Interactive exercises with guided reflection questions and fill-in sections.

**RAG queries**: `["exercises and activities", "reflection questions", "self-assessment prompts", "action planning frameworks"]`

**Output schema**:
```typescript
const WorkbookSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  modules: z.array(z.object({
    moduleNumber: z.number(),
    title: z.string(),
    introduction: z.string(),
    exercises: z.array(z.object({
      exerciseNumber: z.number(),
      title: z.string(),
      instructions: z.string(),
      workingSpace: z.string(),   // prompt for the fill-in area, e.g., "List 5 situations where..."
      lines: z.number(),          // how many blank lines to render in PDF (4–20)
      reflection: z.string().optional(), // follow-up reflection question
    })),
    moduleReflection: z.string().optional(),
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

**PDF rendering**: Uses a special "workbook" Puppeteer template that renders fill-in areas as dotted-line boxes. Buyers fill it in digitally (PDF form fields) or print it.

---

### 3.3.11 Mini-Course (Text + Slides) Generation

A multi-module structured course with lesson content and presentation slides.

**RAG queries**: `["curriculum and learning progression", "module topics", "key concepts to teach", "examples and demonstrations", "assessments and exercises"]`

This is the most complex product type. Generation happens in stages:

**Stage 1** (Heavy model): Generate course outline and module summaries
**Stage 2** (Heavy model, parallel): Generate full lesson content per module (3–5 modules)
**Stage 3** (Light model, parallel): Generate slide decks per module (10–15 slides each)
**Stage 4** (Puppeteer): Render slides as PDF

```typescript
const MiniCourseSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string(),
  description: z.string(),
  targetAudience: z.string(),
  learningObjectives: z.array(z.string()),
  totalModules: z.number(),
  modules: z.array(z.object({
    moduleNumber: z.number(),
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),
    estimatedTime: z.string(),   // "15 minutes"
    lessonContent: z.string(),   // full markdown lesson, 800–1200 words
    keyTakeaways: z.array(z.string()),
    slides: z.array(z.object({
      slideNumber: z.number(),
      slideType: z.enum(['title', 'content', 'quote', 'visual', 'exercise', 'summary']),
      heading: z.string(),
      bullets: z.array(z.string()),
      speakerNotes: z.string(),
    })),
    quiz: z.array(z.object({
      question: z.string(),
      options: z.array(z.string()),
      correctIndex: z.number(),
      explanation: z.string(),
    })).optional(),
  })),
  suggestedPrice: z.number(),
});
```

---

### 3.3.12 Chatbot Configuration

The chatbot is not a generated document — it's a live RAG-based conversational system. Configuration involves:

1. **Knowledge scoping**: Creator selects which topics/chunks to include (defaults to all)
2. **Personality configuration**: Tone, formality level, persona name
3. **Product recommendation logic**: Which products to suggest and in what context
4. **Access control**: Subscription-gated; token budget per conversation

```typescript
// /lib/products/chatbot-config.ts

export interface ChatbotConfig {
  businessId: string;
  productId: string;
  name: string;               // e.g., "Nurse Leadership AI Coach"
  persona: string;            // personality description
  tone: 'professional' | 'friendly' | 'coaching' | 'educational';
  includedTopics: string[];   // empty = all topics
  excludedChunkIds: string[]; // specific chunks to exclude
  productRecommendations: {
    productId: string;
    triggerTopics: string[];  // recommend this product when these topics arise
    ctaMessage: string;
  }[];
  tokenBudgetPerConversation: number; // default: 8000
  conversationHistoryLimit: number;   // default: 20 messages
  systemPromptOverride?: string;      // creator custom instructions
}

// Runtime system prompt built from config
export function buildChatbotSystemPrompt(
  config: ChatbotConfig,
  expertiseProfile: ExpertiseProfile
): string {
  return `${CHATBOT_BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT}

## About You
You are ${config.name}, an AI assistant powered by ${expertiseProfile.targetAudience}-focused expertise.
${config.persona}

## Your Knowledge
You are an expert in: ${expertiseProfile.domains.join(', ')}.
Your tone is ${config.tone}.

## Product Recommendations
When the conversation touches on the following topics, naturally mention the relevant product:
${config.productRecommendations
  .map(r => `- Topics: ${r.triggerTopics.join(', ')} → "${r.ctaMessage}"`)
  .join('\n')}

## Boundaries
- Only answer questions within your expertise domain
- If asked about something outside your knowledge, say: "That's outside my area of expertise, but I can help you with [redirect to relevant topic]"
- Never make medical, legal, or financial claims you cannot support
${config.systemPromptOverride ? `\n## Additional Instructions\n${config.systemPromptOverride}` : ''}`;
}
```

**Conversation history management**:
- Store last `conversationHistoryLimit` (default 20) messages
- When approaching `tokenBudgetPerConversation`, summarize older history using light model
- Token counting uses `tiktoken` (`cl100k_base` encoding)
- Per-conversation token tracking stored in Redis with TTL = 7 days

---

### 3.3.13 Cover Image Generation

Every product gets an AI-generated cover image via DALL-E 3.

```typescript
// /lib/products/cover-image.ts
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { uploadToR2 } from '../storage/r2';

const openai = new OpenAI();

export async function generateProductCover(
  prompt: string,
  productId: string,
  brandColors: BrandColors
): Promise<string> {
  // Enhance prompt with brand context
  const enhancedPrompt = `${prompt}. 
  Color palette: ${brandColors.primary} (primary), ${brandColors.secondary} (secondary). 
  Style: modern, professional, clean. 
  Square format, suitable for a digital product cover. 
  No text overlaid on the image.`;

  const response = await openai.images.generate({
    model: 'dall-e-3',
    prompt: enhancedPrompt,
    size: '1024x1024',
    quality: 'standard',
    n: 1,
  });

  const imageUrl = response.data[0].url!;

  // Download and re-upload to R2 (DALL-E URLs expire after 1 hour)
  const imageBuffer = await fetch(imageUrl).then(r => r.arrayBuffer());
  const r2Key = `covers/${productId}/cover.png`;
  await uploadToR2(r2Key, Buffer.from(imageBuffer), 'image/png');

  return `https://assets.know24.io/${r2Key}`;
}
```

**DALL-E 3 cost**: $0.040 per image (1024×1024 standard quality).

---

## 3.4 AI Workspace (Natural Language Business Management)

The AI Workspace is a per-business chat interface where creators manage their entire business through natural language. It combines streaming chat with tool/function calling to take real actions.

### 3.4.1 Intent Classification

Before routing to tool execution, incoming messages are classified using a lightweight call to the light model.

```typescript
// /lib/workspace/intent-classifier.ts

export type WorkspaceIntent =
  | 'query_analytics'
  | 'update_product'
  | 'create_product'
  | 'update_pricing'
  | 'manage_email'
  | 'generate_content'
  | 'manage_storefront'
  | 'run_scout'
  | 'query_knowledge'
  | 'general_conversation'
  | 'destructive_action';  // delete, disable, bulk changes

const IntentSchema = z.object({
  intent: z.enum([
    'query_analytics', 'update_product', 'create_product', 'update_pricing',
    'manage_email', 'generate_content', 'manage_storefront', 'run_scout',
    'query_knowledge', 'general_conversation', 'destructive_action'
  ]),
  confidence: z.number(),
  entities: z.record(z.string()), // extracted entities: productName, price, etc.
  requiresConfirmation: z.boolean(),
});

export async function classifyIntent(
  message: string,
  businessContext: BusinessContext
): Promise<IntentClassification> {
  return generateStructuredOutput(
    IntentSchema,
    AI_WORKSPACE_INTENT_PROMPT, // see Section 3.8
    `Business: ${businessContext.name}\nUser message: "${message}"`,
    'light'
  );
}
```

**Intents that always require confirmation** (requiresConfirmation = true):
- `destructive_action` (delete product, remove customer, cancel subscription)
- `update_pricing` when price change > 30%
- `manage_email` when sending to > 100 recipients
- `create_product` (show preview before building)

---

### 3.4.2 Tool/Function Calling Architecture

The workspace uses Vercel AI SDK's `tools` parameter to define available actions. Tools are scoped to the current business.

```typescript
// /lib/workspace/tools.ts
import { tool } from 'ai';
import { z } from 'zod';

export function buildWorkspaceTools(businessId: string, userId: string) {
  return {
    // Analytics
    getBusinessStats: tool({
      description: 'Get revenue, orders, subscriber count, and traffic stats for specified time period',
      parameters: z.object({
        period: z.enum(['today', 'this_week', 'this_month', 'last_30_days', 'all_time']),
        metric: z.enum(['revenue', 'orders', 'subscribers', 'traffic', 'all']).default('all'),
      }),
      execute: async ({ period, metric }) => getBusinessStats(businessId, period, metric),
    }),

    // Product management
    listProducts: tool({
      description: 'List all products for this business with their status, pricing, and sales data',
      parameters: z.object({}),
      execute: async () => listProducts(businessId),
    }),

    updateProductPrice: tool({
      description: 'Update the price of a specific product',
      parameters: z.object({
        productId: z.string(),
        newPrice: z.number(),
        pricingModel: z.enum(['one_time', 'monthly', 'annual']).optional(),
      }),
      execute: async ({ productId, newPrice, pricingModel }) =>
        updateProductPrice(businessId, productId, newPrice, pricingModel),
    }),

    updateProductStatus: tool({
      description: 'Activate or deactivate a product on the storefront',
      parameters: z.object({
        productId: z.string(),
        status: z.enum(['active', 'inactive', 'draft']),
      }),
      execute: async ({ productId, status }) =>
        updateProductStatus(businessId, productId, status),
    }),

    triggerProductGeneration: tool({
      description: 'Generate a new product from the knowledge base',
      parameters: z.object({
        productType: z.enum([
          'guide', 'framework', 'cheat_sheet', 'email_course',
          'assessment', 'prompt_pack', 'swipe_file', 'resource_directory',
          'workbook', 'mini_course', 'chatbot'
        ]),
        title: z.string().optional(),
        topic: z.string().optional(),
      }),
      execute: async (params) => enqueueProductGeneration(businessId, params),
    }),

    // Content generation
    generateBlogPost: tool({
      description: 'Generate and publish a new SEO-optimized blog post',
      parameters: z.object({
        topic: z.string().optional(),  // null = AI chooses best topic
        publishImmediately: z.boolean().default(false),
      }),
      execute: async ({ topic, publishImmediately }) =>
        enqueueBlogGeneration(businessId, topic, publishImmediately),
    }),

    generateSocialPost: tool({
      description: 'Generate a social media post for a specific product',
      parameters: z.object({
        productId: z.string(),
        length: z.enum(['short', 'medium', 'long']),
        includeImage: z.boolean().default(true),
      }),
      execute: async (params) => generateSocialPost(businessId, params),
    }),

    // Email management
    getEmailStats: tool({
      description: 'Get email list size, sequence performance, and recent campaign stats',
      parameters: z.object({}),
      execute: async () => getEmailStats(businessId),
    }),

    // Storefront management
    updateStorefrontColor: tool({
      description: 'Change the storefront color palette',
      parameters: z.object({
        palette: z.enum(['ocean', 'forest', 'sunset', 'midnight', 'custom']),
        customPrimary: z.string().optional(), // hex color if palette = custom
      }),
      execute: async ({ palette, customPrimary }) =>
        updateStorefrontPalette(businessId, palette, customPrimary),
    }),

    // Scout
    runScoutScan: tool({
      description: 'Run a Scout market scan to find new opportunities',
      parameters: z.object({
        scanType: z.enum(['full', 'hot_threads', 'influencers', 'podcasts', 'trending']).default('full'),
      }),
      execute: async ({ scanType }) => enqueueScoutScan(businessId, scanType),
    }),
  };
}
```

### 3.4.3 Workspace Chat Handler

```typescript
// /app/api/workspace/chat/route.ts
import { streamText } from 'ai';
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  const { messages, businessId } = await req.json();

  // Auth check: verify user owns this business
  const { userId } = await auth();
  await assertBusinessOwnership(userId, businessId);

  // Build context: business state snapshot
  const context = await buildBusinessContext(businessId);

  const model = await getModel('light');
  const tools = buildWorkspaceTools(businessId, userId);

  const result = streamText({
    model,
    system: buildWorkspaceSystemPrompt(context),
    messages,
    tools,
    maxToolRoundtrips: 3,  // allow up to 3 tool calls per response
    temperature: 0.7,
    maxTokens: 1000,
    onFinish: async ({ usage }) => {
      // Track token usage for metering
      await trackAIUsage(businessId, 'workspace_chat', usage);
    },
  });

  return result.toDataStreamResponse();
}
```

### 3.4.4 Business Context Assembly

The workspace system prompt includes a snapshot of the business state so the AI answers questions accurately without needing tool calls for basic queries.

```typescript
// /lib/workspace/context-builder.ts

export async function buildBusinessContext(businessId: string): Promise<BusinessContext> {
  const [business, products, emailStats, recentActivity] = await Promise.all([
    getBusinessById(businessId),
    listProducts(businessId),
    getEmailStats(businessId),
    getRecentActivity(businessId, 10),
  ]);

  return {
    name: business.name,
    plan: business.plan,
    hasScout: business.hasScoutAddon,
    storefront: {
      status: business.storefrontStatus,
      url: business.storefrontUrl,
      visitsThisMonth: business.visitsThisMonth,
    },
    products: products.map(p => ({
      id: p.id,
      name: p.name,
      type: p.type,
      price: p.price,
      pricingModel: p.pricingModel,
      status: p.status,
      totalRevenue: p.totalRevenue,
      salesCount: p.salesCount,
    })),
    emailList: {
      total: emailStats.totalSubscribers,
      growthThisMonth: emailStats.growthThisMonth,
    },
    revenue: {
      thisMonth: business.revenueThisMonth,
      lastMonth: business.revenueLastMonth,
    },
    usage: {
      aiOpsThisMonth: business.aiOpsThisMonth,
      socialPostsThisMonth: business.socialPostsThisMonth,
      scoutScansThisMonth: business.scoutScansThisMonth,
    },
    recentActivity: recentActivity.map(a => a.description),
  };
}
```

**Context window management**: The workspace system prompt + business context is ~1,500 tokens. Combined with 20-message conversation history (max ~4,000 tokens) and response budget (1,000 tokens), total window is ~6,500 tokens — well within Claude Haiku's 200k limit. No pruning needed at normal usage.

---

### 3.4.5 Confirmation Flow for Destructive Actions

When `requiresConfirmation = true`, the response includes a structured confirmation request instead of immediately executing:

```typescript
// Pattern: AI describes what it will do, asks for confirmation
// Frontend renders a special "confirm action" card

export interface ConfirmationCard {
  actionType: string;
  description: string;
  impact: string[];    // list of what will change
  confirmText: string; // button label: "Yes, do it"
  cancelText: string;  // button label: "Cancel"
  pendingAction: SerializedToolCall; // stored client-side, executed on confirm
}

// Example response for: "Delete the Cheat Sheet product"
const confirmationCard: ConfirmationCard = {
  actionType: 'delete_product',
  description: 'Delete "ICU Medication Quick Reference"',
  impact: [
    'Product will be removed from your storefront immediately',
    '12 customers who purchased this will lose access',
    'This cannot be undone',
  ],
  confirmText: 'Yes, delete it',
  cancelText: 'Keep it',
  pendingAction: { tool: 'deleteProduct', args: { productId: 'prod_xyz' } },
};
```

---

## 3.5 Content Generation Systems

### 3.5.1 Blog Post Generator

Generates SEO-optimized articles in the creator's voice, published automatically on a schedule.

#### Generation Pipeline

```typescript
// /lib/content/blog-generator.ts

export interface BlogPostInput {
  businessId: string;
  topic?: string;       // null = AI selects best topic
  targetKeyword?: string;
  style?: 'how-to' | 'list' | 'opinion' | 'case-study' | 'explainer';
}

export interface GeneratedBlogPost {
  title: string;
  metaDescription: string;  // 150–160 chars
  slug: string;
  focusKeyword: string;
  excerpt: string;           // 200 chars
  content: string;           // full markdown, 800–1500 words
  headings: string[];        // H2 and H3 headings for TOC
  internalLinks: {
    anchorText: string;
    productId: string;
    productName: string;
  }[];
  schema: {                  // JSON-LD schema for GEO optimization
    articleType: string;
    keywords: string[];
    faqItems?: { question: string; answer: string }[];
  };
  estimatedReadTime: number; // minutes
  voiceConsistencyScore: number; // 0–1, how well it matches creator's voice
}
```

#### Topic Selection (when no topic provided)

```typescript
export async function selectBlogTopic(
  businessId: string,
  publishedTitles: string[]
): Promise<{ topic: string; keyword: string; rationale: string }> {
  const profile = await getExpertiseProfile(businessId);
  const products = await listProducts(businessId);

  // Use light model to select topic
  return generateStructuredOutput(
    BlogTopicSchema,
    BLOG_POST_GENERATION_PROMPT, // see Section 3.8
    `Expert domains: ${profile.domains.join(', ')}
Products to internally link: ${products.map(p => p.name).join(', ')}
Already published: ${publishedTitles.slice(-20).join('; ')}
Select the best new blog topic that hasn't been covered yet and has clear SEO value.`,
    'light'
  );
}
```

#### Voice Consistency

The creator's "voice" is derived from their AI Interview transcript and any written content they've provided. A voice profile is stored as part of the expertise profile:

```typescript
export interface VoiceProfile {
  toneDescriptors: string[];   // ["direct", "warm", "evidence-based", "practical"]
  vocabulary: string[];        // domain-specific terms the creator uses
  sentenceStyle: string;       // "Short declarative sentences. No fluff."
  perspective: string;         // "First person, draws on personal experience"
  avoids: string[];            // ["jargon", "passive voice", "hedging"]
}
```

The voice profile is injected into every content generation prompt.

#### Publishing Schedule

- **Frequency**: 3 posts/week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), published at 9 AM creator's timezone
- **Trigger**: Cron job at 8 AM UTC, staggered by timezone
- **Job**: `blog:generate-and-publish:{businessId}`
- Each publish checks: (1) post not already queued for today, (2) under monthly AI ceiling

---

### 3.5.2 Social Media Post Generator

Generates platform-ready posts with 1080×1080 images.

#### Post Generation Pipeline

```typescript
// /lib/content/social-generator.ts

export type PostLength = 'short' | 'medium' | 'long';

export interface SocialPostOutput {
  shortVersion: string;    // tweet/caption: 140–280 chars
  mediumVersion: string;   // standard post: 300–500 chars
  longVersion: string;     // LinkedIn article: 800–1200 chars
  hashtags: string[];      // 5–10 relevant hashtags
  imagePrompt: string;     // DALL-E prompt for 1080x1080 image
  callToAction: string;    // "Get it at [link]" style CTA
}

export async function generateSocialPost(
  businessId: string,
  productId: string,
  length: PostLength
): Promise<StoredSocialPost> {
  const [product, profile] = await Promise.all([
    getProduct(productId),
    getExpertiseProfile(businessId),
  ]);

  // Retrieve relevant knowledge chunks for product context
  const context = await assembleProductContext(businessId, [
    `${product.name} main benefits`,
    `key transformation this product delivers`,
  ], 5);

  const postContent = await generateStructuredOutput(
    SocialPostSchema,
    SOCIAL_MEDIA_POST_PROMPT, // see Section 3.8
    `Product: ${product.name} (${product.type})
Price: ${product.price}
Description: ${product.description}
Creator voice: ${profile.voiceProfile?.toneDescriptors.join(', ')}
Context from knowledge base:\n${context}`,
    'light'
  );

  // Enqueue image generation (async, ~20s)
  const imageJobId = await enqueueImageGeneration({
    businessId,
    productId,
    prompt: postContent.imagePrompt,
    dimensions: '1024x1024',
  });

  // Store in post_tray
  return saveToPostTray(businessId, productId, postContent, imageJobId);
}
```

#### Post Tray Storage

```sql
CREATE TABLE social_post_tray (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_id UUID REFERENCES products(id),
  short_version TEXT NOT NULL,
  medium_version TEXT NOT NULL,
  long_version TEXT NOT NULL,
  hashtags TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  image_url TEXT,           -- populated when image generation completes
  image_job_id TEXT,        -- BullMQ job ID for tracking
  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,      -- null = available in tray
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX social_post_tray_business_idx ON social_post_tray (business_id);
CREATE INDEX social_post_tray_unused_idx ON social_post_tray (business_id) WHERE used_at IS NULL;
```

#### Monthly Metering

The 300 posts/month limit is tracked in the `businesses` table as `social_posts_generated_this_month`. Reset on billing cycle. Incremented atomically:

```typescript
await supabase.rpc('increment_social_post_count', { p_business_id: businessId });
// Throws if count would exceed 300 (checked in RPC)
```

---

### 3.5.3 Email Sequence Generator

Four pre-defined sequence types, all generated from knowledge base + product context.

#### Sequence Types

| Sequence | Emails | Trigger | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|---------|
| Welcome | 5 | Email list signup | Onboard to creator's world, deliver lead magnet |
| Nurture | 7 | Post-welcome, ongoing | Build relationship, establish expertise |
| Product Launch | 3 | Creator-triggered | Drive sales for new/featured product |
| Re-engagement | 4 | 60 days of inactivity | Win back cold subscribers |

#### Generation Schema

```typescript
const EmailSequenceSchema = z.object({
  sequenceType: z.enum(['welcome', 'nurture', 'product_launch', 're_engagement']),
  sequenceName: z.string(),
  emails: z.array(z.object({
    sequencePosition: z.number(),
    sendDelayHours: z.number(),   // hours after sequence start or previous email
    subjectLine: z.string(),
    previewText: z.string(),      // 40–60 chars
    bodyHtml: z.string(),         // full HTML email body
    bodyText: z.string(),         // plain text fallback
    personalizationTokens: z.array(z.object({
      token: z.string(),          // e.g., "{{first_name}}"
      description: z.string(),    // what it contains
      fallback: z.string(),       // fallback if token unavailable
    })),
    primaryCta: z.object({
      text: z.string(),
      url: z.string(),            // template URL with product ID placeholder
    }),
    abVariants: z.array(z.object({
      variantLabel: z.string(),   // "A", "B"
      subjectLine: z.string(),
    })).optional(),
  })),
});
```

#### Email Delivery Integration

Emails are sent via **Resend** (`resend` npm package). Each business has a sending domain configured (subdomain delegation via Cloudflare DNS).

```typescript
// /lib/email/sender.ts
import { Resend } from 'resend';

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY!);

export async function sendSequenceEmail(
  to: string,
  email: SequenceEmail,
  personalization: Record<string, string>
): Promise<string> {
  const body = applyPersonalization(email.bodyHtml, personalization);
  const subject = applyPersonalization(email.subjectLine, personalization);

  const result = await resend.emails.send({
    from: `${personalization.creatorName} <noreply@${personalization.sendingDomain}>`,
    to,
    subject,
    html: body,
    text: applyPersonalization(email.bodyText, personalization),
    tags: [
      { name: 'business_id', value: personalization.businessId },
      { name: 'sequence_type', value: email.sequenceType },
    ],
  });

  return result.id;
}
```

**A/B Testing**: Subject line variants are stored in `email_sequence_variants`. At send time, the BullMQ worker randomly assigns variant A (50%) or variant B (50%) and tracks open rates per variant in `email_events`.

---

## 3.6 Scout System (AI Outbound Intelligence)

Scout is the most architecturally complex feature. A "scan" is a multi-stage pipeline that queries external APIs, scores relevance, and generates draft responses.

### 3.6.1 Scan Architecture

```
Scout Scan Job (Railway worker)
    │
    ├── [Parallel Stage 1: Data Collection]
    │   ├── Reddit API → hot threads in relevant subreddits
    │   ├── X/Twitter API → trending topics + relevant posts
    │   ├── Google Custom Search → forum posts, Quora, news
    │   ├── Podcast Index API → podcast guest opportunities
    │   └── LinkedIn → (compliant approach, see 3.6.5)
    │
    ├── [Stage 2: Raw Opportunity Pool]
    │   └── ~50–200 raw results per scan
    │
    ├── [Stage 3: AI Relevance Scoring]
    │   └── Heavy model batch-scores all results against business profile
    │       → Keep top 20–30 (score ≥ 0.60)
    │
    ├── [Stage 4: Classification]
    │   └── Classify each into: hot_thread, influencer_match, podcast,
    │       trending_topic, community, competitor_activity
    │
    ├── [Stage 5: Draft Generation]
    │   └── Light model generates draft response/outreach for each
    │
    └── [Stage 6: Storage & Notification]
        └── Save to scout_opportunities, notify creator
```

---

### 3.6.2 Data Collection APIs

#### Reddit

```typescript
// /lib/scout/collectors/reddit.ts
// Uses official Reddit API (OAuth2 app, read-only scope)

const REDDIT_CLIENT_ID = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID!;
const REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET!;

export async function searchReddit(
  queries: string[],
  relevantSubreddits: string[],
  timeFilter: 'hour' | 'day' | 'week' = 'day'
): Promise<RedditPost[]> {
  const token = await getRedditToken();
  const posts: RedditPost[] = [];

  // Search within specific subreddits
  for (const subreddit of relevantSubreddits) {
    for (const query of queries.slice(0, 3)) { // max 3 queries per subreddit
      const response = await fetch(
        `https://oauth.reddit.com/r/${subreddit}/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&sort=hot&t=${timeFilter}&limit=10`,
        { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, 'User-Agent': 'Know24Scout/1.0' } }
      );
      const data = await response.json();
      posts.push(...data.data.children.map(parseRedditPost));
    }
  }

  // Also search r/all for broader discovery
  const allSearch = await fetch(
    `https://oauth.reddit.com/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(queries[0])}&sort=hot&t=${timeFilter}&limit=20`,
    { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, 'User-Agent': 'Know24Scout/1.0' } }
  );

  return deduplicateById(posts);
}

// Subreddit selection: derived during knowledge analysis phase
// Stored in expertise_profile.suggestedSubreddits[]
// e.g., for nurse leadership: ["nursing", "nursepractitioner", "noctor", "medicine"]
```

**Reddit API limits**: 60 requests/minute (OAuth). Each full scan uses ~20–30 requests.

#### X/Twitter

```typescript
// /lib/scout/collectors/twitter.ts
// Uses X API v2 (Basic tier: $100/mo, 500k tweet reads/month)

import { TwitterApi } from 'twitter-api-v2';

const client = new TwitterApi(process.env.TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN!);

export async function searchTwitter(
  queries: string[],
  maxResults: number = 50
): Promise<Tweet[]> {
  const tweets: Tweet[] = [];

  for (const query of queries) {
    const results = await client.v2.search(
      `${query} -is:retweet lang:en`,
      {
        max_results: Math.min(maxResults, 100),
        'tweet.fields': ['public_metrics', 'author_id', 'created_at', 'context_annotations'],
        'user.fields': ['name', 'username', 'public_metrics', 'description'],
        expansions: ['author_id'],
        sort_order: 'relevancy',
      }
    );
    tweets.push(...parseTweets(results));
  }

  return deduplicateById(tweets);
}
```

#### Google Custom Search (Quora, Forums, News)

```typescript
// /lib/scout/collectors/google-search.ts
// Uses Google Custom Search JSON API ($5/1000 queries)

export async function searchWeb(
  query: string,
  siteRestrictions?: string[]  // e.g., ['quora.com', 'reddit.com']
): Promise<SearchResult[]> {
  const siteFilter = siteRestrictions
    ? `site:${siteRestrictions.join(' OR site:')}`
    : '';

  const params = new URLSearchParams({
    key: process.env.GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY!,
    cx: process.env.GOOGLE_SEARCH_CX!,  // Custom Search Engine ID
    q: `${query} ${siteFilter}`.trim(),
    num: '10',
    dateRestrict: 'd7',  // last 7 days
  });

  const response = await fetch(`https://www.googleapis.com/customsearch/v1?${params}`);
  const data = await response.json();
  return (data.items ?? []).map(parseSearchResult);
}
```

#### Podcast Index API

```typescript
// /lib/scout/collectors/podcasts.ts
// Podcast Index API (free, requires API key + secret)

export async function searchPodcasts(
  terms: string[],
  minListeners?: number
): Promise<PodcastResult[]> {
  const timestamp = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
  const hash = sha1(`${process.env.PODCAST_INDEX_KEY}${process.env.PODCAST_INDEX_SECRET}${timestamp}`);

  const results: PodcastResult[] = [];
  for (const term of terms.slice(0, 3)) {
    const response = await fetch(
      `https://api.podcastindex.org/api/1.0/search/byterm?q=${encodeURIComponent(term)}&max=10`,
      {
        headers: {
          'X-Auth-Key': process.env.PODCAST_INDEX_KEY!,
          'X-Auth-Date': timestamp.toString(),
          Authorization: `sha1hash ${hash}`,
          'User-Agent': 'Know24Scout/1.0',
        },
      }
    );
    const data = await response.json();
    results.push(...(data.feeds ?? []).map(parsePodcastFeed));
  }

  return results.filter(p => !minListeners || p.itunesId); // active podcasts only
}
```

---

### 3.6.3 Relevance Scoring

After collecting ~50–200 raw results, the heavy model scores each against the business's expertise profile.

```typescript
// /lib/scout/scorer.ts

export interface OpportunityScore {
  rawId: string;
  relevanceScore: number;   // 0.0–1.0
  relevanceReason: string;  // one-sentence explanation
  opportunityType: OpportunityType;
  urgency: 'hot' | 'warm' | 'cold'; // hot = act within 24h
  estimatedReach: number;   // estimated audience size
  productMatch: string;     // which product is most relevant
}

// Batch scoring: send up to 30 opportunities per prompt
export async function batchScoreOpportunities(
  opportunities: RawOpportunity[],
  profile: ExpertiseProfile,
  products: Product[]
): Promise<OpportunityScore[]> {
  const batchSize = 30;
  const results: OpportunityScore[] = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < opportunities.length; i += batchSize) {
    const batch = opportunities.slice(i, i + batchSize);
    const scores = await generateStructuredOutput(
      z.array(OpportunityScoreSchema),
      SCOUT_OPPORTUNITY_ANALYSIS_PROMPT, // see Section 3.8
      formatOpportunitiesForScoring(batch, profile, products),
      'heavy'
    );
    results.push(...scores);
  }

  return results.filter(s => s.relevanceScore >= 0.60);
}
```

---

### 3.6.4 Draft Response Generation

For each opportunity that passes the relevance threshold, a draft response is generated:

```typescript
export interface ScoutDraft {
  opportunityId: string;
  draftType: 'reply' | 'outreach_dm' | 'podcast_pitch' | 'content_post';
  draft: string;
  keyPoints: string[];        // what the draft accomplishes
  naturalnesScore: number;   // 0–1: how organic/non-spammy the draft reads
  productMention: 'direct' | 'soft' | 'none';
}

const draftGenerationPrompt = (opportunity: ScoredOpportunity, creator: Creator): string =>
  `You are writing on behalf of ${creator.name}, an expert in ${creator.domains.join(', ')}.
  
Opportunity: ${opportunity.title}
Platform: ${opportunity.platform}
Context: ${opportunity.description}
Type: ${opportunity.opportunityType}

Write a ${opportunityToDraftType(opportunity.opportunityType)} that:
1. Provides genuine value first — this is NOT an ad
2. Sounds like a real expert, not a marketer
3. Naturally mentions ${opportunity.productMatch} only if it fits organically
4. Matches ${creator.voiceProfile?.toneDescriptors.join(', ')} tone

Draft:`;
```

### 3.6.5 LinkedIn Approach (Compliant)

Direct LinkedIn scraping violates their Terms of Service and triggers legal action (hiQ v. LinkedIn). Know24's approach:

1. **LinkedIn API (Member Data Portability)**: Not suitable for prospecting
2. **LinkedIn Marketing API**: For paid ads only
3. **Google Custom Search restricted to linkedin.com**: Public profile data visible in search results
4. **Manual input**: Creator can paste LinkedIn profile URLs; Scout enriches with public search data

```typescript
// Compliant: Google search restricted to LinkedIn public content
const linkedinOpportunities = await searchWeb(
  `${primaryTopic} thought leader expert`,
  ['linkedin.com/in', 'linkedin.com/pub']
);
// Returns only publicly indexed LinkedIn content
```

**Note for creators**: Scout surfaces LinkedIn opportunities but clearly labels them "LinkedIn (public search)". Direct outreach is done manually by the creator — Scout provides the draft, not the send.

### 3.6.6 Opportunity Storage

```sql
CREATE TABLE scout_opportunities (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  scan_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scout_scans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- Opportunity data
  opportunity_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (
    opportunity_type IN ('hot_thread', 'influencer_match', 'podcast', 'trending_topic', 'community', 'competitor')
  ),
  platform TEXT NOT NULL,       -- 'reddit', 'twitter', 'linkedin', 'quora', 'podcast', 'news'
  source_url TEXT,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  context TEXT,                 -- surrounding context (thread excerpt, bio, etc.)
  -- Scoring
  relevance_score NUMERIC(4,3) NOT NULL,
  relevance_reason TEXT,
  urgency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'warm',
  estimated_reach INTEGER,
  product_match_id UUID REFERENCES products(id),
  -- Draft
  draft_content TEXT,
  draft_type TEXT,
  -- Creator action
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
    CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'edited', 'dismissed', 'sent')),
  creator_notes TEXT,
  actioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX scout_opportunities_business_scan_idx
  ON scout_opportunities (business_id, scan_id);
CREATE INDEX scout_opportunities_pending_idx
  ON scout_opportunities (business_id, status)
  WHERE status = 'pending';
```

### 3.6.7 Rate Limiting & Platform Compliance

| Platform | API Used | Rate Limit | Monthly Budget (20 scans) |
|----------|---------|-----------|--------------------------|
| Reddit | OAuth2 REST | 60 req/min | ~600 requests |
| X/Twitter | API v2 Basic | 500k reads/mo | ~10k reads |
| Google CSE | JSON API | 100 queries/day | ~200 queries |
| Podcast Index | REST API | 1 req/sec | ~200 requests |
| Firecrawl | REST API | Plan-dependent | ~100 page fetches |

**Ethics guardrails**:
1. All Scout drafts include a naturalness check — drafts scoring below 0.70 naturalness are flagged with a warning
2. Scout never auto-sends anything — every action requires creator approval
3. Platform terms are checked quarterly; compliance updates shipped immediately
4. Reddit rate limit: minimum 1 second between requests, tracked in Redis
5. User-Agent always identifies as "Know24Scout/1.0" for transparency

## 3.7 AI Usage Metering & Cost Control

### 3.7.1 Token Counting Per Operation

Every AI call is tracked in the `ai_usage_events` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE ai_usage_events (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  business_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES businesses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,        -- Clerk user ID
  operation_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'knowledge_analysis', 'product_generation', 'blog_post', etc.
  model TEXT NOT NULL,
  prompt_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL,
  completion_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL,
  total_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL,
  cost_usd NUMERIC(10,6) NOT NULL,
  duration_ms INTEGER,
  job_id TEXT,                  -- BullMQ job ID if async
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Fast aggregation queries for billing
CREATE INDEX ai_usage_events_business_month_idx
  ON ai_usage_events (business_id, date_trunc('month', created_at));

-- Materialized view for monthly totals (refreshed hourly)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW business_monthly_ai_usage AS
SELECT
  business_id,
  date_trunc('month', created_at) AS month,
  SUM(total_tokens) AS total_tokens,
  SUM(cost_usd) AS total_cost_usd,
  COUNT(*) AS operation_count
FROM ai_usage_events
GROUP BY business_id, date_trunc('month', created_at);
```

```typescript
// /lib/ai/usage-tracker.ts

export async function trackAIUsage(
  businessId: string,
  operationType: string,
  usage: { promptTokens: number; completionTokens: number },
  model: string
): Promise<void> {
  const costUsd = calculateCost(model, usage.promptTokens, usage.completionTokens);

  await supabase.from('ai_usage_events').insert({
    business_id: businessId,
    operation_type: operationType,
    model,
    prompt_tokens: usage.promptTokens,
    completion_tokens: usage.completionTokens,
    total_tokens: usage.promptTokens + usage.completionTokens,
    cost_usd: costUsd,
  });
}

const COST_PER_MILLION: Record<string, { input: number; output: number }> = {
  'claude-haiku-4-5': { input: 0.80, output: 4.00 },
  'claude-sonnet-4-5': { input: 3.00, output: 15.00 },
  'gpt-4o': { input: 2.50, output: 10.00 },
  'gpt-4o-mini': { input: 0.15, output: 0.60 },
  'text-embedding-3-small': { input: 0.020, output: 0 },
};

function calculateCost(
  model: string,
  promptTokens: number,
  completionTokens: number
): number {
  const rates = COST_PER_MILLION[model] ?? { input: 0.003, output: 0.015 };
  return (promptTokens / 1_000_000) * rates.input +
         (completionTokens / 1_000_000) * rates.output;
}
```

---

### 3.7.2 Monthly Ceiling & Overage

**Base plan monthly AI ceiling**: $8.00 of AI costs per business (approximately 20–30 typical operations with buffer).

At this ceiling:
- 5 full product generations ≈ $0.55
- 12 blog posts ≈ $0.08
- 300 social posts ≈ $0.45
- 200 chatbot conversations ≈ $0.40
- 1 Scout scan ≈ $0.13
- Workspace chat (50 sessions) ≈ $0.13
- Total: ~$1.74/month for a moderately active creator

The $8.00 ceiling is 4.6× the expected P50 usage, meaning ~95% of creators never hit it. Power users who exceed it pay $0.01 per 1,000 tokens (approximately $10/M tokens blended rate with Know24's margin).

```typescript
// /lib/ai/ceiling-checker.ts

const MONTHLY_CEILING_USD = 8.00;
const OVERAGE_RATE_PER_1K_TOKENS = 0.01;

export async function checkAndEnforceAICeiling(
  businessId: string
): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; warningLevel: 'none' | 'approaching' | 'exceeded' }> {
  const { data } = await supabase
    .from('business_monthly_ai_usage')
    .select('total_cost_usd')
    .eq('business_id', businessId)
    .eq('month', startOfCurrentMonth())
    .single();

  const currentCost = data?.total_cost_usd ?? 0;

  if (currentCost >= MONTHLY_CEILING_USD) {
    // Check if overage billing is enabled (Stripe metered)
    const hasOverageBilling = await checkStripeOverageBilling(businessId);
    if (!hasOverageBilling) {
      return { allowed: false, warningLevel: 'exceeded' };
    }
    // Bill overage via Stripe
    await reportStripeUsage(businessId, currentCost - MONTHLY_CEILING_USD);
    return { allowed: true, warningLevel: 'exceeded' };
  }

  if (currentCost >= MONTHLY_CEILING_USD * 0.80) {
    return { allowed: true, warningLevel: 'approaching' };
  }

  return { allowed: true, warningLevel: 'none' };
}
```

---

### 3.7.3 Stripe Metered Billing

```typescript
// /lib/billing/stripe-metering.ts
import Stripe from 'stripe';

const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!);

// Called at the end of each billing cycle (monthly)
export async function reportMonthlyAIOverage(
  businessId: string,
  overageTokens: number
): Promise<void> {
  const subscription = await getBusinessSubscription(businessId);
  if (!subscription?.stripeSubscriptionId) return;

  await stripe.subscriptionItems.createUsageRecord(
    subscription.stripeAIOverageItemId, // separate line item on subscription
    {
      quantity: Math.ceil(overageTokens / 1000), // bill per 1K tokens
      timestamp: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),
      action: 'increment',
    }
  );
}
```

**Stripe product setup**: The Know24 subscription has two line items:
1. Base plan: $99/month flat
2. AI overage: $0.01/unit (1 unit = 1,000 tokens), metered

---

### 3.7.4 Circuit Breakers

Circuit breakers prevent runaway costs from bugs or abuse.

```typescript
// /lib/ai/circuit-breaker.ts

const CIRCUIT_BREAKERS = {
  maxTokensPerRequest: 50_000,    // single request never exceeds this
  maxCostPerHour: 2.00,           // USD: stops if one business spends >$2 in an hour
  maxOperationsPerMinute: 10,     // rate limit per business
  maxCostPerDay: 5.00,            // daily hard cap (blocks until midnight)
};

export async function runWithCircuitBreaker<T>(
  businessId: string,
  operation: string,
  estimatedCost: number,
  fn: () => Promise<T>
): Promise<T> {
  // Check rate limiting (Redis sliding window)
  const recentOps = await redis.incr(`rate:${businessId}:${Math.floor(Date.now() / 60000)}`);
  if (recentOps > CIRCUIT_BREAKERS.maxOperationsPerMinute) {
    throw new AIRateLimitError('Too many AI operations. Please wait a moment.');
  }

  // Check hourly spend
  const hourlySpend = await getHourlySpend(businessId);
  if (hourlySpend + estimatedCost > CIRCUIT_BREAKERS.maxCostPerHour) {
    throw new AICircuitBreakerError('Hourly AI budget exceeded. Operations resume next hour.');
  }

  // Check daily spend
  const dailySpend = await getDailySpend(businessId);
  if (dailySpend + estimatedCost > CIRCUIT_BREAKERS.maxCostPerDay) {
    throw new AICircuitBreakerError('Daily AI budget exceeded. Operations resume at midnight.');
  }

  return fn();
}
```

---

### 3.7.5 Caching Strategy

Identical content is never regenerated. Cache keys are based on content hashes.

```typescript
// /lib/ai/cache.ts

// Cache in Upstash Redis, TTL varies by operation type
const CACHE_TTL: Record<string, number> = {
  knowledge_analysis: 24 * 60 * 60,    // 24 hours
  product_generation: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60, // 7 days (regenerate if knowledge changes)
  blog_post: 0,                          // never cache (always unique)
  social_post: 60 * 60,                  // 1 hour (same product + length = reuse)
  embedding: 30 * 24 * 60 * 60,         // 30 days (embeddings are stable)
  intent_classification: 5 * 60,         // 5 minutes (same message = same intent)
};

export async function getCachedOrGenerate<T>(
  cacheKey: string,
  operation: string,
  generateFn: () => Promise<T>
): Promise<T> {
  const ttl = CACHE_TTL[operation];
  if (ttl === 0) return generateFn(); // never cache

  const cached = await redis.get(cacheKey);
  if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached) as T;

  const result = await generateFn();
  await redis.setex(cacheKey, ttl, JSON.stringify(result));
  return result;
}

// Cache key construction
export function buildCacheKey(operation: string, ...parts: string[]): string {
  const hash = createHash('sha256')
    .update(parts.join(':'))
    .digest('hex')
    .slice(0, 16);
  return `ai:${operation}:${hash}`;
}
```

**Knowledge-change invalidation**: When new knowledge is ingested, all cached product generations for that business are invalidated:

```typescript
await redis.del(...await redis.keys(`ai:product_generation:${businessId}:*`));
```

### 3.7.6 Operator Cost Dashboard

Platform operators (Know24 team) can view aggregate AI costs across all businesses at `/admin/ai-costs`. Key metrics:

- Total AI spend this month (all businesses)
- Average cost per business per month
- Most expensive operations by type
- Businesses approaching ceiling
- Model distribution (which models are being called)
- Cache hit rate (target: >30%)

Data served from the `business_monthly_ai_usage` materialized view, refreshed hourly.
