### CAN-SPAM / Email Compliance

Know24 sends two categories of email:
1. **Platform emails** — to creators (transactional: billing, onboarding, alerts)
2. **Creator-to-buyer emails** — sequences generated by Know24 on behalf of creators (marketing)

**Both categories must comply with CAN-SPAM. Category 2 also requires compliance with GDPR consent requirements for EU recipients.**

**Requirements (enforced at template level, not just policy):**

1. **Unsubscribe mechanism in every email** — Every email template must include a one-click unsubscribe link. Know24's email templates use a standardized footer component that injects this automatically. Creators cannot send emails without it.

```typescript
// Email footer component (always appended)
const emailFooter = `
<div style="margin-top:32px;padding-top:16px;border-top:1px solid #e5e7eb;font-size:12px;color:#6b7280;">
  <p>You received this email because you purchased from {{creator_name}}.</p>
  <p>
    <a href="{{unsubscribe_url}}" style="color:#0891b2;">Unsubscribe</a> | 
    <a href="{{privacy_url}}" style="color:#0891b2;">Privacy Policy</a>
  </p>
  <p>{{creator_name}} · {{creator_physical_address}}</p>
</div>
`;
```

2. **Physical address requirement** — Creators must provide a physical or P.O. Box address during onboarding (required field). This address is included in all outbound marketing emails. Transactional emails from Know24 use Know24's business address.

3. **Opt-in confirmation** — Buyers who provide email via lead magnet receive a double opt-in confirmation email before entering any nurture sequence. Buyers who purchase are added to the sequence automatically (legitimate interest / contract performance basis) but can unsubscribe at any time.

4. **Unsubscribe processing** — Unsubscribe requests are honored within 10 business days (CAN-SPAM requires 10; GDPR requires prompt processing). In practice, implement immediate unsubscribe: clicking the link calls `/api/unsubscribe?token=[signed-jwt]` and removes the contact from all sequences in real time.

5. **Bounce handling** — Monitor email delivery via the chosen ESP (transactional email service — recommend Resend.com for API-driven email). Hard bounces: remove immediately. Soft bounces: retry 3 times over 72 hours, then suppress. Integrate bounce webhooks from Resend.

6. **Complaint handling** — Implement FBL (Feedback Loop) monitoring. When a recipient marks an email as spam, the ESP sends a complaint notification. Process these within 24 hours by suppressing the contact.

7. **Subject line accuracy** — Know24's AI-generated email subject lines are reviewed at template level. The AI is instructed to never generate deceptive or misleading subject lines. This is documented in the AI prompt library.

---

### Accessibility Compliance

**WCAG 2.1 AA Requirements**

Know24's dashboard and all creator storefronts must meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA. This is both a legal obligation (ADA, Section 508 context) and a quality standard.

Key requirements by principle:

**Perceivable:**
- All images have meaningful `alt` text (AI-generated images get auto-generated alt text; creators can edit)
- Color is not the only means of conveying information (error states use icon + text + color)
- Color contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text — Know24's teal (#0891b2) on white passes AA at large text; use dark variants (#0369a1) for body text on white backgrounds
- Video content has captions (mini-course videos must have transcripts/captions)
- Audio content has text alternatives

**Operable:**
- All functionality accessible by keyboard
- No keyboard traps
- Focus indicator visible at all times (`outline: 2px solid #0891b2` — never `outline: none` without a visible replacement)
- Skip navigation link at top of every page
- No content that flashes more than 3 times per second

**Understandable:**
- Language of page declared in `<html lang="en">`
- Error messages identify the field and describe what to fix
- Consistent navigation patterns across all pages
- Forms have properly associated `<label>` elements

**Robust:**
- Valid HTML (no duplicate IDs)
- Aria attributes used correctly (`role`, `aria-label`, `aria-describedby`)
- Components work with screen readers (test with NVDA + Firefox, VoiceOver + Safari)

**ADA Considerations**

While the ADA does not explicitly reference WCAG, courts have consistently held that websites of public accommodations must be accessible. Know24's public storefront pages and checkout flow are the highest-risk surfaces (they serve the general public). Prioritize accessibility on: storefront pages, checkout, chatbot interface, product access pages.

**Remediation Process**

1. **Pre-launch audit**: Run Axe DevTools or Lighthouse accessibility audit on all page templates before launch. Fix all Critical and Serious violations.
2. **Ongoing**: Include accessibility checks in CI pipeline (axe-core automated testing in Playwright E2E tests).
3. **User reports**: Provide `accessibility@know24.com` in the footer for accessibility issues.
4. **Response SLA**: Acknowledge within 2 business days, remediate Critical issues within 14 days.
5. **Annual audit**: Commission a manual accessibility audit with assistive technology testing annually.

---

## 5.3 Legal Documents Needed

The following documents are required before launching. Each outline specifies what the document must cover. Engage a SaaS-specialized attorney to draft the actual text.

### 1. Terms of Service (Know24 → Creators)

*The agreement between Know24 Inc. and creator subscribers.*

Required sections:
- Definitions (Know24, Creator, Customer, Platform, Content, Subscription)
- Eligibility (age 18+, legal capacity to contract, not on prohibited persons lists)
- Account registration and security obligations
- Subscription terms: billing cycle, auto-renewal, price changes (30-day notice), failed payment grace period
- Know24's license to process creator content to provide the service (AI processing, storage, distribution)
- Creator's ownership of their content (Know24 claims no ownership)
- Acceptable use policy (reference AUP document)
- Prohibited content (illegal content, copyright infringement, misinformation marketed as fact, adult content without age verification)
- Platform availability (no uptime guarantee in ToS; SLA in separate enterprise agreements)
- AI-generated content disclaimer: Know24 does not guarantee accuracy of AI-generated products; creator is responsible for reviewing before publishing
- Intellectual property: Know24's platform IP, creator's content IP, AI-generated content ownership (creator owns outputs)
- Revenue share mechanics: Know24 takes subscription fee; creators receive product revenue directly via Stripe Connect
- Payment terms: subscription fees, failed payment handling, refund policy (reference Refund Policy)
- Know24's right to suspend/terminate accounts for AUP violations (with cure period for non-material violations)
- Limitation of liability (cap at 12 months of fees paid)
- Disclaimer of warranties (platform provided "as is")
- Indemnification (creator indemnifies Know24 for their content; Know24 indemnifies creator for platform IP claims)
- Dispute resolution: arbitration clause, class action waiver, California law governing
- Changes to terms (30-day notice for material changes)
- Contact information

### 2. Terms of Service (Creators → Their Customers)

*Know24 provides a template that creators can customize. The creator presents this as their own ToS.*

Required sections:
- Who the seller is (the creator — Know24 is named as the platform powering fulfillment)
- Products offered and their descriptions
- Pricing and payment (Stripe payment processing disclosed)
- Refund policy (creator sets their own; Know24's policy applies to subscriptions)
- Digital product delivery (instant access via download link or account)
- License grant to buyer (personal, non-commercial use; no resale; no redistribution)
- Chatbot usage terms (if applicable): fair use, no abusive queries, no attempts to extract training data
- Expert Engine terms (if applicable): API usage limits, prohibited use cases
- Disclaimer: products are educational; not professional advice (legal, medical, financial)
- Acceptable use of purchased content
- Dispute resolution: reference creator's contact for issues; Know24 dispute escalation path
- Governing law (creator's jurisdiction)

*Know24 provides this as a template with `[CREATOR NAME]`, `[WEBSITE]`, `[JURISDICTION]` placeholders. Creator customizes before going live.*

### 3. Privacy Policy

*Covers all Know24 platform users (creators and buyers).*

Sections (detailed requirements in GDPR section above). Key additions:
- Make clear it covers both: (a) creator accounts on know24.com and (b) buyer interactions with creator storefronts powered by Know24
- Document that buyer data is processed by Know24 as a Data Processor on behalf of the Creator (Data Controller)
- CCPA-specific section with California rights and Do Not Sell link
- Last updated date prominently displayed
- Version history maintained

### 4. Cookie Policy

- List every cookie name, purpose, duration, and whether it is first-party or third-party
- Explain the categories (strictly necessary, analytics, marketing)
- Link to each third party's own cookie/privacy policy
- Instructions for managing cookies in major browsers
- Note that opting out of analytics cookies may affect the quality of creator analytics dashboards

### 5. Acceptable Use Policy

*Referenced from ToS; enforced independently.*

Prohibited uses of Know24:
- Creating, hosting, or distributing illegal content
- Copyright or trademark infringement
- Impersonation of other creators or persons
- Spam or unsolicited commercial communication (beyond opt-in lists)
- Creating products that make false medical, legal, or financial claims
- Using the platform to conduct unauthorized data harvesting of other creators or their customers
- Interfering with platform security or other users' access (hacking, scraping, DDoS)
- Using AI features to generate content that violates third-party rights
- Creating adult content on storefronts accessible to minors
- Discrimination-based targeting or content
- Money laundering or using Know24 for fraudulent financial schemes
- Circumventing Stripe Connect's prohibited business categories

Enforcement:
- First violation (non-material): warning + 7-day cure period
- Second violation or material first violation: immediate suspension pending review
- Severe violations (illegal content, fraud): immediate permanent termination + law enforcement referral

### 6. Data Processing Agreement

*Formal DPA between Know24 (Processor) and Creator (Controller).*

Required elements per GDPR Article 28:
- Duration mirrors the subscription term
- Nature and purpose: operating the Know24 platform to provide the creator's business services
- Type of personal data: buyer names, emails, purchase history, IP addresses (transient)
- Categories of data subjects: the creator's end customers
- Know24's obligations as processor (full list per Article 28(3)(a)-(h))
- Sub-processor list with current version (must notify creator 14 days before adding new sub-processors)
- Assistance with data subject rights: Know24 will assist the creator in responding to data subject requests within 72 hours of receiving notice
- Security measures: encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, audit logging, backup
- Audit rights: creator may request annual audit report; Know24 responds with security questionnaire within 30 days
- Data deletion: at end of relationship, Know24 deletes all buyer data within 30 days unless instructed to export
- Standard Contractual Clauses for EU → US transfers (Module 2: Controller to Processor)
- Governing law: GDPR and applicable national law

### 7. Creator Agreement (Revenue Share & Content Ownership)

*Separate from ToS for clarity; may be combined in ToS.*

Key provisions:
- Content ownership: creators own all content they input and all AI-generated outputs Know24 creates from their inputs
- Know24 license: creator grants Know24 a non-exclusive license to process, store, and display content for service delivery; no right to resell or sublicense
- AI training: Know24 does not use creator content to train AI models (this is a strong differentiator; commit to it explicitly)
- Revenue mechanics:
  - Creator subscriptions: $99/month or $199/month (Scout) billed to Know24
  - Product sales: processed directly by Stripe Connect to creator's connected account
  - Platform fee on product sales: **none** (Know24's revenue is the subscription fee only — confirm this model; if there is a transaction fee, document the exact percentage here)
  - Chargebacks: creator is responsible for chargebacks on their product sales; Know24 will assist in dispute evidence collection
- Payout schedule: Stripe Connect standard (2-business-day rolling payouts to creator's bank)
- Creator's responsibilities: accuracy of product content, compliance with applicable laws in their jurisdiction, tax obligations on their product revenue
- Stripe Connect requirements: creator must complete Stripe identity verification (KYC)
- Know24's right to remove products that violate AUP even if creator disputes the decision

### 8. Refund Policy

*For Know24 subscriptions (not for creator product sales, which creators control).*

Know24 subscription refunds:
- 14-day money-back guarantee from first charge
- No refunds after 14 days for annual plans that have been used
- If the platform has a material outage of more than 24 hours: pro-rated credit for affected period
- Scout add-on: same 14-day guarantee, pro-rated cancellation for unused scans if cancelled mid-month
- Process: request via support@know24.com or in-app support; processed within 5 business days

Creator product refunds:
- Each creator sets their own refund policy (documented in their ToS to buyers)
- Know24 does not intermediate creator-to-buyer refunds; these are handled directly via Stripe
- Know24 provides creators with a recommended refund policy template

### 9. DMCA / Copyright Policy

*Know24 hosts creator-generated content and is entitled to DMCA safe harbor under 17 U.S.C. § 512.*

Requirements for safe harbor maintenance:
- Designated DMCA agent registered with the US Copyright Office (required)
- Agent contact information posted at `know24.com/legal/dmca` and in the Privacy Policy
- Repeat infringer policy: creators who receive three valid DMCA takedown notices will have their account terminated

Takedown process:
1. Copyright owner sends written notice to `dmca@know24.com` with: identification of the copyrighted work; identification of the infringing material with URL; good faith statement; accuracy statement under penalty of perjury; signature
2. Know24 processes valid notices within 5 business days: removes or disables access to the content; notifies the creator
3. Creator may submit a counter-notice within 14 days
4. Know24 restores content 10-14 business days after counter-notice unless copyright owner files suit

AI-generated content consideration: if a third party claims that Know24's AI output infringes their copyright, Know24's response policy is to: (a) evaluate the claim; (b) remove or modify the content if the claim has merit; (c) notify the creator. This is uncharted legal territory — document the policy clearly and review with counsel.

---

## 5.4 DevOps & CI/CD

### GitHub Repository Setup

**Repository Structure**

```
know24/
├── .github/
│   ├── workflows/
│   │   ├── ci.yml
│   │   ├── deploy-staging.yml
│   │   ├── deploy-production.yml
│   │   └── dependency-audit.yml
│   ├── PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
│   └── dependabot.yml
├── app/                    # Next.js App Router
├── components/
├── lib/
├── workers/                # Railway background workers
├── supabase/
│   ├── migrations/         # Database migrations
│   └── seed.sql
├── e2e/                    # Playwright tests
├── tests/                  # Vitest unit + integration tests
├── public/
├── .env.example
├── next.config.js
├── package.json
└── README.md
```

**Branch Strategy**

```
main          ← Production. Protected. Only accepts PRs. Auto-deploys to Vercel production.
staging       ← Staging. Protected. Only accepts PRs from feature branches. Auto-deploys to Vercel preview.
feature/*     ← Feature development. Branch from staging. Name: feature/[ticket-id]-brief-description
fix/*         ← Bug fixes. Branch from staging (or main for hotfixes).
hotfix/*      ← Critical production fixes only. Branch from main. Merge to both main and staging.
chore/*       ← Non-feature work (dependency updates, config changes).
```

**Branch Protection Rules (GitHub Settings → Branches)**

For `main`:
- Require pull request reviews: 1 approving review required
- Require status checks to pass: `ci / type-check`, `ci / lint`, `ci / test`, `ci / build`
- Require branches to be up to date before merging
- Include administrators: Yes (no bypassing protection)
- Restrict pushes: only GitHub Actions can push directly (for release tagging)
- Require signed commits: Yes

For `staging`:
- Require pull request reviews: 1 approving review required
- Require status checks to pass: `ci / type-check`, `ci / lint`, `ci / test`, `ci / build`
- Allow squash merging; disallow merge commits (keep history clean)

**PR Template**

```markdown
<!-- .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md -->
## Summary
<!-- 1-3 sentences: what does this PR do and why? -->

## Type of Change
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Dependency update
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Configuration / infrastructure

## Related Issues
Closes #<!-- issue number -->

## Changes Made
<!-- Bullet list of specific changes -->
-
-

## Testing Done
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E tests added/updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Tested manually in local dev

## Screenshots / Videos
<!-- For UI changes, before/after screenshots -->

## Database Changes
- [ ] Includes migration file
- [ ] Migration is backwards-compatible
- [ ] Migration tested on a copy of production data

## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validated with Zod
- [ ] Authorization checked (user owns the resource)
- [ ] No PII in logs
- [ ] No secrets in code

## Accessibility
- [ ] Keyboard navigable
- [ ] Screen reader tested (if UI change)
- [ ] Color contrast checked

## Deployment Notes
<!-- Anything ops needs to know: new env vars, feature flags, required migrations, etc. -->
```

**Commit Message Format (Conventional Commits)**

```
<type>(<scope>): <short description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

Types:
- `feat` — new feature
- `fix` — bug fix
- `docs` — documentation only
- `style` — formatting, no logic change
- `refactor` — code change that neither fixes nor adds a feature
- `perf` — performance improvement
- `test` — adding or correcting tests
- `chore` — build process, dependency updates
- `ci` — CI/CD changes
- `revert` — reverts a previous commit

Examples:
```
feat(products): add PDF generation for guide product type
fix(checkout): resolve stripe connect redirect loop on mobile
perf(knowledge): batch embedding generation to reduce OpenAI calls
chore(deps): bump next from 15.1.0 to 15.2.0
```

Enforce with `commitlint` + `husky` pre-commit hooks:

```json
// package.json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "husky"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@commitlint/cli": "^19.0.0",
    "@commitlint/config-conventional": "^19.0.0",
    "husky": "^9.0.0",
    "lint-staged": "^15.0.0"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": ["eslint --fix", "prettier --write"],
    "*.{json,md,css}": ["prettier --write"]
  }
}
```

---

### GitHub Actions Workflows

**1. CI Pipeline — Runs on Every PR**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, staging]
  push:
    branches: [main, staging]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '20'
  PNPM_VERSION: '9'

jobs:
  type-check:
    name: TypeScript
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Type check
        run: pnpm tsc --noEmit

  lint:
    name: Lint & Format
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: ESLint
        run: pnpm eslint . --max-warnings=0
      - name: Prettier check
        run: pnpm prettier --check .

  test:
    name: Unit & Integration Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: supabase/postgres:15.1.1.41
        env:
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
          POSTGRES_DB: know24_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/know24_test
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: http://localhost:54321
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: test-anon-key
      SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: test-service-role-key
      CLERK_SECRET_KEY: sk_test_xxxx
      NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: pk_test_xxxx
      STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: sk_test_xxxx
      UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL: ${{ secrets.TEST_UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL }}
      UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TEST_UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Run migrations on test DB
        run: pnpm supabase db push --db-url $DATABASE_URL
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: pnpm vitest run --reporter=verbose --coverage
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          fail_ci_if_error: false

  build:
    name: Build Verification
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: pk_test_placeholder
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: https://placeholder.supabase.co
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: placeholder-anon-key
      NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: pk_test_placeholder
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Build
        run: pnpm build
      - name: Check bundle size
        uses: andresz1/size-limit-action@v1
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          skip_step: install
        continue-on-error: true  # Warning only, not a blocking failure

  security:
    name: Security Audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: npm audit
        run: pnpm audit --audit-level=high
        continue-on-error: true  # Report, don't block (Dependabot handles remediation)
```

**2. Staging Deployment — On Merge to `staging`**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  push:
    branches: [staging]

concurrency:
  group: deploy-staging
  cancel-in-progress: false  # Never cancel an in-progress staging deploy

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Run CI checks
    uses: ./.github/workflows/ci.yml
    secrets: inherit

  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Vercel Preview
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      preview-url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.preview-url }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Deploy to Vercel (staging)
        id: deploy
        uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
        with:
          vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
          vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
          vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID }}
          scope: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
          working-directory: ./
          alias-domains: staging.know24.com

  migrate-staging:
    name: Run DB Migrations (Staging)
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: supabase/setup-cli@v1
        with:
          version: latest
      - name: Run migrations on staging database
        run: |
          supabase db push \
            --db-url ${{ secrets.STAGING_DATABASE_URL }} \
            --include-all
        env:
          SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN }}

  e2e:
    name: E2E Tests (Playwright)
    needs: [deploy, migrate-staging]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: pnpm playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: pnpm playwright test
        env:
          BASE_URL: ${{ needs.deploy.outputs.preview-url }}
          TEST_CREATOR_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_CREATOR_EMAIL }}
          TEST_CREATOR_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_CREATOR_PASSWORD }}
          TEST_STRIPE_CARD: 4242424242424242
      - name: Upload Playwright report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: playwright-report-staging
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 7

  notify-staging:
    name: Notify Slack
    needs: [e2e]
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify Slack
        uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1.26.0
        with:
          payload: |
            {
              "text": "${{ needs.e2e.result == 'success' && '✅' || '❌' }} *Staging Deploy* ${{ needs.e2e.result }}",
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "type": "section",
                  "text": {
                    "type": "mrkdwn",
                    "text": "${{ needs.e2e.result == 'success' && ':white_check_mark:' || ':x:' }} *Staging deployment ${{ needs.e2e.result }}*\n<${{ github.event.head_commit.url }}|${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}>\nPreview: <${{ needs.deploy.outputs.preview-url }}|View staging>"
                  }
                }
              ]
            }
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_TYPE: INCOMING_WEBHOOK
```

**3. Production Deployment — On Merge to `main`**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml
name: Deploy to Production

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: deploy-production
  cancel-in-progress: false  # NEVER cancel a production deployment

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Run CI checks
    uses: ./.github/workflows/ci.yml
    secrets: inherit

  migrate-production:
    name: Deploy DB Migrations (Production)
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production  # Requires manual approval gate in GitHub Environments
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: supabase/setup-cli@v1
        with:
          version: latest
      - name: Run migrations on production database
        run: |
          supabase db push \
            --db-url ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_DATABASE_URL }} \
            --include-all
        env:
          SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy to Vercel Production
    needs: [migrate-production]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    outputs:
      deployment-url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.preview-url }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Deploy to Vercel Production
        id: deploy
        uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
        with:
          vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
          vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
          vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID }}
          vercel-args: '--prod'
          scope: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}

  smoke-test:
    name: Production Smoke Tests
    needs: [deploy-production]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Install Playwright
        run: pnpm playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Run smoke tests
        run: pnpm playwright test --project=smoke
        env:
          BASE_URL: https://know24.com
      # Smoke tests verify: homepage loads, sign-in page loads,
      # /api/health returns 200, storefront sample loads, checkout page loads

  tag-release:
    name: Tag Release
    needs: [smoke-test]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Get version from package.json
        id: pkg
        run: echo "version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - name: Create Release Tag
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const version = '${{ steps.pkg.outputs.version }}';
            const sha = context.sha;
            const date = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
            const tag = `v${version}-${date.replace(/-/g, '')}`;
            
            await github.rest.git.createRef({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              ref: `refs/tags/${tag}`,
              sha: sha,
            });
            
            await github.rest.repos.createRelease({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              tag_name: tag,
              name: `Release ${tag}`,
              generate_release_notes: true,
              prerelease: false,
            });
            
            console.log(`Tagged release: ${tag}`);

  notify-production:
    name: Notify Slack
    needs: [smoke-test, tag-release]
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify Slack
        uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1.26.0
        with:
          payload: |
            {
              "text": "${{ needs.smoke-test.result == 'success' && '🚀' || '🔥' }} *Production Deploy* ${{ needs.smoke-test.result }}",
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "type": "section",
                  "text": {
                    "type": "mrkdwn",
                    "text": "${{ needs.smoke-test.result == 'success' && ':rocket:' || ':fire:' }} *Production deployment ${{ needs.smoke-test.result }}*\n<${{ github.event.head_commit.url }}|${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}>\n<https://know24.com|View production>"
                  }
                }
              ]
            }
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_TYPE: INCOMING_WEBHOOK
```

**4. Dependency Audit — Weekly**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/dependency-audit.yml
name: Weekly Dependency Audit

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 * * 1'  # Every Monday at 9:00 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  npm-audit:
    name: npm Audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Run pnpm audit
        run: pnpm audit --audit-level=moderate --json > audit-results.json || true
      - name: Parse audit results
        id: audit
        run: |
          HIGH=$(cat audit-results.json | jq '.metadata.vulnerabilities.high // 0')
          CRITICAL=$(cat audit-results.json | jq '.metadata.vulnerabilities.critical // 0')
          echo "high=$HIGH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "critical=$CRITICAL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - name: Notify Slack if vulnerabilities found
        if: steps.audit.outputs.critical != '0' || steps.audit.outputs.high != '0'
        uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1.26.0
        with:
          payload: |
            {
              "text": ":warning: *Dependency Vulnerabilities Found*\nCritical: ${{ steps.audit.outputs.critical }} | High: ${{ steps.audit.outputs.high }}\n<https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/security/dependabot|View Dependabot alerts>"
            }
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_TYPE: INCOMING_WEBHOOK
      - name: Fail if critical vulnerabilities exist
        if: steps.audit.outputs.critical != '0'
        run: |
          echo "CRITICAL vulnerabilities found! Immediate action required."
          exit 1

  license-check:
    name: License Compliance Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: '9'
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Check licenses
        run: |
          npx license-checker --onlyAllow "MIT;ISC;BSD-2-Clause;BSD-3-Clause;Apache-2.0;0BSD;CC0-1.0;Unlicense;Python-2.0;CC-BY-4.0" \
            --excludePrivatePackages \
            --json > license-report.json
      - name: Upload license report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: license-report
          path: license-report.json
          retention-days: 30
```

### Environment Management

**Three Environments:**

| Environment | Vercel | Database | Branch | URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Development | Local (`next dev`) | Local Supabase (`supabase start`) | Any | `localhost:3000` |
| Staging | Vercel Preview | Staging Supabase project | `staging` | `staging.know24.com` |
| Production | Vercel Production | Production Supabase project | `main` | `know24.com` |

**Local Development Setup**

```bash
# One-time setup
pnpm install
cp .env.example .env.local
supabase start              # Starts local Postgres + Auth + Storage
supabase db push            # Applies migrations to local DB
pnpm dev
```

Local `.env.local` uses:
- Local Supabase URL: `http://localhost:54321`
- Stripe test mode keys: `sk_test_...`, `pk_test_...`
- Clerk development keys: `sk_test_...`, `pk_test_...`
- Test OpenAI/Anthropic keys (separate project with budget limits)
- Real Upstash Redis in test mode (or local Redis via Docker)

**Environment Variables by Environment**

All secrets managed in Vercel Dashboard → Project → Environment Variables. Set each variable to apply to "Production", "Preview", or "All Environments" as appropriate.

```
Variable                              Development    Staging       Production
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL                   localhost:3000 staging.k24   know24.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY     pk_test_...    pk_test_...   pk_live_...
CLERK_SECRET_KEY                      sk_test_...    sk_test_...   sk_live_...
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL              localhost:54321 staging-url  prod-url
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY             local-key      staging-key   prod-key
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY                     sk_test_...    sk_test_...   sk_live_...
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY    pk_test_...    pk_test_...   pk_live_...
OPENAI_API_KEY                        test-key       staging-key   prod-key
SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT                    development    staging       production
LOG_LEVEL                             debug          info          warn
FEATURE_SCOUT                         true           true          true
FEATURE_EXPERT_ENGINE                 true           false         false  ← Phase 2
```

Feature flags (prefixed `FEATURE_`) allow progressive rollout: enable on staging before production, without code changes.

## 5.5 Monitoring & Observability

### Error Tracking (Sentry)

**Installation and Configuration**

```bash
pnpm add @sentry/nextjs
npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs
```

```javascript
// sentry.client.config.ts
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs';

Sentry.init({
  dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
  environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT || 'development',
  
  // Performance monitoring
  tracesSampleRate: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT === 'production' ? 0.1 : 1.0,
  // 10% sampling in production (adjust based on volume & cost)
  
  // Session replay for error investigation
  replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0,   // Always capture replay on errors
  replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.01,  // 1% of normal sessions
  
  integrations: [
    Sentry.replayIntegration({
      maskAllText: true,    // PII protection: mask all text in replays
      blockAllMedia: false,
    }),
    Sentry.browserTracingIntegration(),
  ],
  
  // Release tracking — inject version from CI
  release: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_RELEASE,
  
  // Filter out noise
  ignoreErrors: [
    'ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded',
    'Network request failed',      // Handled in UI; not actionable in Sentry
    'ChunkLoadError',               // Deployment-related; user needs to refresh
  ],
  
  beforeSend(event, hint) {
    // Strip PII from events
    if (event.user) {
      delete event.user.email;     // Don't send email to Sentry
      delete event.user.ip_address; // Or IP
    }
    return event;
  },
});
```

```javascript
// sentry.server.config.ts
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs';

Sentry.init({
  dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
  environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT,
  tracesSampleRate: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT === 'production' ? 0.05 : 1.0,
  release: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_RELEASE,
  
  integrations: [
    Sentry.prismaIntegration(),   // or supabase integration if available
  ],
});
```

```javascript
// sentry.edge.config.ts
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs';

Sentry.init({
  dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
  environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT,
  tracesSampleRate: 0.05,
});
```

**Custom Error Boundaries**

```typescript
// components/error-boundary/AppErrorBoundary.tsx
'use client';

import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs';
import { useEffect } from 'react';

export default function GlobalError({
  error,
  reset,
}: {
  error: Error & { digest?: string };
  reset: () => void;
}) {
  useEffect(() => {
    Sentry.captureException(error, {
      tags: {
        errorBoundary: 'global',
        digest: error.digest,
      },
    });
  }, [error]);

  return (
    <div className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center">
      <div className="text-center max-w-md">
        <h1 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-2">Something went wrong</h1>
        <p className="text-muted-foreground mb-4">We've been notified and are looking into it.</p>
        <button onClick={reset} className="btn-primary">Try again</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

**Release Tracking**

In production deployment workflow, set the Sentry release before deploying:

```yaml
# In deploy-production.yml, before the Vercel deploy step:
- name: Create Sentry release
  uses: getsentry/action-release@v1
  env:
    SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
    SENTRY_ORG: know24
    SENTRY_PROJECT: know24-nextjs
  with:
    environment: production
    version: ${{ steps.pkg.outputs.version }}
    sourcemaps: '.next'
    url_prefix: '~/_next'
    set_commits: auto
```

**Alert Rules (Specific Rules)**

Configure in Sentry → Alerts → Alert Rules:

```
Rule 1: Critical — New error spike
  Condition: Number of events > 50 in 5 minutes (new issue)
  Filter: environment = production
  Action: Notify via email (admin) + Slack #alerts-critical
  
Rule 2: Critical — Regression
  Condition: A previously resolved issue has been seen again
  Filter: environment = production
  Action: Notify via email + Slack #alerts-critical

Rule 3: Warning — High error rate
  Condition: Error rate exceeds 1% of sessions in 15 minutes
  Filter: environment = production
  Action: Slack #alerts-warning

Rule 4: Warning — Payment errors
  Condition: Any event with tag payment = failed occurs > 5 times in 10 minutes
  Filter: environment = production
  Action: Slack #alerts-critical (payment failures are always critical)

Rule 5: Performance — Slow AI generation
  Condition: P95 latency for transaction 'ai.product.generate' > 30s
  Action: Slack #alerts-warning

Rule 6: Performance — Slow page loads
  Condition: P95 LCP > 4000ms on any route in last 30 minutes
  Action: Slack #alerts-warning

Rule 7: Info — Weekly digest
  Condition: Scheduled weekly summary
  Action: Email admin with top 10 issues, error trends, performance summary
```

### Application Monitoring

**Vercel Analytics**

Enabled automatically via `@vercel/analytics` package. Provides:
- Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS, TTFB, FCP) per page
- Unique visitors, page views, top pages
- Geographic distribution of traffic
- Privacy-first: no cookies, no PII tracking

```typescript
// app/layout.tsx
import { Analytics } from '@vercel/analytics/react';
import { SpeedInsights } from '@vercel/speed-insights/next';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <Analytics />           {/* Page view tracking */}
        <SpeedInsights />       {/* Real user performance metrics */}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

**Custom Metrics to Track**

Use a custom metrics table in Supabase with aggregation queries, plus Sentry performance transactions for real-time metrics.

```typescript
// lib/metrics.ts
interface MetricEvent {
  metric: string;
  value: number;
  unit: string;
  tags?: Record<string, string>;
  businessId?: string;
  userId?: string;
}

export async function recordMetric(event: MetricEvent) {
  // For Sentry performance transactions (real-time alerting):
  Sentry.metrics.increment(event.metric, event.value, { tags: event.tags });
  
  // For dashboard analytics (aggregated):
  await supabase.from('metric_events').insert({
    metric: event.metric,
    value: event.value,
    unit: event.unit,
    tags: event.tags,
    business_id: event.businessId,
    user_id: event.userId,
    recorded_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  });
}
```

**Metrics to implement across the application:**

```typescript
// Signups (call on successful Clerk user creation webhook)
recordMetric({ metric: 'signups.total', value: 1, unit: 'count' });

// Onboarding completion rate (call when onboarding step completes)
recordMetric({ metric: 'onboarding.step_completed', value: 1, unit: 'count',
  tags: { step: 'knowledge_intake' | 'ai_analysis' | 'product_selection' | 'ai_build' | 'go_live' } });

// Time to first product (ms from signup to first product created)
recordMetric({ metric: 'onboarding.time_to_first_product_ms', value: durationMs, unit: 'ms' });

// AI generation success/failure
recordMetric({ metric: 'ai.generation.result', value: 1, unit: 'count',
  tags: { product_type: productType, status: 'success' | 'failure', provider: 'openai' | 'anthropic' } });

// AI generation latency
recordMetric({ metric: 'ai.generation.latency_ms', value: durationMs, unit: 'ms',
  tags: { product_type: productType, provider: 'openai' } });

// API latency (middleware, per endpoint)
recordMetric({ metric: 'api.latency_ms', value: durationMs, unit: 'ms',
  tags: { route: '/api/products/generate', method: 'POST', status: '200' } });

// Background job completion
recordMetric({ metric: 'jobs.result', value: 1, unit: 'count',
  tags: { job_name: 'scout-scan', status: 'completed' | 'failed' } });

// Stripe payment success rate
recordMetric({ metric: 'payments.result', value: 1, unit: 'count',
  tags: { type: 'subscription' | 'product', status: 'succeeded' | 'failed' } });

// Scout scan completion
recordMetric({ metric: 'scout.scan.result', value: 1, unit: 'count',
  tags: { status: 'completed' | 'failed', opportunities_found: opportunityCount.toString() } });

// Storefront page load (via Web Vitals in Vercel Analytics — automatic)
// Additional custom metric for chatbot response time:
recordMetric({ metric: 'chatbot.response_latency_ms', value: durationMs, unit: 'ms',
  tags: { business_id: businessId } });
```

**Dashboard queries for internal metrics:**

```sql
-- Weekly signup funnel
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('week', recorded_at) as week,
  metric,
  SUM(value) as total
FROM metric_events
WHERE metric LIKE 'onboarding.%'
  AND recorded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1 DESC, 2;

-- AI generation success rate (last 7 days)
SELECT
  tags->>'provider' as provider,
  tags->>'product_type' as product_type,
  SUM(CASE WHEN tags->>'status' = 'success' THEN value ELSE 0 END) as successes,
  SUM(value) as total,
  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN tags->>'status' = 'success' THEN value ELSE 0 END)::numeric / SUM(value) * 100, 1) as success_rate
FROM metric_events
WHERE metric = 'ai.generation.result'
  AND recorded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY 1, 2;

-- API latency percentiles (last 1 hour)
SELECT
  tags->>'route' as route,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p50_ms,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p95_ms,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY value) as p99_ms,
  COUNT(*) as request_count
FROM metric_events
WHERE metric = 'api.latency_ms'
  AND recorded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY p95_ms DESC;
```

### Uptime Monitoring

**Health Check Endpoints**

```typescript
// app/api/health/route.ts
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase/server';

export async function GET() {
  const checks = {
    status: 'ok' as 'ok' | 'degraded' | 'down',
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    version: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_RELEASE || 'unknown',
    checks: {
      database: 'unknown' as 'ok' | 'error',
      redis: 'unknown' as 'ok' | 'error',
    },
  };

  // Check database connectivity
  try {
    const supabase = createClient();
    await supabase.from('businesses').select('id').limit(1);
    checks.checks.database = 'ok';
  } catch {
    checks.checks.database = 'error';
    checks.status = 'degraded';
  }

  // Check Redis connectivity
  try {
    const { redis } = await import('@/lib/redis');
    await redis.ping();
    checks.checks.redis = 'ok';
  } catch {
    checks.checks.redis = 'error';
    checks.status = 'degraded';
  }

  const statusCode = checks.status === 'ok' ? 200 : 503;
  return NextResponse.json(checks, { status: statusCode });
}

// Deep health check (authenticated — for internal monitoring only)
// app/api/health/deep/route.ts — checks AI provider connectivity,
// Stripe API reachability, R2 connectivity, background worker queue depth
```

**External Uptime Monitoring**

Use **BetterUptime** (or **Checkly** — preferred for developer-centric teams):

Monitors to configure:

| Check | URL | Frequency | Alert Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | `https://know24.com` | 1 min | 2 consecutive failures |
| API Health | `https://know24.com/api/health` | 1 min | 1 failure |
| Sample Storefront | `https://know24.com/s/demo` | 5 min | 2 consecutive failures |
| Checkout flow | Playwright check via Checkly | 10 min | 1 failure |

Alert routing:
- First failure: email to admin
- 2 consecutive failures: SMS to admin + Slack #alerts-critical
- 5+ minute outage: incident auto-created in BetterUptime, status page updated

**Status Page**

Host at `status.know24.com` using BetterUptime's built-in status page. Components to display:
- Platform (dashboard)
- Creator Storefronts
- Payment Processing
- AI Services
- Email Delivery
- API

Update manually for planned maintenance. Auto-updates on incident detection and resolution. Creators can subscribe to status updates.

### Logging

**Structured Logging Format (JSON)**

```typescript
// lib/logger.ts
type LogLevel = 'error' | 'warn' | 'info' | 'debug';

interface LogEntry {
  level: LogLevel;
  message: string;
  timestamp: string;
  requestId?: string;
  userId?: string;
  businessId?: string;
  route?: string;
  duration?: number;
  error?: {
    name: string;
    message: string;
    stack?: string;   // Only in development
  };
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

const LOG_LEVEL_ORDER: Record<LogLevel, number> = {
  error: 0, warn: 1, info: 2, debug: 3,
};

const currentLevel = (process.env.LOG_LEVEL as LogLevel) || 'info';

function log(level: LogLevel, message: string, meta: Record<string, unknown> = {}) {
  if (LOG_LEVEL_ORDER[level] > LOG_LEVEL_ORDER[currentLevel]) return;

  const entry: LogEntry = {
    level,
    message,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    ...meta,
  };

  // Never log PII — strip it before outputting
  delete entry.email;
  delete entry.password;
  delete entry.token;
  delete entry.cardNumber;
  delete entry.ssn;

  console[level === 'debug' ? 'log' : level](JSON.stringify(entry));
}

export const logger = {
  error: (msg: string, meta?: Record<string, unknown>) => log('error', msg, meta),
  warn: (msg: string, meta?: Record<string, unknown>) => log('warn', msg, meta),
  info: (msg: string, meta?: Record<string, unknown>) => log('info', msg, meta),
  debug: (msg: string, meta?: Record<string, unknown>) => log('debug', msg, meta),
};
```

**What to Log**

```typescript
// API requests (middleware)
logger.info('API request', {
  route: req.nextUrl.pathname,
  method: req.method,
  userId: userId || 'anonymous',
  statusCode: res.status,
  duration: Date.now() - startTime,
  requestId: req.headers.get('x-request-id'),
});

// AI operations
logger.info('AI generation started', {
  operation: 'product.generate',
  productType,
  provider: 'openai',
  businessId,
  userId,
  requestId,
});
logger.info('AI generation completed', {
  operation: 'product.generate',
  duration: Date.now() - start,
  tokensUsed: usage.total_tokens,
  businessId,
  userId,
});

// Payment events
logger.info('Payment processed', {
  event: 'subscription.payment_succeeded',
  stripeCustomerId,      // Not card data — just customer ID
  subscriptionId,
  amount: amountCents,
  currency,
  businessId,
});

// Auth events
logger.info('User signed in', { userId, method: 'email' | 'google' });
logger.warn('Rate limit exceeded', { userId, endpoint, limit, window });
logger.warn('Unauthorized access attempt', { route, userId: userId || 'anonymous', ip: ipAddress });
```

**What NOT to Log**

- Email addresses
- Names or other PII beyond user IDs
- Passwords or password hashes
- Session tokens, JWTs, API keys
- Card numbers, CVVs, expiry dates, full PANs
- Social Security Numbers or government IDs
- Full request bodies (log only validated/sanitized fields)
- OpenAI/Anthropic API keys or response metadata containing billing info
- Webhook raw payloads containing customer financial data

**Log Retention Policy**

| Log Type | Retention |
|---|---|
| Application logs (error, warn) | 90 days |
| Application logs (info) | 30 days |
| Application logs (debug) | 7 days (development only) |
| Security event logs (auth, rate limits, suspicious activity) | 1 year |
| Audit logs (see Section 5.2 SOC 2) | 2 years |
| Vercel function logs | 3 days (Vercel default; export to Datadog/Logtail if longer needed) |

For longer retention: configure Vercel Log Drains to ship logs to **Logtail** (Better Stack) or **Axiom**. Logtail offers structured log search with 30-day retention on free tier, 90 days on paid.

---

### Alerting

**Alert Matrix**

| Severity | Condition | Channels | Response Time |
|---|---|---|---|
| Critical | Payment processing failures > 3 in 5 min | Email + SMS + Slack #alerts-critical | Immediate |
| Critical | Auth system (Clerk) errors → 5xx responses | Email + SMS + Slack #alerts-critical | Immediate |
| Critical | Database connection errors | Email + SMS + Slack #alerts-critical | Immediate |
| Critical | API health check fails 2x consecutive | Email + SMS + Slack #alerts-critical + PagerDuty | Immediate |
| Critical | Any production deploy fails | Slack #alerts-critical | Review within 1 hour |
| Warning | Error rate > 1% of requests in 15 min | Slack #alerts-warning | Review within 2 hours |
| Warning | AI response latency P95 > 30 seconds | Slack #alerts-warning | Review within 4 hours |
| Warning | Approaching AI token usage limit (80%) | Slack #alerts-warning + email | Review within 24 hours |
| Warning | Background job queue depth > 500 jobs | Slack #alerts-warning | Review within 2 hours |
| Warning | Failed email delivery rate > 5% | Slack #alerts-warning | Review within 4 hours |
| Info | New creator signup | Slack #metrics | No action required |
| Info | Creator reaches first paid sale | Slack #metrics | No action required |
| Info | Successful weekly deploy | Slack #deployments | No action required |

**Notification Channels**

- **Email**: admin@know24.com (and optionally a distribution group)
- **Slack**: `#alerts-critical`, `#alerts-warning`, `#metrics`, `#deployments`
- **SMS**: Use PagerDuty or OpsGenie (free tier) for critical-severity SMS/phone escalation
- **Status page**: BetterUptime auto-updates on critical health check failures

---

## 5.6 Backup & Disaster Recovery

### Backup Strategy

**Database — Supabase Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR)**

- Supabase Pro plan includes PITR with 7-day recovery window
- Continuous WAL (Write-Ahead Log) streaming to S3; recovery granularity: 1 second
- To restore: Supabase Dashboard → Database → Backups → Select point in time → Restore to new project → Verify → Swap connection strings

**Daily Logical Backups**

In addition to PITR, run daily `pg_dump` for offline backup copies:

```sql
-- supabase/functions/daily-backup/index.ts (Supabase Edge Function, scheduled daily)
-- Or as a Railway cron job:
-- SCHEDULE: 0 3 * * * (3 AM UTC daily)

const dumpResult = await exec(
  `pg_dump ${DATABASE_URL} --format=custom --no-owner > /tmp/backup-${date}.dump`
);
// Upload to R2 bucket: know24-db-backups/daily/
// Retain 90 days; auto-expire via R2 lifecycle rule
```

**File Storage — Cloudflare R2**

- R2 stores data across multiple availability zones within the region (Cloudflare-managed redundancy)
- Enable object versioning on the `know24-assets` bucket: deleted objects become versioned (recoverable for 30 days)
- Replication to a second R2 bucket in a different region for critical assets (Phase 2, when volume justifies cost)

**Code — GitHub**

Git's distributed nature means every contributor and GitHub's own infrastructure holds a full copy. No additional backup needed.

**Secrets — Last Resort Recovery**

All secrets are also recorded in a password manager (1Password) with access restricted to the founding team. In case of complete Vercel environment variable loss, secrets can be reinjected within 30 minutes.

---

### Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO)

| Scenario | RPO Target | RTO Target |
|---|---|---|
| Database failure (Supabase-managed) | 1 second (PITR) | 30 minutes (Supabase restores automatically) |
| Database corruption requiring restore | 1 hour (last daily backup if PITR unavailable) | 2 hours |
| Vercel deployment failure | N/A | 15 minutes (instant rollback to previous deployment) |
| R2 file loss (accidental deletion) | 24 hours (versioning) | 1 hour |
| Complete cloud provider outage (Supabase) | 24 hours | 4 hours (restore to alternate provider using logical backup) |
| Security breach requiring full rebuild | 24 hours | 8 hours |

**Current SLA target for creators**: 99.5% uptime (4.38 hours downtime/year). Achievable on Vercel + Supabase infrastructure. This is the implicit commitment; document it in ToS.

---

### Incident Response Procedure

**Severity Levels**

| Level | Definition | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| P1 — Critical | Complete service unavailability or active security breach | Site down, database unreachable, payment system down, data breach |
| P2 — High | Major feature broken affecting many users | AI generation failing for all users, checkout broken, dashboard inaccessible |
| P3 — Medium | Degraded performance or partial feature failure | Slow AI generation, Scout scans delayed, email delivery issues |
| P4 — Low | Minor issues affecting few users | UI bug, minor analytics discrepancy, edge case error |

**P1 Incident Response Steps**

```
T+0:00   Alert fires (Sentry / BetterUptime / PagerDuty)
T+0:05   On-call engineer acknowledges in Slack
T+0:05   Status page updated: "Investigating issue with [component]"
T+0:15   Root cause identified OR escalation to Supabase/Vercel/Stripe support
T+0:30   Fix deployed OR workaround communicated to affected users
T+1:00   Service fully restored
T+1:00   Status page updated: "Resolved"
T+24h    Post-mortem document written and shared internally
T+48h    Preventive measures scoped and added to backlog
```

**Rollback Procedures**

*Vercel deployment rollback (< 5 minutes):*
```
Vercel Dashboard → Deployments → Previous deployment → Promote to Production
```
or via CLI: `vercel rollback [deployment-url] --scope=know24`

*Database migration rollback:*
All migrations must include a `down.sql` rollback script. Before any migration ships to production:
1. Test the down migration on staging
2. Keep down migration script ready in `supabase/migrations/down/`
3. If a bad migration reaches production: restore from PITR to just before the migration ran

*Feature flag kill switch:*
Set `FEATURE_[NAME]=false` in Vercel environment variables. Changes propagate to new function invocations within 30 seconds (no deployment needed).

**Communication Protocol**

- **Creator communication**: If downtime exceeds 15 minutes during business hours, post to status page. If exceeds 1 hour, email active subscribers.
- **Email template**: Direct, factual, no marketing language. Example: "We are experiencing an issue with [feature]. We expect to resolve this by [time]. We'll update you when resolved. — The Know24 Team"
- **Post-mortem format**: What happened → Impact → Root cause → Timeline → Fix applied → Prevention

---

## 5.7 Scalability Plan

### Current Architecture Capacity

At launch, with the described tech stack, the platform can handle:

| Metric | Baseline Capacity | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Concurrent dashboard users | ~500 | Vercel serverless auto-scales; limit is Supabase connection pool |
| API requests/second | ~1,000 | Vercel edge functions; Supabase Supavisor handles pooling |
| Concurrent AI generation jobs | ~50 | Gated by Railway worker count and AI provider rate limits |
| Concurrent storefront page loads | ~10,000+ | Static/CDN-served; unlimited via Cloudflare |
| Chatbot messages/minute (across all storefronts) | ~500 | Limited by OpenAI/Anthropic concurrent request limits |
| Knowledge embeddings stored | ~10M vectors | pgvector on Pro Supabase |
| File storage | Unlimited (R2) | Cloudflare R2 has no capacity limit |

### First Bottleneck: Database Connections

The first scalability constraint will be **Supabase database connection pool saturation** at approximately **300-500 active subscribers** using the dashboard concurrently.

*Why:* Each Next.js serverless function invocation creates a new connection to Postgres. With hundreds of concurrent requests, this exhausts the connection limit.

*Solution already in architecture:*
- Supabase Supavisor (connection pooler) is enabled by default on Supabase Pro. Increases effective connection limit from ~100 to ~10,000.
- Use the pooler connection string (`aws-0-us-east-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543`) instead of the direct connection string for all API routes.

*Next bottleneck after connection pooling:* AI provider rate limits (~1,000 simultaneous subscribers doing heavy AI work). Solution: implement a job queue with priority levels (new user onboarding > existing user AI workspace > background SEO blog generation).

### Scaling Strategy by Layer

**Vercel (Frontend + API Routes)**

Vercel serverless functions auto-scale to demand with no configuration. Each request spins a new function invocation. Limits:
- 1,000 concurrent executions per project (default, can be raised)
- 250MB per function
- 10-second timeout for API routes (60 seconds for Pro; use background jobs for long operations)

Scaling actions as users grow:
- 0–1,000 customers: no action needed
- 1,000–10,000 customers: move long-running AI operations to Railway workers (already designed this way); increase Vercel Pro concurrent execution limit
- 10,000+ customers: evaluate Vercel Enterprise for custom limits and dedicated infrastructure

**Supabase (Database)**

| Stage | Action |
|---|---|
| 0–500 customers | Supabase Pro: Supavisor connection pooling, PITR, 8GB database |
| 500–2,000 customers | Increase Supabase plan; add read replica for analytics queries (Supabase read replicas available in beta) |
| 2,000–10,000 customers | Enable Supabase read replicas; route dashboard analytics to read replica; tune pgvector HNSW index parameters |
| 10,000+ customers | Evaluate Supabase dedicated infrastructure or migrate to self-managed Postgres on RDS |

Connection pooling configuration:

```typescript
// lib/supabase/server.ts
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';

// Use Supavisor URL (pooler) for transaction-mode connections in API routes
const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
const supabaseKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!;

export function createServerClient() {
  return createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey, {
    db: { schema: 'public' },
    auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false },
    global: {
      fetch: fetch.bind(globalThis),
    },
  });
}
```

**Railway Workers (Background Jobs)**

Railway supports horizontal scaling of worker containers:

- 0–100 customers: 1 worker instance (Scout + AI generation + email sending)
- 100–500 customers: 2 worker instances; split queues (ai-heavy vs. lightweight)
- 500–2,000 customers: 4–8 worker instances; dedicated Scout workers (CPU-intensive web scraping) vs. AI generation workers vs. email workers
- 2,000+ customers: Kubernetes-managed worker fleet (Railway supports this in its Pro tier); auto-scale based on BullMQ queue depth

Queue management as users grow:

```typescript
// workers/queues.ts
// Priority tiers for fair resource allocation
const queues = {
  critical: new Queue('jobs:critical', { redis }),   // Onboarding AI generation — highest priority
  standard: new Queue('jobs:standard', { redis }),   // AI Workspace, product edits
  batch: new Queue('jobs:batch', { redis }),          // Scout scans, SEO blog posts — runs during off-peak
};
```

**Upstash Redis**

Upstash is serverless Redis that auto-scales with no configuration. Pricing is per-request, not per-connection. No scaling actions needed until extremely high volume.

Rate limiter keys consume minimal memory. At 10,000 active users, Redis usage is estimated at < 10MB active keys.

**Cloudflare R2 and CDN**

R2 has no capacity limits and scales automatically. No action needed at any user count.

Cloudflare's CDN handles storefront page delivery. All static storefront pages should be pre-rendered (Next.js `generateStaticParams` for known storefronts) for maximum CDN cache effectiveness. At scale, cache hit rate should be >90% for storefront pages.

**AI Providers**

| Stage | Action |
|---|---|
| 0–100 customers | Single OpenAI account, standard rate limits (10,000 TPM) |
| 100–500 customers | Apply for OpenAI usage tier increases; add Anthropic as fallback provider |
| 500–2,000 customers | Implement provider routing: OpenAI for speed-critical tasks, Anthropic for quality-critical tasks, Google Gemini for cost-sensitive batch tasks |
| 2,000+ customers | Negotiate enterprise API agreements with preferred providers; implement caching for identical/similar knowledge queries; fine-tune smaller models on common product types |

---

### Cost Projections

Cost modeling based on observed SaaS infrastructure benchmarks. All figures are monthly.

**At 100 Paying Customers ($99/month = ~$9,900 MRR)**

| Service | Cost/month | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel Pro | $20 | Team plan |
| Supabase Pro | $25 | 8GB database, PITR, 250GB bandwidth |
| Railway Starter | $30 | 2 worker containers, 1GB RAM each |
| Upstash Redis | $10 | ~2M requests/month |
| Cloudflare R2 | $5 | ~50GB storage, 5M ops |
| OpenAI / Anthropic | $200 | ~$2/customer/month AI inference (product generation, chatbot, blog) |
| Clerk | $25 | 1,000 MAUs |
| Stripe | $0 | % fees only; no monthly fee |
| Resend (email) | $20 | 50,000 emails/month |
| Sentry | $26 | Team plan |
| BetterUptime | $20 | Uptime monitoring + status page |
| GitHub | $0 | Free for small teams |
| **Total** | **~$381/month** | **4% of MRR** |

**At 1,000 Paying Customers (~$99,000 MRR)**

| Service | Cost/month | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel Pro | $150 | Higher bandwidth, function invocations |
| Supabase Pro/Team | $200 | 50GB database, read replica |
| Railway Pro | $200 | 8 worker containers |
| Upstash Redis | $50 | ~20M requests/month |
| Cloudflare R2 | $40 | ~500GB storage |
| OpenAI / Anthropic | $2,000 | $2/customer/month; Scout users add ~$5 more |
| Clerk | $100 | 10,000 MAUs |
| Resend | $89 | 500,000 emails/month |
| Sentry | $80 | Business plan |
| BetterUptime | $40 | Pro plan |
| **Total** | **~$2,949/month** | **3% of MRR** |

**At 10,000 Paying Customers (~$990,000 MRR)**

| Service | Cost/month | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel Enterprise | $1,500 | Custom SLA, DDoS, higher limits |
| Supabase Team + read replicas | $1,200 | Multiple read replicas, 200GB DB |
| Railway Pro (16 workers) | $800 | Auto-scaling worker fleet |
| Upstash Redis Pro | $300 | ~200M requests/month |
| Cloudflare R2 + Enterprise | $400 | ~5TB storage |
| OpenAI / Anthropic (enterprise) | $18,000 | Negotiated rate ~$1.80/customer |
| Clerk Enterprise | $700 | 100,000 MAUs, custom SLA |
| Resend / SendGrid | $500 | 5M emails/month |
| Sentry Business | $200 | |
| BetterUptime | $80 | |
| **Total** | **~$23,680/month** | **2.4% of MRR** |

**Gross margin improves with scale** as infrastructure costs grow sub-linearly relative to revenue. At 10,000 customers, infrastructure is ~2.4% of revenue. The primary variable cost is AI inference (OpenAI/Anthropic), which is driven by actual usage rather than seat count.

**AI cost sensitivity:** If average AI usage is higher than modeled (heavy chatbot traffic, frequent Scout scans), AI costs could be 2–3× the estimates above. The metered overage billing model in Know24's pricing allows cost recovery on high-usage customers.

**Scout add-on economics:** At $199/month, Scout customers generate significantly higher AI inference costs (web searching + summarization + response drafting for 20 comprehensive scans). Model these customers at $15–25/month in AI costs; Scout is profitable at $199 but less so than the base plan.

---

# Know24 Build Plan — Part 6: Build Milestones, Sprint Plan & AI Coding Instructions

> **Purpose of This Document**
> This is the operational execution guide. Every command, every file, every step. A non-technical founder using Claude Code + Cursor should be able to open this document on Day 1 and build a production-grade SaaS platform from zero. Nothing is assumed. Nothing is left vague.

---

## 6.1 Milestone Overview

The build is organized into 11 milestones across ~16 weeks. Each milestone ends with something **deployable, demonstrable, and testable**. No milestone is "invisible" work — every one produces a tangible output the founder can show.

### Milestone 0 — Foundation (Week 1)
**Goal:** A deployed, authenticated skeleton. Nothing works yet, but the entire infrastructure is wired.

| Task | Deliverable |
|------|-------------|
| Next.js 15 + TypeScript project created | Repo with compilable codebase |
| Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui configured | Design system tokens in place |
| Clerk auth integrated | Sign up / sign in / sign out working |
| Supabase project created + schema v1 | Users, businesses, products tables exist |
| Vercel project created + auto-deploy on main | Every push to main deploys |
| GitHub Actions CI pipeline | Lint + type check + test run on every PR |
| Environment variable management | `.env.example` with every required variable |
| `CLAUDE.md` + `.cursorrules` in repo root | AI coding context established |

**Definition of Done:** A URL exists (e.g. `https://know24-staging.vercel.app`) where a user can sign up, sign in, and land on an empty dashboard shell. No errors in console. CI is green.

---

### Milestone 1 — Landing & Marketing Pages (Week 1–2)
**Goal:** The public-facing marketing site is live and converts visitors to signups.

| Task | Deliverable |
|------|-------------|
| Landing page (hero, how it works, features, social proof) | `/` route |
| Pricing page with Stripe Checkout | `/pricing` route, real Stripe test checkout |
| Know24 Blog index + article pages | `/blog` and `/blog/[slug]` routes |
| Help Center index + article pages | `/help` and `/help/[slug]` routes |
| SEO metadata on all public pages | Open Graph, Twitter cards, canonical URLs |
| `sitemap.xml` + `robots.txt` | Search indexing ready |
| Cookie consent banner | GDPR/CCPA baseline |

**Definition of Done:** All pages render correctly on mobile and desktop. Stripe test checkout completes successfully and creates a Stripe Customer. Lighthouse score ≥ 90 on all public pages.

---

**Goal:** An authenticated creator can sign up and see their (empty) dashboard.

| Task | Deliverable |
|------|-------------|
| Dashboard layout: sidebar + header + content area | `/dashboard` route |
| `BusinessRow` component (empty state) | Renders placeholder for businesses |
| `BusinessRow` component (populated state) | Renders with real data when business exists |
| 5 department card stubs: Product, Store, Sales, Operations, Marketing | Cards render with skeleton loaders |
| Activity page with KPI cards | `/dashboard/activity` route |
| Settings page with 4 tabs | `/dashboard/settings` route |
| Theme toggle (light/dark) | Persisted to localStorage |
| Responsive sidebar (collapsible on mobile) | Works on all screen sizes |

**Definition of Done:** A new user can sign up via Clerk, land on the dashboard, see an empty state with "Create Your First Business" CTA, and navigate to Activity and Settings without errors.

| Step 1: Knowledge Intake (URL, file upload, video, AI interview) | Files upload to R2, URLs scraped and stored |
| Step 2: AI Analysis (knowledge processing + product recommendations) | Background job via Upstash + BullMQ |
| Step 3: Product type selection grid | 12 product type cards, multi-select |
| Step 4: AI product generation pipeline | Generates content, covers, pages per product |
| Step 5: Storefront generation + color palette selection | Storefront record created in DB |

| Supabase schema: knowledge_chunks, products, storefronts | Schema migration v2 |

**Definition of Done:** A test creator can complete all 5 steps and have a business record with at least 2 generated products and a storefront URL in the database. The AI pipeline runs without timeout. Error states handle gracefully.

**Goal:** The chatbot product is purchasable and functional on storefronts.

### Milestone 10 — Production Hardening (Week 14–16)
**Goal:** The platform is secure, fast, and ready for real users.

| Task | Deliverable |
|------|-------------|
| Security audit: RLS policies on all tables | Every Supabase table has row-level security |
| Security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame | `next.config.js` security headers |
| Input validation on all API routes (Zod) | No unvalidated inputs in production |
| Rate limiting on all API routes | Upstash Rate Limit on auth-sensitive routes |
| Error handling: every API route returns structured errors | Consistent `{error, code, message}` shape |
| Loading states on every page | Skeleton loaders, no layout shift |
| Empty states on every page | Helpful CTAs, no blank pages |
| Error boundaries on every route | Graceful fallback UI |
| Accessibility pass (axe-core) | Zero critical accessibility violations |
| Performance optimization | Lighthouse ≥ 90 on all pages |
| Legal pages: ToS, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy | `/legal/tos`, `/legal/privacy`, `/legal/cookies` |
| Sentry error tracking | Every unhandled error captured |
| Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights | Enabled on all routes |
| Uptime monitoring (Better Uptime or similar) | Alert within 2 minutes of downtime |
| Load testing (k6 or Artillery) | Platform handles 200 concurrent users |

**Definition of Done:** All Lighthouse scores ≥ 90. Zero critical Sentry errors in a 24-hour soak test. RLS audit passes. Legal pages published. Load test completes without errors.

## 6.3 Claude Code / Cursor Setup Instructions

### Initial Project Setup — Exact Commands

Run these commands in order. Copy-paste exactly.

```bash
# ============================================================
# STEP 1: Create Next.js project
# ============================================================
npx create-next-app@latest know24 \
  --typescript \
```
